Tool Result #33 Read toolu_01QuThzERtNGcpKgW8AQzzck 11:02:27
Read output=1→/** 2→ * GitHub Pages deployment: publish report output to a dedicated branch. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import { cpSync, existsSync, mkdtempSync, readdirSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from '...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * GitHub Pages deployment: publish report output to a dedicated branch.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { cpSync, existsSync, mkdtempSync, readdirSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     6→import { join } from 'node:path'
     7→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     8→import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process'
     9→
    10→import { getLogger } from '../../heartbeat/log.js'
    11→
    12→export interface GithubPagesOptions {
    13→  /** Repository root (contains .git/) */
    14→  repoRoot: string
    15→  /** Report output directory (e.g. Memory/07_reports) */
    16→  outputDir: string
    17→  /** Branch name to push to (e.g. 'gh-pages'). Empty string = skip. */
    18→  branch: string
    19→  /** Custom domain (CNAME). Empty string = skip. */
    20→  cname: string
    21→}
    22→
    23→const COMMIT_MESSAGE = 'chore: publish memorytree report'
    24→const COMMITTER_NAME = 'MemoryTree'
    25→const COMMITTER_EMAIL = 'memorytree@local.invalid'
    26→
    27→/** Safe character set for branch names. */
    28→const BRANCH_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9._/-]+$/
    29→
    30→export async function deployGithubPages(options: GithubPagesOptions): Promise<void> {
    31→  const { repoRoot, outputDir, branch, cname } = options
    32→  const logger = getLogger()
    33→
    34→  if (!branch) return
    35→
    36→  if (!BRANCH_RE.test(branch)) {
    37→    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Invalid branch name, skipping deploy: "${branch}"`)
    38→    return
    39→  }
    40→
    41→  if (!existsSync(outputDir)) {
    42→    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Output directory does not exist, skipping deploy: ${outputDir}`)
    43→    return
    44→  }
    45→
    46→  try {
    47→    if (cname) {
    48→      const cnamePath = join(outputDir, 'CNAME')
    49→      writeFileSync(cnamePath, cname + '\n', 'utf-8')
    50→      logger.info(`[gh-pages] CNAME written: ${cname}`)
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    const remoteUrl = git(repoRoot, 'remote', 'get-url', 'origin').trim()
    54→    if (!remoteUrl) {
    55→      logger.warn('[gh-pages] No origin remote configured, skipping deploy.')
    56→      return
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    const publishRoot = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'mt-gh-pages-'))
    60→    try {
    61→      git(publishRoot, 'init')
    62→      git(publishRoot, 'remote', 'add', 'origin', remoteUrl)
    63→
    64→      if (remoteBranchExists(publishRoot, branch)) {
    65→        logger.info(`[gh-pages] Updating existing origin/${branch} branch.`)
    66→        git(publishRoot, 'fetch', '--depth', '1', 'origin', branch)
    67→        git(publishRoot, 'checkout', '-B', branch, 'FETCH_HEAD')
    68→      } else {
    69→        logger.info(`[gh-pages] Branch '${branch}' not found, creating orphan publish branch.`)
    70→        git(publishRoot, 'checkout', '--orphan', branch)
    71→      }
    72→
    73→      clearDirectory(publishRoot)
    74→      copyDirectoryContents(outputDir, publishRoot)
    75→
    76→      git(publishRoot, 'add', '--all')
    77→      if (!hasPendingChanges(publishRoot)) {
    78→        logger.info(`[gh-pages] No changes to publish for origin/${branch}.`)
    79→        return
    80→      }
    81→
    82→      git(
    83→        publishRoot,
    84→        '-c', `user.name=${COMMITTER_NAME}`,
    85→        '-c', `user.email=${COMMITTER_EMAIL}`,
    86→        'commit',
    87→        '-m',
    88→        COMMIT_MESSAGE,
    89→      )
    90→      git(publishRoot, 'push', 'origin', `HEAD:${branch}`)
    91→      logger.info(`[gh-pages] Successfully pushed report to origin/${branch}`)
    92→    } finally {
    93→      rmSync(publishRoot, { recursive: true, force: true })
    94→    }
    95→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    96→    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Deploy failed: ${String(err)}`)
    97→    // Never throw; report deploy failure must not abort heartbeat.
    98→  }
    99→}
   100→
   101→function remoteBranchExists(cwd: string, branch: string): boolean {
   102→  try {
   103→    git(cwd, 'ls-remote', '--exit-code', '--heads', 'origin', branch)
   104→    return true
   105→  } catch {
   106→    return false
   107→  }
   108→}
   109→
   110→function clearDirectory(root: string): void {
   111→  for (const entry of readdirSync(root)) {
   112→    if (entry === '.git') continue
   113→    rmSync(join(root, entry), { recursive: true, force: true })
   114→  }
   115→}
   116→
   117→function copyDirectoryContents(source: string, destination: string): void {
   118→  for (const entry of readdirSync(source)) {
   119→    cpSync(join(source, entry), join(destination, entry), { recursive: true, force: true })
   120→  }
   121→}
   122→
   123→function hasPendingChanges(cwd: string): boolean {
   124→  return git(cwd, 'status', '--short').trim().length > 0
   125→}
   126→
   127→/** Invoke git with explicit args; no shell interpolation. */
   128→function git(cwd: string, ...args: string[]): string {
   129→  return execFileSync('git', args, { cwd, stdio: 'pipe' }).toString()
   130→}
   131→
Tool Result #34 Read toolu_019QA7vt1LeWejn6kQGDnJVm 11:02:27
Read output=1→/** 2→ * Webhook notification: sends a POST to configurable endpoints. 3→ * Supports Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and generic webhooks. 4→ * Never throws — failures are l...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Webhook notification: sends a POST to configurable endpoints.
     3→ * Supports Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and generic webhooks.
     4→ * Never throws — failures are logged as warnings.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { getLogger } from '../../heartbeat/log.js'
     8→
     9→export interface WebhookOptions {
    10→  url: string
    11→  sessionCount: number
    12→  newSessionIds?: string[]
    13→  reportUrl?: string
    14→}
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// Platform detection
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→type Platform = 'feishu' | 'telegram' | 'discord' | 'slack' | 'generic'
    21→
    22→/**
    23→ * Detect platform from a parsed URL.
    24→ * Uses exact hostname matching to prevent subdomain-spoofing attacks like
    25→ * https://my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com being mistaken for Feishu.
    26→ */
    27→function detectPlatform(parsedUrl: URL): Platform {
    28→  const h = parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase()
    29→  if (h === 'open.feishu.cn' || h === 'open.larksuite.com') return 'feishu'
    30→  if (h === 'api.telegram.org') return 'telegram'
    31→  if (h === 'discord.com' || h === 'discordapp.com') return 'discord'
    32→  if (h === 'hooks.slack.com') return 'slack'
    33→  return 'generic'
    34→}
    35→
    36→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// Payload builders
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→function buildFeishuPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    41→  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options
    42→  const newCount = newSessionIds.length
    43→  const actionBtn = reportUrl
    44→    ? [{
    45→        tag: 'button',
    46→        text: { content: 'View Report', tag: 'plain_text' },
    47→        url: reportUrl,
    48→        type: 'primary',
    49→      }]
    50→    : []
    51→
    52→  return {
    53→    msg_type: 'interactive',
    54→    card: {
    55→      header: {
    56→        title: { content: 'MemoryTree Report Updated', tag: 'plain_text' },
    57→        template: 'blue',
    58→      },
    59→      elements: [
    60→        {
    61→          tag: 'div',
    62→          text: {
    63→            tag: 'lark_md',
    64→            content: `**Sessions:** ${sessionCount} total${newCount ? `, ${newCount} new` : ''}`,
    65→          },
    66→        },
    67→        ...(actionBtn.length > 0 ? [{ tag: 'action', actions: actionBtn }] : []),
    68→      ],
    69→    },
    70→  }
    71→}
    72→
    73→function buildTelegramPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    74→  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options
    75→  const newCount = newSessionIds.length
    76→  let text = `📊 *MemoryTree Report Updated*\n\nSessions: ${sessionCount} total`
    77→  if (newCount > 0) text += `, ${newCount} new`
    78→  if (reportUrl) text += `\n[View Report](${reportUrl})`
    79→  return { text, parse_mode: 'Markdown' }
    80→}
    81→
    82→function buildDiscordPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    83→  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options
    84→  const newCount = newSessionIds.length
    85→  let content = `📊 **MemoryTree Report Updated** — ${sessionCount} sessions total`
    86→  if (newCount > 0) content += `, ${newCount} new`
    87→  if (reportUrl) content += ` · [View Report](${reportUrl})`
    88→  return { content }
    89→}
    90→
    91→function buildSlackPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
    92→  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options
    93→  const newCount = newSessionIds.length
    94→  let text = `📊 MemoryTree Report Updated — ${sessionCount} sessions total`
    95→  if (newCount > 0) text += `, ${newCount} new`
    96→  if (reportUrl) text += ` · <${reportUrl}|View Report>`
    97→  return { text }
    98→}
    99→
   100→function buildGenericPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
   101→  return {
   102→    event: 'memorytree.report.updated',
   103→    session_count: options.sessionCount,
   104→    new_session_count: options.newSessionIds?.length ?? 0,
   105→    new_session_ids: options.newSessionIds ?? [],
   106→    report_url: options.reportUrl ?? '',
   107→  }
   108→}
   109→
   110→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   111→// URL safety validation
   112→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   113→
   114→/** Private / loopback IPv4 ranges. */
   115→const PRIVATE_IPV4_RE =
   116→  /^(localhost|127\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+|0\.0\.0\.0|10\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+|172\.(1[6-9]|2\d|3[01])\.\d+\.\d+|192\.168\.\d+\.\d+|169\.254\.\d+\.\d+)$/i
   117→
   118→/**
   119→ * Returns true for all private / loopback IPv6 addresses.
   120→ * Node.js URL.hostname may return IPv6 with or without surrounding brackets,
   121→ * so we strip them before checking.
   122→ *   ::1           loopback
   123→ *   fe80::/10     link-local (fe80 – febf)
   124→ *   fc00::/7      ULA (fc and fd)
   125→ *   ::ffff:0:0/96 IPv4-mapped
   126→ */
   127→function isPrivateIPv6(hostname: string): boolean {
   128→  // Strip optional surrounding brackets added by some URL implementations
   129→  const h = hostname.toLowerCase().replace(/^\[|]$/g, '')
   130→  if (h === '::1') return true
   131→  if (/^fe[89ab]/i.test(h)) return true       // fe80::/10 link-local
   132→  if (h.startsWith('fc') || h.startsWith('fd')) return true  // fc00::/7 ULA
   133→  if (h.startsWith('::ffff:')) return true    // IPv4-mapped
   134→  return false
   135→}
   136→
   137→function validateWebhookUrl(raw: string): URL {
   138→  let url: URL
   139→  try {
   140→    url = new URL(raw)
   141→  } catch {
   142→    throw new Error(`Invalid webhook URL: ${raw}`)
   143→  }
   144→  if (url.protocol !== 'https:') {
   145→    throw new Error(`Webhook URL must use https: (got ${url.protocol})`)
   146→  }
   147→  if (PRIVATE_IPV4_RE.test(url.hostname) || isPrivateIPv6(url.hostname)) {
   148→    throw new Error(`Webhook URL hostname is private/loopback: ${url.hostname}`)
   149→  }
   150→  return url
   151→}
   152→
   153→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   154→// Main send function
   155→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   156→
   157→export async function sendWebhook(options: WebhookOptions): Promise<void> {
   158→  if (!options.url) return
   159→  const logger = getLogger()
   160→
   161→  let parsedUrl: URL
   162→  try {
   163→    parsedUrl = validateWebhookUrl(options.url)
   164→  } catch (err) {
   165→    logger.warn(`[webhook] Rejected URL: ${String(err)}`)
   166→    return
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  const platform = detectPlatform(parsedUrl)
   170→
   171→  const payloadMap: Record<Platform, (o: WebhookOptions) => Record<string, unknown>> = {
   172→    feishu: buildFeishuPayload,
   173→    telegram: buildTelegramPayload,
   174→    discord: buildDiscordPayload,
   175→    slack: buildSlackPayload,
   176→    generic: buildGenericPayload,
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  const payload = payloadMap[platform](options)
   180→
   181→  try {
   182→    const { default: https } = await import('node:https')
   183→    const body = JSON.stringify(payload)
   184→
   185→    await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
   186→      const url = parsedUrl
   187→      const req = https.request(
   188→        {
   189→          hostname: url.hostname,
   190→          port: url.port || 443,
   191→          path: url.pathname + url.search,
   192→          method: 'POST',
   193→          headers: {
   194→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
   195→            'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(body),
   196→          },
   197→        },
   198→        res => {
   199→          // Consume response to avoid socket leak
   200→          res.resume()
   201→          if (res.statusCode && res.statusCode >= 400) {
   202→            reject(new Error(`HTTP ${res.statusCode}`))
   203→          } else {
   204→            resolve()
   205→          }
   206→        },
   207→      )
   208→      req.on('error', reject)
   209→      req.setTimeout(5000, () => { req.destroy(); reject(new Error('timeout')) })
   210→      req.write(body)
   211→      req.end()
   212→    })
   213→
   214→    logger.info(`[webhook] Sent ${platform} notification`)
   215→  } catch (err: unknown) {
   216→    logger.warn(`[webhook] Failed to send notification: ${String(err)}`)
   217→    // Never throw
   218→  }
   219→}
   220→

1→/**
     2→ * i18n system: build-time locale loading.
     3→ * Supported: 'en' (default), 'zh-CN'. Unknown locales fall back to 'en'.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→export type { Translations } from './types.js'
     7→import type { Translations } from './types.js'
     8→import { en } from './en.js'
     9→import { zhCN } from './zh-CN.js'
    10→
    11→const LOCALES: Record<string, Translations> = {
    12→  'en': en,
    13→  'zh-CN': zhCN,
    14→}
    15→
    16→export function loadLocale(locale: string): Translations {
    17→  return LOCALES[locale] ?? en
    18→}
    19→

Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * HTML layout helpers: shell, navigation, and HTML escaping.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { basename } from 'node:path'
     6→
     7→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'
     8→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js'
     9→import { REPORT_CSS } from './css.js'
    10→
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→// HTML escaping
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→
    15→export function escHtml(text: string): string {
    16→  return text
    17→    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')
    18→    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
    19→    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')
    20→    .replace(/"/g, '&quot;')
    21→    .replace(/'/g, '&#39;')
    22→}
    23→
    24→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25→// Navigation page IDs
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→
    28→export type NavPage =
    29→  | 'dashboard'
    30→  | 'transcripts'
    31→  | 'projects'
    32→  | 'graph'
    33→  | 'goals'
    34→  | 'todos'
    35→  | 'knowledge'
    36→  | 'archive'
    37→  | 'search'
    38→
    39→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40→// Sidebar navigation
    41→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    42→
    43→const SIDEBAR_INLINE_JS = `
    44→<script>
    45→(function() {
    46→  // Theme
    47→  var saved = localStorage.getItem('mt-theme') || 'dark';
    48→  document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', saved);
    49→  // Sidebar collapse
    50→  if (localStorage.getItem('mt-sidebar') === 'collapsed') document.body.classList.add('sidebar-collapsed');
    51→  function updateThemeBtn() {
    52→    var btn = document.getElementById('theme-toggle');
    53→    if (btn) btn.textContent = document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') === 'dark' ? '☀ Light' : '☾ Dark';
    54→  }
    55→  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
    56→    updateThemeBtn();
    57→    var btn = document.getElementById('theme-toggle');
    58→    if (btn) btn.addEventListener('click', function() {
    59→      var next = document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark';
    60→      document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', next);
    61→      localStorage.setItem('mt-theme', next);
    62→      updateThemeBtn();
    63→    });
    64→    // Mobile hamburger
    65→    var hb = document.getElementById('hamburger');
    66→    var sb = document.getElementById('sidebar');
    67→    var ov = document.getElementById('sidebar-overlay');
    68→    function closeSidebar() {
    69→      if (sb) sb.classList.remove('open');
    70→      if (ov) ov.classList.remove('open');
    71→    }
    72→    if (hb) hb.addEventListener('click', function() {
    73→      if (sb) sb.classList.toggle('open');
    74→      if (ov) ov.classList.toggle('open');
    75→    });
    76→    if (ov) ov.addEventListener('click', closeSidebar);
    77→    // Sidebar collapse toggle
    78→    var collapseBtn = document.getElementById('sidebar-collapse');
    79→    if (collapseBtn) collapseBtn.addEventListener('click', function() {
    80→      var collapsed = document.body.classList.toggle('sidebar-collapsed');
    81→      localStorage.setItem('mt-sidebar', collapsed ? 'collapsed' : 'expanded');
    82→    });
    83→    // TOC toggle
    84→    var tocToggle = document.getElementById('toc-toggle');
    85→    var tocList = document.getElementById('toc-list');
    86→    if (tocToggle && tocList) tocToggle.addEventListener('click', function() {
    87→      var expanded = tocToggle.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true';
    88→      tocToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', expanded ? 'false' : 'true');
    89→      if (expanded) { tocList.setAttribute('hidden', ''); } else { tocList.removeAttribute('hidden'); }
    90→    });
    91→    // Reader Mode toggle
    92→    var readerBtn = document.getElementById('reader-toggle');
    93→    if (readerBtn) {
    94→      if (localStorage.getItem('mt-reader') === '1') document.body.classList.add('reader-mode');
    95→      readerBtn.addEventListener('click', function() {
    96→        var on = document.body.classList.toggle('reader-mode');
    97→        localStorage.setItem('mt-reader', on ? '1' : '0');
    98→      });
    99→    }
   100→    // View Transitions API (progressive enhancement)
   101→    if (document.startViewTransition) {
   102→      document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
   103→        var a = e.target && (e.target.closest ? e.target.closest('a[href]:not([target]):not([download])') : null);
   104→        if (!a) return;
   105→        var href = a.getAttribute('href');
   106→        if (!href || href.startsWith('#') || href.startsWith('http')) return;
   107→        e.preventDefault();
   108→        document.startViewTransition(function() { window.location.href = href; });
   109→      });
   110→    }
   111→    // Popover preview
   112→    var popover = null;
   113→    document.querySelectorAll('a[data-summary]').forEach(function(el) {
   114→      el.addEventListener('mouseenter', function(e) {
   115→        var summary = el.getAttribute('data-summary') || '';
   116→        var meta = el.getAttribute('data-meta') || '';
   117→        if (!summary && !meta) return;
   118→        if (!popover) {
   119→          popover = document.createElement('div');
   120→          popover.className = 'popover';
   121→          document.body.appendChild(popover);
   122→        }
   123→        popover.innerHTML = '';
   124→        if (meta) {
   125→          var metaDiv = document.createElement('div');
   126→          metaDiv.className = 'popover-meta';
   127→          metaDiv.textContent = meta;
   128→          popover.appendChild(metaDiv);
   129→        }
   130→        if (summary) {
   131→          var sumDiv = document.createElement('div');
   132→          sumDiv.className = 'popover-summary';
   133→          sumDiv.textContent = summary;
   134→          popover.appendChild(sumDiv);
   135→        }
   136→        popover.style.display = 'block';
   137→        positionPopover(e);
   138→      });
   139→      el.addEventListener('mousemove', positionPopover);
   140→      el.addEventListener('mouseleave', function() {
   141→        if (popover) popover.style.display = 'none';
   142→      });
   143→    });
   144→    function positionPopover(e) {
   145→      if (!popover) return;
   146→      var x = e.clientX + 12;
   147→      var y = e.clientY + 12;
   148→      var pw = popover.offsetWidth || 320;
   149→      var ph = popover.offsetHeight || 80;
   150→      if (x + pw > window.innerWidth - 8) x = e.clientX - pw - 12;
   151→      if (y + ph > window.innerHeight - 8) y = e.clientY - ph - 12;
   152→      popover.style.left = x + 'px';
   153→      popover.style.top = y + 'px';
   154→    }
   155→  });
   156→})();
   157→</script>`
   158→
   159→interface NavItem {
   160→  id: NavPage
   161→  icon: string
   162→  labelKey: keyof Translations['nav']
   163→  href: string
   164→}
   165→
   166→function buildNavItems(depth: 0 | 1 | 2): NavItem[] {
   167→  const p = '../'.repeat(depth)
   168→  return [
   169→    { id: 'dashboard', icon: '📊', labelKey: 'dashboard', href: `${p}index.html` },
   170→    { id: 'transcripts', icon: '💬', labelKey: 'sessions', href: `${p}transcripts/index.html` },
   171→    { id: 'projects', icon: '📁', labelKey: 'projects', href: `${p}projects/index.html` },
   172→    { id: 'graph', icon: '🕸️', labelKey: 'graph', href: `${p}graph.html` },
   173→    { id: 'goals', icon: '🎯', labelKey: 'goals', href: `${p}goals/index.html` },
   174→    { id: 'todos', icon: '✅', labelKey: 'todos', href: `${p}todos/index.html` },
   175→    { id: 'knowledge', icon: '📚', labelKey: 'knowledge', href: `${p}knowledge/index.html` },
   176→    { id: 'archive', icon: '🗄️', labelKey: 'archive', href: `${p}archive/index.html` },
   177→    { id: 'search', icon: '🔍', labelKey: 'search', href: `${p}search.html` },
   178→  ]
   179→}
   180→
   181→export function renderNav(current: NavPage, depth: 0 | 1 | 2, t?: Translations): string {
   182→  const items = buildNavItems(depth)
   183→
   184→  // Labels for each nav item
   185→  const label = (item: NavItem): string => {
   186→    if (t) return t.nav[item.labelKey]
   187→    // Fallback English labels
   188→    const defaults: Record<string, string> = {
   189→      dashboard: 'Dashboard', sessions: 'Sessions', projects: 'Projects',
   190→      graph: 'Graph', goals: 'Goals', todos: 'Todos',
   191→      knowledge: 'Knowledge', archive: 'Archive', search: 'Search',
   192→    }
   193→    return defaults[item.labelKey] ?? item.labelKey
   194→  }
   195→
   196→  const themeLabel = '☀ Light'
   197→
   198→  const navLinks = items.map(item => {
   199→    const cls = item.id === current ? 'nav-link active' : 'nav-link'
   200→    return `<a href="${escHtml(item.href)}" class="${cls}" title="${escHtml(label(item))}">${escHtml(item.icon)} <span class="nav-label">${escHtml(label(item))}</span></a>`
   201→  }).join('\n      ')
   202→
   203→  return `<aside class="sidebar" id="sidebar">
   204→  <div class="sidebar-header">
   205→    <span class="sidebar-brand">MemoryTree</span>
   206→    <button class="sidebar-collapse-btn" id="sidebar-collapse" type="button" title="Collapse sidebar">«</button>
   207→  </div>
   208→  <nav class="sidebar-nav">
   209→    ${navLinks}
   210→  </nav>
   211→  <div class="sidebar-footer">
   212→    <button class="theme-toggle-btn" id="theme-toggle" type="button">${escHtml(themeLabel)}</button>
   213→  </div>
   214→</aside>
   215→<div class="sidebar-overlay" id="sidebar-overlay"></div>`
   216→}
   217→
   218→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   219→// HTML shell
   220→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   221→
   222→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   223→// Transcript URL helpers (single canonical implementation)
   224→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   225→
   226→/**
   227→ * Compute the stem (filename without .md) for a manifest's clean path.
   228→ * Falls back to session_id.
   229→ */
   230→export function manifestStem(m: ManifestEntry): string {
   231→  const cleanPath = m.repo_clean_path || m.global_clean_path || ''
   232→  return cleanPath ? basename(cleanPath, '.md') : m.session_id
   233→}
   234→
   235→/**
   236→ * URL to a transcript page relative to the **root** of the report output dir.
   237→ * Use this from index.html and transcripts/index.html.
   238→ */
   239→export function transcriptUrlFromRoot(m: ManifestEntry): string {
   240→  return `transcripts/${m.client}/${manifestStem(m)}.html`
   241→}
   242→
   243→/**
   244→ * URL to a transcript page relative to **another transcript** page.
   245→ * Use this from individual transcript pages.
   246→ */
   247→export function transcriptUrlFromTranscript(m: ManifestEntry): string {
   248→  return `../${m.client}/${manifestStem(m)}.html`
   249→}
   250→
   251→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   252→// Client badge (allowlisted CSS class)
   253→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   254→
   255→const KNOWN_CLIENTS = new Set(['codex', 'claude', 'gemini'])
   256→
   257→/** Render a coloured client badge. CSS class is allowlisted to prevent injection. */
   258→export function clientBadge(client: string): string {
   259→  const safeClass = KNOWN_CLIENTS.has(client) ? client : 'unknown'
   260→  return `<span class="badge badge-${safeClass}">${escHtml(client)}</span>`
   261→}
   262→
   263→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   264→// Shared MarkdownFile type and slug helper
   265→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   266→
   267→export interface MarkdownFile {
   268→  filename: string
   269→  title: string
   270→  content: string
   271→}
   272→
   273→export function slugifyName(name: string): string {
   274→  return name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
   275→}
   276→
   277→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   278→// HTML shell
   279→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   280→
   281→export interface HtmlShellOptions {
   282→  extraHead?: string
   283→  lang?: string
   284→  breadcrumb?: string
   285→  /** Inject reader-mode toggle button into the page */
   286→  readerMode?: boolean
   287→  /** Open Graph description (og:description). Truncated to 200 chars. */
   288→  ogDescription?: string
   289→  /** Absolute URL for og:url. Omitted when empty. */
   290→  ogUrl?: string
   291→}
   292→
   293→export function htmlShell(
   294→  title: string,
   295→  content: string,
   296→  nav: string,
   297→  extraHeadOrOpts: string | HtmlShellOptions = '',
   298→  lang = 'en',
   299→): string {
   300→  // Accept legacy string arg (extraHead) or new options object
   301→  const opts: HtmlShellOptions = typeof extraHeadOrOpts === 'string'
   302→    ? { extraHead: extraHeadOrOpts, lang }
   303→    : extraHeadOrOpts
   304→  const extraHead = opts.extraHead ?? ''
   305→  const resolvedLang = opts.lang ?? lang
   306→  const breadcrumb = opts.breadcrumb ?? ''
   307→  const readerBtn = opts.readerMode !== false
   308→    ? `<button class="reader-toggle-btn" id="reader-toggle" type="button" title="Toggle reader mode">📖</button>`
   309→    : ''
   310→
   311→  // Open Graph meta tags
   312→  const ogDesc = opts.ogDescription ? opts.ogDescription.slice(0, 200) : ''
   313→  const ogMeta = [
   314→    `<meta property="og:title" content="${escHtml(title)}">`,
   315→    `<meta property="og:type" content="website">`,
   316→    ogDesc ? `<meta property="og:description" content="${escHtml(ogDesc)}">` : '',
   317→    opts.ogUrl ? `<meta property="og:url" content="${escHtml(opts.ogUrl)}">` : '',
   318→  ].filter(Boolean).join('\n')
   319→
   320→  const topbar = `<header class="topbar" id="topbar">
   321→  <span class="topbar-brand">MemoryTree</span>
   322→  <div class="topbar-actions">
   323→    <button class="theme-toggle-btn" id="theme-toggle-mobile" type="button">☀</button>
   324→    <button class="hamburger" id="hamburger" type="button">☰</button>
   325→  </div>
   326→</header>`
   327→
   328→  return `<!DOCTYPE html>
   329→<html lang="${escHtml(resolvedLang)}" data-theme="dark">
   330→<head>
   331→<meta charset="UTF-8">
   332→<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
   333→<title>${escHtml(title)} — MemoryTree</title>
   334→${ogMeta}
   335→<style>${REPORT_CSS}</style>
   336→${extraHead}
   337→${SIDEBAR_INLINE_JS}
   338→</head>
   339→<body>
   340→${topbar}
   341→${nav}
   342→<div class="main-content">
   343→  <div class="container">
   344→${breadcrumb ? `${breadcrumb}\n` : ''}<div style="display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;margin-bottom:0.5rem">${readerBtn}</div>
   345→${content}
   346→  </div>
   347→</div>
   348→</body>
   349→</html>`
   350→}
   351→

}

Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/log.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Minimal logger for @openmnemo/report (no external dependencies).\n */\n\nexport interface Logger {\n  debug(msg: string): void\n  info(msg: string): void\n  warn(msg: string): void\n  error(msg: string): void\n}\n\nexport function getLogger(): Logger {\n  const prefix = (): string => new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 19).replace('T', ' ')\n  return {\n    debug: (m) => console.debug(`${prefix()} [DEBUG] ${m}`),\n    info:  (m) => console.info(`${prefix()} [INFO]  ${m}`),\n    warn:  (m) => console.warn(`${prefix()} [WARN]  ${m}`),\n    error: (m) => console.error(`${prefix()} [ERROR] ${m}`),\n  }\n}\n"
}

  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/types.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Type definitions for the report generation subsystem.\n */\n\nexport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\n\nexport interface ReportStats {\n  totalSessions: number\n  totalMessages: number\n  totalToolEvents: number\n  activeDays: number\n  dateRange: { from: string; to: string }\n  clientCounts: Record<string, number>\n  /** 'YYYY-MM-DD' → session count */\n  dayBuckets: Record<string, number>\n  /** 'YYYY-WNN' → message count */\n  weekBuckets: Record<string, number>\n  /** tool name → invocation count */\n  toolCounts: Record<string, number>\n}\n\nexport interface SummaryCache {\n  sha256: string\n  summary: string\n  generated_at: string\n}\n\nexport interface LinkGraph {\n  /** session_id → list of session_ids that reference it */\n  backlinks: Record<string, string[]>\n  /** session_id → list of session_ids it references */\n  forwardLinks: Record<string, string[]>\n}\n\nexport interface SearchIndexEntry {\n  url: string\n  title: string\n  client: string\n  /** Last path segment of ManifestEntry.cwd (e.g. 'openmnemo') */\n  project: string\n  date: string\n  snippet: string\n}\n\nexport interface BuildReportOptions {\n  root: string\n  output: string\n  noAi?: boolean\n  model?: string\n  /** Build-time locale (e.g. 'en', 'zh-CN'). Default: 'en'. */\n  locale?: string\n  /** gh-pages branch name. Empty string = skip deploy. Default: ''. */\n  ghPagesBranch?: string\n  /** Custom domain for CNAME file. Empty string = skip. Default: ''. */\n  cname?: string\n  /** Webhook URL for post-build notifications. Empty string = skip. Default: ''. */\n  webhookUrl?: string\n  /** Session IDs newly imported in this heartbeat cycle (for webhook). */\n  newSessionIds?: string[]\n  /** Absolute base URL for RSS/OG links (e.g. 'https://memory.example.com'). Empty = skip. */\n  reportBaseUrl?: string\n}\n"
}

Tool Call #100 Write toolu_01SxopoRRfcrLTSJHLmWBLPD 11:06:10
Write input={"content":"/**\n * Statistics computation from ManifestEntry arrays.\n * All purely algorithmic — no I/O, no external deps.\n */\n\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmne...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/stats.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Statistics computation from ManifestEntry arrays.\n * All purely algorithmic — no I/O, no external deps.\n */\n\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\nimport type { ReportStats } from './types.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Main stats computation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function computeStats(\n  manifests: ManifestEntry[],\n  toolCounts: Record<string, number> = {},\n): ReportStats {\n  const totalSessions = manifests.length\n  let totalMessages = 0\n  let totalToolEvents = 0\n  const clientCounts: Record<string, number> = {}\n  const daySet = new Set<string>()\n\n  for (const m of manifests) {\n    totalMessages += m.message_count\n    totalToolEvents += m.tool_event_count\n\n    const client = m.client || 'unknown'\n    clientCounts[client] = (clientCounts[client] ?? 0) + 1\n\n    const day = isoDay(m.started_at)\n    if (day) daySet.add(day)\n  }\n\n  const dayBuckets = buildDayBuckets(manifests)\n  const weekBuckets = buildWeekBuckets(manifests)\n\n  const dates = manifests\n    .map(m => m.started_at)\n    .filter(Boolean)\n    .sort()\n\n  const from = dates[0] ?? ''\n  const to = dates[dates.length - 1] ?? ''\n\n  return {\n    totalSessions,\n    totalMessages,\n    totalToolEvents,\n    activeDays: daySet.size,\n    dateRange: { from, to },\n    clientCounts,\n    dayBuckets,\n    weekBuckets,\n    toolCounts,\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Day buckets: 'YYYY-MM-DD' → session count\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function buildDayBuckets(manifests: ManifestEntry[]): Record<string, number> {\n  const buckets: Record<string, number> = {}\n  for (const m of manifests) {\n    const day = isoDay(m.started_at)\n    if (day) {\n      buckets[day] = (buckets[day] ?? 0) + 1\n    }\n  }\n  return buckets\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Week buckets: 'YYYY-WNN' → message count\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function buildWeekBuckets(manifests: ManifestEntry[]): Record<string, number> {\n  const buckets: Record<string, number> = {}\n  for (const m of manifests) {\n    const week = isoWeek(m.started_at)\n    if (week) {\n      buckets[week] = (buckets[week] ?? 0) + m.message_count\n    }\n  }\n  return buckets\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tool name extraction\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Extract tool names from tool event summary strings.\n * Summary format is typically: \"function_call input=...\" or \"Bash ...\" etc.\n */\nexport function extractToolNames(summaries: string[]): string[] {\n  return summaries.map(s => {\n    const trimmed = s.trim()\n    // Try to extract first identifier word\n    const match = trimmed.match(/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_:-]*)/)\n    if (match?.[1]) {\n      return match[1]\n    }\n    return 'unknown'\n  })\n}\n\n/**\n * Accumulate tool name counts into an existing map.\n */\nexport function accumulateToolCounts(\n  toolCounts: Record<string, number>,\n  names: string[],\n): Record<string, number> {\n  const result = { ...toolCounts }\n  for (const name of names) {\n    result[name] = (result[name] ?? 0) + 1\n  }\n  return result\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Internal helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Extract 'YYYY-MM-DD' from an ISO timestamp. Returns '' if invalid. */\nfunction isoDay(ts: string): string {\n  if (!ts) return ''\n  const match = ts.match(/^(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})/)\n  return match?.[1] ?? ''\n}\n\n/** Compute 'YYYY-WNN' ISO week string from an ISO timestamp. */\nfunction isoWeek(ts: string): string {\n  if (!ts) return ''\n  try {\n    const d = new Date(ts)\n    if (isNaN(d.getTime())) return ''\n    return isoWeekKey(d)\n  } catch {\n    return ''\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Compute 'YYYY-WNN' ISO week key from a Date object.\n * Exported so chart renderers can reuse without duplicating the formula.\n */\nexport function isoWeekKey(d: Date): string {\n  // ISO week: the week containing Thursday; Monday = week start\n  const thursday = new Date(d)\n  thursday.setDate(d.getDate() + (4 - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7 + 1)))\n  const year = thursday.getFullYear()\n  const jan4 = new Date(year, 0, 4)\n  const week = Math.ceil(\n    ((thursday.getTime() - jan4.getTime()) / 86400000 + jan4.getDay() + 1) / 7,\n  )\n  return `${year}-W${String(week).padStart(2, '0')}`\n}\n"
}

Write input={"content":"/**\n * AI summary generation with SHA256-based caching.\n * Falls back gracefully when API key is absent or SDK is missing.\n */\n\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, ...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/summarize.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * AI summary generation with SHA256-based caching.\n * Falls back gracefully when API key is absent or SDK is missing.\n */\n\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\n\nimport type { SummaryCache } from './types.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Types\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface SummaryMessage {\n  role: string\n  text: string\n}\n\nexport interface SummaryOptions {\n  cacheDir: string\n  noAi: boolean\n  model: string\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Semaphore (max 5 concurrent AI calls)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nlet activeCalls = 0\nconst MAX_CONCURRENT = 5\n\nasync function withSemaphore<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {\n  while (activeCalls >= MAX_CONCURRENT) {\n    await new Promise<void>(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100))\n  }\n  activeCalls++\n  try {\n    return await fn()\n  } finally {\n    activeCalls--\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Main entry point\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport async function getSummary(\n  sha256: string,\n  messages: SummaryMessage[],\n  options: SummaryOptions,\n): Promise<string> {\n  // Check cache\n  const cached = readCache(sha256, options.cacheDir)\n  if (cached !== null) {\n    return cached\n  }\n\n  if (options.noAi) return ''\n\n  const apiKey = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n  if (!apiKey) return ''\n\n  return withSemaphore(() => callApi(sha256, messages, options))\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// API call\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync function callApi(\n  sha256: string,\n  messages: SummaryMessage[],\n  options: SummaryOptions,\n): Promise<string> {\n  try {\n    // Dynamic import so the build never fails if @anthropic-ai/sdk is absent\n    const { default: Anthropic } = await (import('@anthropic-ai/sdk') as Promise<{ default: typeof import('@anthropic-ai/sdk').default }>)\n\n    const client = new Anthropic()\n    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(messages)\n\n    const response = await client.messages.create({\n      model: options.model,\n      max_tokens: 200,\n      system:\n        'Summarize in 2-3 sentences. What was accomplished? What problems were solved? Be specific. Use past tense.',\n      messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }],\n    })\n\n    const block = response.content[0]\n    const text = block && block.type === 'text' ? block.text : ''\n    if (text) {\n      writeCache(sha256, text, options.cacheDir)\n    }\n    return text\n  } catch (err: unknown) {\n    // MODULE_NOT_FOUND or any API error — never crash the build\n    const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code\n    if (code !== 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND') {\n      console.warn(`[summarize] API call failed: ${String(err)}`)\n    }\n    return ''\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Prompt construction\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** First 30 messages, each truncated to 500 chars. */\nexport function buildSummaryPrompt(messages: SummaryMessage[]): string {\n  const slice = messages.slice(0, 30)\n  const lines = slice.map(m => {\n    const text = m.text.slice(0, 500)\n    return `${m.role.toUpperCase()}: ${text}`\n  })\n  return lines.join('\\n\\n')\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Cache read / write\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function readCache(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string | null {\n  const path = cachePath(sha256, cacheDir)\n  if (!existsSync(path)) return null\n  try {\n    const raw = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')\n    const entry = JSON.parse(raw) as SummaryCache\n    return entry.summary ?? null\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n}\n\nexport function writeCache(sha256: string, summary: string, cacheDir: string): void {\n  try {\n    mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true })\n    const entry: SummaryCache = {\n      sha256,\n      summary,\n      generated_at: new Date().toISOString(),\n    }\n    writeFileSync(cachePath(sha256, cacheDir), JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2) + '\\n', 'utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    // Never crash on cache write failure\n  }\n}\n\nfunction cachePath(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string {\n  return join(cacheDir, `${sha256}.json`)\n}\n"
}

Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/tags.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * AI tag extraction with SHA256-based caching.\n * Extracts 3–5 keyword tags from a session summary using Claude.\n * Falls back to [] when noAi=true, API key absent, or any error occurs.\n */\n\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { escHtml } from './render/layout.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Types\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface TagOptions {\n  cacheDir: string\n  noAi: boolean\n  model: string\n}\n\ninterface TagCache {\n  sha256: string\n  tags: string[]\n  generated_at: string\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Main entry point\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport async function getTags(\n  sha256: string,\n  summary: string,\n  options: TagOptions,\n): Promise<string[]> {\n  // noAi mode: skip entirely\n  if (options.noAi) return []\n\n  // No summary → nothing to tag\n  if (!summary.trim()) return []\n\n  // Check cache first\n  const cached = readTagCache(sha256, options.cacheDir)\n  if (cached !== null) return cached\n\n  const apiKey = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n  if (!apiKey) return []\n\n  return callTagApi(sha256, summary, options)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// API call\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync function callTagApi(\n  sha256: string,\n  summary: string,\n  options: TagOptions,\n): Promise<string[]> {\n  try {\n    const { default: Anthropic } = await (import('@anthropic-ai/sdk') as Promise<{ default: typeof import('@anthropic-ai/sdk').default }>)\n\n    const client = new Anthropic()\n    const response = await client.messages.create({\n      model: options.model,\n      max_tokens: 60,\n      system: 'You are a tag extractor. Respond with a JSON array only — no prose, no markdown.',\n      messages: [\n        {\n          role: 'user',\n          content: `Extract 3-5 short keyword tags from this session summary. Respond with JSON array only: [\"tag1\",\"tag2\"]\\n\\n${summary.slice(0, 500)}`,\n        },\n      ],\n    })\n\n    const block = response.content[0]\n    const text = block && block.type === 'text' ? block.text.trim() : ''\n    if (!text) return []\n\n    const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(text)\n    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return []\n\n    const tags = parsed\n      .filter((t): t is string => typeof t === 'string' && t.length > 0 && t.length <= 50)\n      .slice(0, 5)\n      .map(t => t.trim().toLowerCase())\n      .filter(t => t.length > 0)\n\n    if (tags.length > 0) {\n      writeTagCache(sha256, tags, options.cacheDir)\n    }\n    return tags\n  } catch {\n    // Never crash — API errors, JSON parse errors, missing SDK\n    return []\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rendering\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Render tags as pill badges. Returns '' for empty array. */\nexport function renderTagBadges(tags: string[]): string {\n  if (tags.length === 0) return ''\n  const pills = tags\n    .map(t => `<span class=\"tag\" data-tag=\"${escHtml(t)}\">${escHtml(t)}</span>`)\n    .join('')\n  return `<div class=\"tag-list\">${pills}</div>`\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Cache helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction readTagCache(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string[] | null {\n  const path = tagCachePath(sha256, cacheDir)\n  if (!existsSync(path)) return null\n  try {\n    const raw = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')\n    const entry = JSON.parse(raw) as TagCache\n    return Array.isArray(entry.tags) ? entry.tags : null\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n}\n\nfunction writeTagCache(sha256: string, tags: string[], cacheDir: string): void {\n  try {\n    const tagsDir = join(cacheDir, 'tags')\n    mkdirSync(tagsDir, { recursive: true })\n    const entry: TagCache = { sha256, tags, generated_at: new Date().toISOString() }\n    writeFileSync(tagCachePath(sha256, cacheDir), JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2) + '\\n', 'utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    // Best-effort\n  }\n}\n\nfunction tagCachePath(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string {\n  return join(cacheDir, 'tags', `${sha256}.json`)\n}\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/build.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Report build orchestrator.\n * Generates a self-contained multi-page HTML website in Memory/07_reports/.\n */\n\nimport {\n  existsSync,\n  mkdirSync,\n  readdirSync,\n  readFileSync,\n  writeFileSync,\n  appendFileSync,\n  rmSync,\n} from 'node:fs'\nimport { basename, dirname, join } from 'node:path'\n\nimport type { BuildReportOptions } from './types.js'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\nimport { computeStats, extractToolNames, accumulateToolCounts } from './stats.js'\nimport { renderDashboard } from './render/dashboard.js'\nimport { renderTranscriptList } from './render/transcript-list.js'\nimport { renderTranscript } from './render/transcript.js'\nimport type { RenderedMessage } from './render/transcript.js'\nimport { renderKnowledge } from './render/knowledge.js'\nimport { renderGoals } from './render/goals.js'\nimport { renderTodos } from './render/todos.js'\nimport { renderArchive } from './render/archive.js'\nimport { renderProjects } from './render/projects.js'\nimport { renderGraph } from './render/graph.js'\nimport { buildSearchIndex, renderSearchPage } from './render/search.js'\nimport { buildLinkGraph } from './render/links.js'\nimport { renderRssFeed } from './render/rss.js'\nimport { getSummary } from './summarize.js'\nimport type { SummaryOptions } from './summarize.js'\nimport { getTags } from './tags.js'\nimport { loadLocale } from './i18n/index.js'\nimport type { Translations } from './i18n/types.js'\nimport { getLogger } from './log.js'\n\nconst DEFAULT_MODEL = 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Main entry point\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport async function buildReport(options: BuildReportOptions): Promise<void> {\n  const { root, output } = options\n  const noAi = options.noAi ?? false\n  const model = options.model ?? DEFAULT_MODEL\n  const locale = options.locale ?? 'en'\n  const ghPagesBranch = options.ghPagesBranch ?? ''\n  const cname = options.cname ?? ''\n  const webhookUrl = options.webhookUrl ?? ''\n  const newSessionIds = options.newSessionIds ?? []\n  const reportBaseUrl = options.reportBaseUrl ?? ''\n\n  // Cache lives outside the gitignored output dir so it survives rm -rf\n  const cacheDir = join(root, 'Memory', '.report-cache')\n\n  // Load translations\n  const t: Translations = loadLocale(locale)\n\n  clearOutputDir(output)\n\n  // Ensure output dirs exist\n  mkdirSync(output, { recursive: true })\n  mkdirSync(join(output, 'transcripts'), { recursive: true })\n  mkdirSync(join(output, 'goals'), { recursive: true })\n  mkdirSync(join(output, 'knowledge'), { recursive: true })\n  mkdirSync(join(output, 'todos'), { recursive: true })\n  mkdirSync(join(output, 'archive'), { recursive: true })\n  mkdirSync(join(output, 'projects'), { recursive: true })\n  mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true })\n\n  // Ensure Memory/07_reports is gitignored\n  ensureGitignore(root, 'Memory/07_reports/')\n\n  // Load all manifests\n  const manifests = loadManifests(root)\n\n  // Build link graph from clean markdown\n  const linkGraph = buildLinkGraph(manifests, root)\n\n  // Accumulate tool counts while processing transcripts\n  let toolCounts: Record<string, number> = {}\n\n  // Summary options\n  const summaryOptions: SummaryOptions = { cacheDir, noAi, model }\n\n  // Process each transcript: parse → summarize → render → write → discard\n  const snippets: Record<string, string> = {}\n  const summaries: Record<string, string> = {}\n  const summaryPromises: Array<Promise<void>> = []\n\n  for (const m of manifests) {\n    const messages = parseCleanMarkdownMessages(m, root)\n\n    // Tool events from parsed transcript (try raw path)\n    const rawPath = resolveRawPath(m, root)\n    if (rawPath && existsSync(rawPath)) {\n      try {\n        const toolSummaries = extractToolSummariesFromRaw(rawPath, m.client)\n        const names = extractToolNames(toolSummaries)\n        toolCounts = accumulateToolCounts(toolCounts, names)\n      } catch {\n        // Skip if raw parsing fails\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Search snippet from first message text\n    const firstMsg = messages[0]\n    snippets[m.session_id] = firstMsg ? firstMsg.text.slice(0, 300) : ''\n\n    // Summarize + render (deferred to allow parallel API calls)\n    const manifest = m\n    const msgs = messages\n    summaryPromises.push(\n      (async () => {\n        try {\n        const summary = await getSummary(manifest.raw_sha256, msgs, summaryOptions)\n        summaries[manifest.session_id] = summary\n        const backlinkIds = linkGraph.backlinks[manifest.session_id] ?? []\n        const backlinkManifests = backlinkIds\n          .map(id => manifests.find(x => x.session_id === id))\n          .filter((x): x is ManifestEntry => x !== undefined)\n\n        const html = renderTranscript(msgs, manifest, summary, backlinkManifests, t, reportBaseUrl)\n        const outPath = transcriptOutputPath(output, manifest)\n        mkdirSync(dirname(outPath), { recursive: true })\n        writeFileSync(outPath, html, 'utf-8')\n        } catch (err: unknown) {\n          // eslint-disable-next-line no-console\n          getLogger().warn(`[build] Failed to render ${manifest.session_id}: ${String(err)}`)\n        }\n      })(),\n    )\n  }\n\n  // Wait for all transcript renders (parallel, bounded by semaphore in summarize.ts)\n  await Promise.all(summaryPromises)\n\n  // Extract tags in parallel (after summaries are ready)\n  const tagOptions = { cacheDir, noAi, model }\n  const tags: Record<string, string[]> = {}\n  await Promise.all(\n    manifests.map(async m => {\n      const summary = summaries[m.session_id] ?? ''\n      tags[m.session_id] = await getTags(m.raw_sha256, summary, tagOptions)\n    }),\n  )\n\n  // Compute final stats with accumulated tool counts\n  const stats = computeStats(manifests, toolCounts)\n\n  // Load markdown content for various pages\n  const goalFiles = loadMarkdownFiles(join(root, 'Memory', '01_goals'))\n  const todoFiles = loadMarkdownFiles(join(root, 'Memory', '02_todos'))\n  const knowledgeFiles = loadMarkdownFiles(join(root, 'Memory', '04_knowledge'))\n  const archiveFiles = loadMarkdownFiles(join(root, 'Memory', '05_archive'))\n\n  // Dashboard\n  writeFileSync(join(output, 'index.html'), renderDashboard(stats, manifests, t), 'utf-8')\n\n  // Session list\n  writeFileSync(\n    join(output, 'transcripts', 'index.html'),\n    renderTranscriptList(manifests, t, summaries, tags),\n    'utf-8',\n  )\n\n  // Goals page\n  writeFileSync(\n    join(output, 'goals', 'index.html'),\n    renderGoals(goalFiles, t),\n    'utf-8',\n  )\n\n  // Knowledge page\n  writeFileSync(\n    join(output, 'knowledge', 'index.html'),\n    renderKnowledge(knowledgeFiles, t),\n    'utf-8',\n  )\n\n  // Todos page\n  writeFileSync(\n    join(output, 'todos', 'index.html'),\n    renderTodos(todoFiles, t),\n    'utf-8',\n  )\n\n  // Archive page\n  writeFileSync(\n    join(output, 'archive', 'index.html'),\n    renderArchive(archiveFiles, t),\n    'utf-8',\n  )\n\n  // Projects page\n  writeFileSync(\n    join(output, 'projects', 'index.html'),\n    renderProjects(manifests, t),\n    'utf-8',\n  )\n\n  // Knowledge graph\n  writeFileSync(\n    join(output, 'graph.html'),\n    renderGraph(manifests, knowledgeFiles, linkGraph, t),\n    'utf-8',\n  )\n\n  // Search\n  const searchIndex = buildSearchIndex(manifests, m => snippets[m.session_id] ?? '')\n  writeFileSync(join(output, 'search.html'), renderSearchPage(searchIndex, t), 'utf-8')\n\n  // RSS Feed\n  writeFileSync(join(output, 'feed.xml'), renderRssFeed(manifests, summaries, reportBaseUrl), 'utf-8')\n\n  // GitHub Pages deployment (non-blocking on failure)\n  if (ghPagesBranch) {\n    try {\n      const { deployGithubPages } = await import('./deploy/github-pages.js')\n      await deployGithubPages({ repoRoot: root, outputDir: output, branch: ghPagesBranch, cname })\n    } catch {\n      // Logged inside deployGithubPages\n    }\n  } else if (cname) {\n    // Write CNAME even without deploy (writeFileSync already imported at top)\n    writeFileSync(join(output, 'CNAME'), cname + '\\n', 'utf-8')\n  }\n\n  // Webhook notification (non-blocking on failure)\n  if (webhookUrl) {\n    try {\n      const { sendWebhook } = await import('./deploy/webhook.js')\n      await sendWebhook({\n        url: webhookUrl,\n        sessionCount: manifests.length,\n        newSessionIds,\n      })\n    } catch {\n      // Logged inside sendWebhook\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Manifest loading\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction loadManifests(root: string): ManifestEntry[] {\n  const manifestsDir = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'manifests')\n  if (!existsSync(manifestsDir)) return []\n\n  const manifests: ManifestEntry[] = []\n  loadManifestsRecursive(manifestsDir, manifests)\n  return manifests\n}\n\nfunction loadManifestsRecursive(dir: string, out: ManifestEntry[]): void {\n  let entries: string[]\n  try {\n    entries = readdirSync(dir)\n  } catch {\n    return\n  }\n\n  for (const entry of entries) {\n    const fullPath = join(dir, entry)\n    if (entry.endsWith('.json')) {\n      try {\n        const raw = readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8')\n        const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(raw)\n        if (\n          parsed !== null &&\n          typeof parsed === 'object' &&\n          typeof (parsed as Record<string, unknown>)['session_id'] === 'string' &&\n          typeof (parsed as Record<string, unknown>)['client'] === 'string'\n        ) {\n          out.push(parsed as ManifestEntry)\n        }\n      } catch {\n        // Skip malformed manifests\n      }\n    } else {\n      // Recurse into subdirectories\n      loadManifestsRecursive(fullPath, out)\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Clean markdown message parsing\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Parse messages from a clean markdown file. */\nexport function parseCleanMarkdownMessages(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): RenderedMessage[] {\n  const cleanPath = m.repo_clean_path\n    ? join(root, m.repo_clean_path)\n    : m.global_clean_path\n  if (!cleanPath || !existsSync(cleanPath)) return []\n\n  let content: string\n  try {\n    content = readFileSync(cleanPath, 'utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    return []\n  }\n\n  return parseMessagesFromMarkdown(content)\n}\n\n/** Extract role/timestamp/text from clean markdown format. */\nexport function parseMessagesFromMarkdown(content: string): RenderedMessage[] {\n  const messages: RenderedMessage[] = []\n\n  // Find ## Messages section\n  const msgSection = content.indexOf('\\n## Messages')\n  if (msgSection === -1) return []\n\n  const body = content.slice(msgSection + 12) // after \"## Messages\\n\"\n\n  // Split on ### N. role headers\n  const msgRe = /^### \\d+\\.\\s+(\\w+)/gm\n  let match: RegExpExecArray | null\n  const starts: Array<{ index: number; role: string }> = []\n\n  while ((match = msgRe.exec(body)) !== null) {\n    starts.push({ index: match.index, role: match[1] ?? 'unknown' })\n  }\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < starts.length; i++) {\n    const start = starts[i]\n    if (!start) continue\n    const nextStart = starts[i + 1]\n    const chunk = nextStart\n      ? body.slice(start.index, nextStart.index)\n      : body.slice(start.index)\n\n    const lines = chunk.split('\\n')\n    // First line is the header, second may be timestamp\n    let timestamp = ''\n    const textLines: string[] = []\n    let pastHeader = false\n\n    for (const line of lines) {\n      if (!pastHeader && line.startsWith('### ')) {\n        pastHeader = true\n        continue\n      }\n      if (!pastHeader) continue\n      const tsMatch = line.match(/^- Timestamp:\\s*`([^`]+)`/)\n      if (tsMatch && !timestamp) {\n        timestamp = tsMatch[1] ?? ''\n        continue\n      }\n      textLines.push(line)\n    }\n\n    // Remove leading/trailing blank lines from text\n    const text = textLines.join('\\n').trim()\n    if (text || timestamp) {\n      messages.push({ role: start.role, timestamp, text })\n    }\n  }\n\n  return messages\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Raw transcript tool extraction\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Best-effort extraction of tool event summaries from a raw JSONL file. */\nfunction extractToolSummariesFromRaw(rawPath: string, _client: string): string[] {\n  const summaries: string[] = []\n  let content: string\n  try {\n    content = readFileSync(rawPath, 'utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    return []\n  }\n\n  for (const line of content.split('\\n')) {\n    const trimmed = line.trim()\n    if (!trimmed) continue\n    try {\n      const record = JSON.parse(trimmed) as Record<string, unknown>\n      // Codex: function_call type\n      const type = record['type'] as string | undefined\n      if (type === 'function_call' || type === 'custom_tool_call') {\n        const name = (record['name'] as string | undefined) ?? type\n        summaries.push(name)\n        continue\n      }\n      // Claude: tool_use content blocks\n      const payload = record['message'] as Record<string, unknown> | undefined\n      const content2 = (payload?.['content'] ?? record['content']) as unknown[] | undefined\n      if (Array.isArray(content2)) {\n        for (const block of content2) {\n          if (\n            block !== null &&\n            typeof block === 'object' &&\n            (block as Record<string, unknown>)['type'] === 'tool_use'\n          ) {\n            const name = ((block as Record<string, unknown>)['name'] as string | undefined) ?? 'tool_use'\n            summaries.push(name)\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // Skip malformed lines\n    }\n  }\n\n  return summaries\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Output path helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction transcriptOutputPath(output: string, m: ManifestEntry): string {\n  const cleanPath = m.repo_clean_path || m.global_clean_path || ''\n  const stem = cleanPath ? basename(cleanPath, '.md') : m.session_id\n  return join(output, 'transcripts', m.client, `${stem}.html`)\n}\n\nfunction resolveRawPath(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): string | null {\n  if (m.repo_raw_path) {\n    return join(root, m.repo_raw_path)\n  }\n  if (m.global_raw_path) {\n    return m.global_raw_path\n  }\n  return null\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Markdown file loader (for goals/knowledge/todos/archive)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction loadMarkdownFiles(dir: string): Array<{ filename: string; title: string; content: string }> {\n  if (!existsSync(dir)) return []\n\n  let entries: string[]\n  try {\n    entries = readdirSync(dir)\n  } catch {\n    return []\n  }\n\n  return entries\n    .filter(e => e.endsWith('.md'))\n    .sort()\n    .map(filename => {\n      const fullPath = join(dir, filename)\n      let content = ''\n      try {\n        content = readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8')\n      } catch {\n        // empty\n      }\n      const title = extractMarkdownTitle(content) || basename(filename, '.md')\n      return { filename, title, content }\n    })\n}\n\nfunction clearOutputDir(output: string): void {\n  if (!existsSync(output)) return\n\n  let entries: string[]\n  try {\n    entries = readdirSync(output)\n  } catch {\n    return\n  }\n\n  for (const entry of entries) {\n    try {\n      rmSync(join(output, entry), { recursive: true, force: true })\n    } catch {\n      // Best-effort cleanup\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfunction extractMarkdownTitle(content: string): string {\n  const match = content.match(/^#\\s+(.+)$/m)\n  return match?.[1]?.trim() ?? ''\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// .gitignore management\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function ensureGitignore(root: string, entry: string): void {\n  const gitignorePath = join(root, '.gitignore')\n  let existing = ''\n  if (existsSync(gitignorePath)) {\n    try {\n      existing = readFileSync(gitignorePath, 'utf-8')\n    } catch {\n      return\n    }\n  }\n  // Check if already present (normalize line endings)\n  const lines = existing.replace(/\\r\\n/g, '\\n').split('\\n')\n  if (lines.some(l => l.trim() === entry.trim())) return\n\n  // Append with trailing newline\n  const append = existing.endsWith('\\n') ? entry + '\\n' : '\\n' + entry + '\\n'\n  try {\n    appendFileSync(gitignorePath, append, 'utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    // Best-effort\n  }\n}\n"
}

{

  "content": "/**\n * i18n system: build-time locale loading.\n * Supported: 'en' (default), 'zh-CN'. Unknown locales fall back to 'en'.\n */\n\nexport type { Translations } from './types.js'\nimport type { Translations } from './types.js'\nimport { en } from './en.js'\nimport { zhCN } from './zh-CN.js'\n\nconst LOCALES: Record<string, Translations> = {\n  'en': en,\n  'zh-CN': zhCN,\n}\n\nexport function loadLocale(locale: string): Translations {\n  return LOCALES[locale] ?? en\n}\n"
}

}

  "content": "import type { Translations } from './types.js'\n\nexport const zhCN: Translations = {\n  nav: {\n    dashboard: '仪表盘',\n    sessions: '会话',\n    projects: '项目',\n    graph: '知识图谱',\n    goals: '目标',\n    todos: '待办',\n    knowledge: '知识库',\n    archive: '归档',\n    search: '搜索',\n  },\n  dashboard: {\n    title: '记忆仪表盘',\n    subtitle: '活动时间：{from} 至 {to}',\n    sessions: '会话数',\n    messages: '消息数',\n    toolEvents: '工具调用',\n    activeDays: '活跃天数',\n    recentSessions: '最近会话',\n  },\n  sessions: {\n    title: '会话列表',\n    noSessions: '暂无导入的会话。',\n    client: '客户端',\n    date: '日期',\n    id: 'ID',\n    msgs: '消息',\n    tools: '工具',\n    all: '全部',\n  },\n  transcript: {\n    aiSummary: 'AI 摘要',\n    referencedBy: '被引用',\n    messages: '消息',\n    noMessages: '未找到消息。',\n    client: '客户端',\n    sessionId: '会话 ID',\n    branch: '分支',\n    workingDir: '工作目录',\n    sha256: 'SHA-256',\n    toolEvents: '工具调用',\n  },\n  graph: {\n    title: '知识图谱',\n    subtitle: '会话与知识文件之间的关联',\n    noData: '暂无图谱数据。',\n  },\n  projects: {\n    title: '项目',\n    noProjects: '未找到项目。',\n    sessions: '个会话',\n  },\n  search: {\n    title: '搜索',\n    placeholder: '搜索会话...',\n    results: '条结果',\n    noResults: '未找到结果。',\n  },\n  common: {\n    loading: '加载中...',\n    unknown: '未知',\n  },\n}\n"
}

Write input={"content":"/**\n * GitHub Pages deployment: publish report output to a dedicated branch.\n */\n\nimport { cpSync, existsSync, mkdtempSync, readdirSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } ...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/deploy/github-pages.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * GitHub Pages deployment: publish report output to a dedicated branch.\n */\n\nimport { cpSync, existsSync, mkdtempSync, readdirSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\nimport { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process'\n\nimport { getLogger } from '../log.js'\n\nexport interface GithubPagesOptions {\n  /** Repository root (contains .git/) */\n  repoRoot: string\n  /** Report output directory (e.g. Memory/07_reports) */\n  outputDir: string\n  /** Branch name to push to (e.g. 'gh-pages'). Empty string = skip. */\n  branch: string\n  /** Custom domain (CNAME). Empty string = skip. */\n  cname: string\n}\n\nconst COMMIT_MESSAGE = 'chore: publish memorytree report'\nconst COMMITTER_NAME = 'MemoryTree'\nconst COMMITTER_EMAIL = 'memorytree@local.invalid'\n\n/** Safe character set for branch names. */\nconst BRANCH_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9._/-]+$/\n\nexport async function deployGithubPages(options: GithubPagesOptions): Promise<void> {\n  const { repoRoot, outputDir, branch, cname } = options\n  const logger = getLogger()\n\n  if (!branch) return\n\n  if (!BRANCH_RE.test(branch)) {\n    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Invalid branch name, skipping deploy: \"${branch}\"`)\n    return\n  }\n\n  if (!existsSync(outputDir)) {\n    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Output directory does not exist, skipping deploy: ${outputDir}`)\n    return\n  }\n\n  try {\n    if (cname) {\n      const cnamePath = join(outputDir, 'CNAME')\n      writeFileSync(cnamePath, cname + '\\n', 'utf-8')\n      logger.info(`[gh-pages] CNAME written: ${cname}`)\n    }\n\n    const remoteUrl = git(repoRoot, 'remote', 'get-url', 'origin').trim()\n    if (!remoteUrl) {\n      logger.warn('[gh-pages] No origin remote configured, skipping deploy.')\n      return\n    }\n\n    const publishRoot = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'mt-gh-pages-'))\n    try {\n      git(publishRoot, 'init')\n      git(publishRoot, 'remote', 'add', 'origin', remoteUrl)\n\n      if (remoteBranchExists(publishRoot, branch)) {\n        logger.info(`[gh-pages] Updating existing origin/${branch} branch.`)\n        git(publishRoot, 'fetch', '--depth', '1', 'origin', branch)\n        git(publishRoot, 'checkout', '-B', branch, 'FETCH_HEAD')\n      } else {\n        logger.info(`[gh-pages] Branch '${branch}' not found, creating orphan publish branch.`)\n        git(publishRoot, 'checkout', '--orphan', branch)\n      }\n\n      clearDirectory(publishRoot)\n      copyDirectoryContents(outputDir, publishRoot)\n\n      git(publishRoot, 'add', '--all')\n      if (!hasPendingChanges(publishRoot)) {\n        logger.info(`[gh-pages] No changes to publish for origin/${branch}.`)\n        return\n      }\n\n      git(\n        publishRoot,\n        '-c', `user.name=${COMMITTER_NAME}`,\n        '-c', `user.email=${COMMITTER_EMAIL}`,\n        'commit',\n        '-m',\n        COMMIT_MESSAGE,\n      )\n      git(publishRoot, 'push', 'origin', `HEAD:${branch}`)\n      logger.info(`[gh-pages] Successfully pushed report to origin/${branch}`)\n    } finally {\n      rmSync(publishRoot, { recursive: true, force: true })\n    }\n  } catch (err: unknown) {\n    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Deploy failed: ${String(err)}`)\n    // Never throw; report deploy failure must not abort heartbeat.\n  }\n}\n\nfunction remoteBranchExists(cwd: string, branch: string): boolean {\n  try {\n    git(cwd, 'ls-remote', '--exit-code', '--heads', 'origin', branch)\n    return true\n  } catch {\n    return false\n  }\n}\n\nfunction clearDirectory(root: string): void {\n  for (const entry of readdirSync(root)) {\n    if (entry === '.git') continue\n    rmSync(join(root, entry), { recursive: true, force: true })\n  }\n}\n\nfunction copyDirectoryContents(source: string, destination: string): void {\n  for (const entry of readdirSync(source)) {\n    cpSync(join(source, entry), join(destination, entry), { recursive: true, force: true })\n  }\n}\n\nfunction hasPendingChanges(cwd: string): boolean {\n  return git(cwd, 'status', '--short').trim().length > 0\n}\n\n/** Invoke git with explicit args; no shell interpolation. */\nfunction git(cwd: string, ...args: string[]): string {\n  return execFileSync('git', args, { cwd, stdio: 'pipe' }).toString()\n}\n"
}

Write input={"content":"/**\n * Webhook notification: sends a POST to configurable endpoints.\n * Supports Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and generic webhooks.\n * Never throws — failure...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/deploy/webhook.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Webhook notification: sends a POST to configurable endpoints.\n * Supports Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and generic webhooks.\n * Never throws — failures are logged as warnings.\n */\n\nimport { getLogger } from '../log.js'\n\nexport interface WebhookOptions {\n  url: string\n  sessionCount: number\n  newSessionIds?: string[]\n  reportUrl?: string\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Platform detection\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ntype Platform = 'feishu' | 'telegram' | 'discord' | 'slack' | 'generic'\n\n/**\n * Detect platform from a parsed URL.\n * Uses exact hostname matching to prevent subdomain-spoofing attacks like\n * https://my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com being mistaken for Feishu.\n */\nfunction detectPlatform(parsedUrl: URL): Platform {\n  const h = parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase()\n  if (h === 'open.feishu.cn' || h === 'open.larksuite.com') return 'feishu'\n  if (h === 'api.telegram.org') return 'telegram'\n  if (h === 'discord.com' || h === 'discordapp.com') return 'discord'\n  if (h === 'hooks.slack.com') return 'slack'\n  return 'generic'\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Payload builders\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction buildFeishuPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {\n  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options\n  const newCount = newSessionIds.length\n  const actionBtn = reportUrl\n    ? [{\n        tag: 'button',\n        text: { content: 'View Report', tag: 'plain_text' },\n        url: reportUrl,\n        type: 'primary',\n      }]\n    : []\n\n  return {\n    msg_type: 'interactive',\n    card: {\n      header: {\n        title: { content: 'MemoryTree Report Updated', tag: 'plain_text' },\n        template: 'blue',\n      },\n      elements: [\n        {\n          tag: 'div',\n          text: {\n            tag: 'lark_md',\n            content: `**Sessions:** ${sessionCount} total${newCount ? `, ${newCount} new` : ''}`,\n          },\n        },\n        ...(actionBtn.length > 0 ? [{ tag: 'action', actions: actionBtn }] : []),\n      ],\n    },\n  }\n}\n\nfunction buildTelegramPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {\n  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options\n  const newCount = newSessionIds.length\n  let text = `📊 *MemoryTree Report Updated*\\n\\nSessions: ${sessionCount} total`\n  if (newCount > 0) text += `, ${newCount} new`\n  if (reportUrl) text += `\\n[View Report](${reportUrl})`\n  return { text, parse_mode: 'Markdown' }\n}\n\nfunction buildDiscordPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {\n  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options\n  const newCount = newSessionIds.length\n  let content = `📊 **MemoryTree Report Updated** — ${sessionCount} sessions total`\n  if (newCount > 0) content += `, ${newCount} new`\n  if (reportUrl) content += ` · [View Report](${reportUrl})`\n  return { content }\n}\n\nfunction buildSlackPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {\n  const { sessionCount, newSessionIds = [], reportUrl } = options\n  const newCount = newSessionIds.length\n  let text = `📊 MemoryTree Report Updated — ${sessionCount} sessions total`\n  if (newCount > 0) text += `, ${newCount} new`\n  if (reportUrl) text += ` · <${reportUrl}|View Report>`\n  return { text }\n}\n\nfunction buildGenericPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {\n  return {\n    event: 'memorytree.report.updated',\n    session_count: options.sessionCount,\n    new_session_count: options.newSessionIds?.length ?? 0,\n    new_session_ids: options.newSessionIds ?? [],\n    report_url: options.reportUrl ?? '',\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// URL safety validation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/** Private / loopback IPv4 ranges. */\nconst PRIVATE_IPV4_RE =\n  /^(localhost|127\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|0\\.0\\.0\\.0|10\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|172\\.(1[6-9]|2\\d|3[01])\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|192\\.168\\.\\d+\\.\\d+|169\\.254\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)$/i\n\n/**\n * Returns true for all private / loopback IPv6 addresses.\n * Node.js URL.hostname may return IPv6 with or without surrounding brackets,\n * so we strip them before checking.\n *   ::1           loopback\n *   fe80::/10     link-local (fe80 – febf)\n *   fc00::/7      ULA (fc and fd)\n *   ::ffff:0:0/96 IPv4-mapped\n */\nfunction isPrivateIPv6(hostname: string): boolean {\n  // Strip optional surrounding brackets added by some URL implementations\n  const h = hostname.toLowerCase().replace(/^\\[|]$/g, '')\n  if (h === '::1') return true\n  if (/^fe[89ab]/i.test(h)) return true       // fe80::/10 link-local\n  if (h.startsWith('fc') || h.startsWith('fd')) return true  // fc00::/7 ULA\n  if (h.startsWith('::ffff:')) return true    // IPv4-mapped\n  return false\n}\n\nfunction validateWebhookUrl(raw: string): URL {\n  let url: URL\n  try {\n    url = new URL(raw)\n  } catch {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid webhook URL: ${raw}`)\n  }\n  if (url.protocol !== 'https:') {\n    throw new Error(`Webhook URL must use https: (got ${url.protocol})`)\n  }\n  if (PRIVATE_IPV4_RE.test(url.hostname) || isPrivateIPv6(url.hostname)) {\n    throw new Error(`Webhook URL hostname is private/loopback: ${url.hostname}`)\n  }\n  return url\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Main send function\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport async function sendWebhook(options: WebhookOptions): Promise<void> {\n  if (!options.url) return\n  const logger = getLogger()\n\n  let parsedUrl: URL\n  try {\n    parsedUrl = validateWebhookUrl(options.url)\n  } catch (err) {\n    logger.warn(`[webhook] Rejected URL: ${String(err)}`)\n    return\n  }\n\n  const platform = detectPlatform(parsedUrl)\n\n  const payloadMap: Record<Platform, (o: WebhookOptions) => Record<string, unknown>> = {\n    feishu: buildFeishuPayload,\n    telegram: buildTelegramPayload,\n    discord: buildDiscordPayload,\n    slack: buildSlackPayload,\n    generic: buildGenericPayload,\n  }\n\n  const payload = payloadMap[platform](options)\n\n  try {\n    const { default: https } = await import('node:https')\n    const body = JSON.stringify(payload)\n\n    await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {\n      const url = parsedUrl\n      const req = https.request(\n        {\n          hostname: url.hostname,\n          port: url.port || 443,\n          path: url.pathname + url.search,\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: {\n            'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n            'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(body),\n          },\n        },\n        res => {\n          // Consume response to avoid socket leak\n          res.resume()\n          if (res.statusCode && res.statusCode >= 400) {\n            reject(new Error(`HTTP ${res.statusCode}`))\n          } else {\n            resolve()\n          }\n        },\n      )\n      req.on('error', reject)\n      req.setTimeout(5000, () => { req.destroy(); reject(new Error('timeout')) })\n      req.write(body)\n      req.end()\n    })\n\n    logger.info(`[webhook] Sent ${platform} notification`)\n  } catch (err: unknown) {\n    logger.warn(`[webhook] Failed to send notification: ${String(err)}`)\n    // Never throw\n  }\n}\n"
}
