   124→  })
   125→
   126→  it('shows total session count in center', () => {
   127→    const svg = renderClientDoughnut({ codex: 7, claude: 3 })
   128→    expect(svg).toContain('10')
   129→    expect(svg).toContain('sessions')
   130→  })
   131→
   132→  it('handles single client', () => {
   133→    const svg = renderClientDoughnut({ codex: 10 })
   134→    expect(svg).toContain('10')
   135→    expect(svg).not.toContain('No data')
   136→  })
   137→
   138→  it('skips zero-count clients', () => {
   139→    const svg = renderClientDoughnut({ codex: 5, claude: 0 })
   140→    expect(svg).toContain('codex')
   141→    expect(svg).not.toContain('>claude<')
   142→  })
   143→})
   144→
   145→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   146→// renderWeeklyLine
   147→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   148→
   149→describe('renderWeeklyLine', () => {
   150→  it('produces valid SVG with empty data', () => {
   151→    const svg = renderWeeklyLine({})
   152→    expect(svg).toContain('<svg')
   153→    expect(svg).toContain('<polyline')
   154→  })
   155→
   156→  it('renders area path and line', () => {
   157→    const svg = renderWeeklyLine({ '2026-W10': 10, '2026-W11': 20 })
   158→    expect(svg).toContain('<path')
   159→    expect(svg).toContain('<polyline')
   160→  })
   161→
   162→  it('handles single non-zero week', () => {
   163→    const svg = renderWeeklyLine({ '2026-W01': 5 })
   164→    expect(svg).toContain('<svg')
   165→    expect(svg).not.toContain('NaN')
   166→  })
   167→
   168→  it('does not contain NaN with all-zero data', () => {
   169→    const svg = renderWeeklyLine({})
   170→    expect(svg).not.toContain('NaN')
   171→  })
   172→})
   173→
   174→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   175→// renderToolBar
   176→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   177→
   178→describe('renderToolBar', () => {
   179→  it('shows no-data message for empty input', () => {
   180→    const svg = renderToolBar({})
   181→    expect(svg).toContain('No tool data available')
   182→  })
   183→
   184→  it('renders bars for provided tools', () => {
   185→    const svg = renderToolBar({ Bash: 10, Read: 5, Write: 3 })
   186→    expect(svg).toContain('Bash')
   187→    expect(svg).toContain('Read')
   188→    expect(svg).toContain('Write')
   189→  })
   190→
   191→  it('limits to top 10 tools', () => {
   192→    const tools: Record<string, number> = {}
   193→    for (let i = 0; i < 15; i++) {
   194→      tools[`Tool${i}`] = 15 - i
   195→    }
   196→    const svg = renderToolBar(tools)
   197→    // Should not contain Tool10 (11th ranked) since sorted by count desc
   198→    const rectCount = (svg.match(/<rect/g) ?? []).length
   199→    expect(rectCount).toBeLessThanOrEqual(10)
   200→  })
   201→
   202→  it('sorts tools by count descending', () => {
   203→    const svg = renderToolBar({ LowUsage: 1, HighUsage: 100 })
   204→    const highPos = svg.indexOf('HighUsage')
   205→    const lowPos = svg.indexOf('LowUsage')
   206→    expect(highPos).toBeGreaterThan(-1)
   207→    expect(lowPos).toBeGreaterThan(-1)
   208→    // HighUsage should appear before LowUsage in the SVG
   209→    expect(highPos).toBeLessThan(lowPos)
   210→  })
   211→})
   212→=== C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/graph.test.ts ===
   213→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
   214→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'
   215→import type { LinkGraph } from '../../../src/types/report.js'
   216→import type { MarkdownFile } from '../../../src/report/render/layout.js'
   217→import { buildGraphData, renderGraph } from '../../../src/report/render/graph.js'
   218→import { en } from '../../../src/report/i18n/en.js'
   219→
   220→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   221→// Fixture helpers
   222→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   223→
   224→function makeManifest(overrides: Partial<ManifestEntry> = {}): ManifestEntry {
   225→  return {
   226→    client: 'claude',
   227→    project: 'test',
   228→    session_id: 'sess-001',
   229→    raw_sha256: 'abc123',
   230→    title: 'Test Session',
   231→    started_at: '2026-01-01T10:00:00Z',
   232→    imported_at: '2026-01-01T10:00:00Z',
   233→    cwd: '/home/user/project',
   234→    branch: 'main',
   235→    raw_source_path: '',
   236→    raw_upload_permission: 'allowed',
   237→    global_raw_path: '',
   238→    global_clean_path: '',
   239→    global_manifest_path: '',
   240→    repo_raw_path: '',
   241→    repo_clean_path: '',
   242→    repo_manifest_path: '',
   243→    message_count: 5,
   244→    tool_event_count: 2,
   245→    cleaning_mode: 'standard',
   246→    repo_mirror_enabled: false,
   247→    ...overrides,
   248→  }
   249→}
   250→
   251→function emptyLinkGraph(): LinkGraph {
   252→  return { backlinks: {}, forwardLinks: {} }
   253→}
   254→
   255→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   256→// buildGraphData
   257→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   258→
   259→describe('buildGraphData', () => {
   260→  it('creates one session node per manifest', () => {
   261→    const manifests = [
   262→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's1' }),
   263→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's2' }),
   264→    ]
   265→    const { nodes } = buildGraphData(manifests, [], emptyLinkGraph())
   266→    const sessionNodes = nodes.filter(n => n.type === 'session')
   267→    expect(sessionNodes).toHaveLength(2)
   268→  })
   269→
   270→  it('creates knowledge nodes for each knowledge file', () => {
   271→    const kf: MarkdownFile[] = [
   272→      { filename: 'note.md', title: 'Note', content: '# Note' },
   273→    ]
   274→    const { nodes } = buildGraphData([], kf, emptyLinkGraph())
   275→    const knowledgeNodes = nodes.filter(n => n.type === 'knowledge')
   276→    expect(knowledgeNodes).toHaveLength(1)
   277→    expect(knowledgeNodes[0]!.id).toBe('knowledge:note.md')
   278→  })
   279→
   280→  it('creates edges from forwardLinks', () => {
   281→    const manifests = [
   282→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's1' }),
   283→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's2' }),
   284→    ]
   285→    const linkGraph: LinkGraph = {
   286→      backlinks: { s2: ['s1'] },
   287→      forwardLinks: { s1: ['s2'] },
   288→    }
   289→    const { edges } = buildGraphData(manifests, [], linkGraph)
   290→    expect(edges).toHaveLength(1)
   291→    expect(edges[0]!.source).toBe('s1')
   292→    expect(edges[0]!.target).toBe('s2')
   293→  })
   294→
   295→  it('ignores edges to non-existent nodes', () => {
   296→    const manifests = [makeManifest({ session_id: 's1' })]
   297→    const linkGraph: LinkGraph = {
   298→      backlinks: {},
   299→      forwardLinks: { s1: ['does-not-exist'] },
   300→    }
   301→    const { edges } = buildGraphData(manifests, [], linkGraph)
   302→    expect(edges).toHaveLength(0)
   303→  })
   304→
   305→  it('caps nodes at 500 (most recent first)', () => {
   306→    const manifests = Array.from({ length: 600 }, (_, i) =>
   307→      makeManifest({
   308→        session_id: `s${i}`,
   309→        started_at: `2026-01-${String(i % 28 + 1).padStart(2, '0')}T10:00:00Z`,
   310→      })
   311→    )
   312→    const { nodes } = buildGraphData(manifests, [], emptyLinkGraph())
   313→    const sessionNodes = nodes.filter(n => n.type === 'session')
   314→    expect(sessionNodes).toHaveLength(500)
   315→  })
   316→
   317→  it('handles empty input', () => {
   318→    const { nodes, edges } = buildGraphData([], [], emptyLinkGraph())
   319→    expect(nodes).toHaveLength(0)
   320→    expect(edges).toHaveLength(0)
   321→  })
   322→
   323→  it('sets correct node url for sessions', () => {
   324→    const m = makeManifest({ session_id: 'sess-abc', client: 'claude' })
   325→    const { nodes } = buildGraphData([m], [], emptyLinkGraph())
   326→    const n = nodes.find(x => x.id === 'sess-abc')
   327→    expect(n?.url).toContain('transcripts/claude')
   328→  })
   329→})
   330→
   331→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   332→// renderGraph
   333→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   334→
   335→describe('renderGraph', () => {
   336→  it('renders empty state when no manifests', () => {
   337→    const html = renderGraph([], [], emptyLinkGraph())
   338→    expect(html).toContain('No graph data available')
   339→  })
   340→
   341→  it('renders canvas element with nodes', () => {
   342→    const manifests = [makeManifest({ session_id: 's1' })]
   343→    const html = renderGraph(manifests, [], emptyLinkGraph())
   344→    expect(html).toContain('graph-canvas')
   345→    expect(html).toContain('GRAPH_DATA')
   346→  })
   347→
   348→  it('embeds graph data as JSON', () => {
   349→    const manifests = [makeManifest({ session_id: 'unique-sess-id' })]
   350→    const html = renderGraph(manifests, [], emptyLinkGraph())
   351→    expect(html).toContain('unique-sess-id')
   352→  })
   353→
   354→  it('uses translations for title', () => {
   355→    const manifests = [makeManifest({ session_id: 's1' })]
   356→    const html = renderGraph(manifests, [], emptyLinkGraph(), en)
   357→    expect(html).toContain('Knowledge Graph')
   358→  })
   359→
   360→  it('escapes </script> injection in graph data JSON', () => {
   361→    // The graph data is embedded as JSON inside a <script> block.
   362→    // </script> in node labels must be escaped to prevent premature script close.
   363→    const manifests = [makeManifest({ session_id: 's1', title: 'foo</script>bar' })]
   364→    const html = renderGraph(manifests, [], emptyLinkGraph())
   365→    // The literal </script> must not appear inside the <script> block
   366→    // (it gets escaped as <\/script> in the JSON)
   367→    expect(html).not.toContain('foo</script>bar')
   368→  })
   369→
   370→  it('shows node and edge counts in subtitle', () => {
   371→    const manifests = [
   372→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's1' }),
   373→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's2' }),
   374→    ]
   375→    const linkGraph: LinkGraph = {
   376→      backlinks: { s2: ['s1'] },
   377→      forwardLinks: { s1: ['s2'] },
   378→    }
   379→    const html = renderGraph(manifests, [], linkGraph)
   380→    expect(html).toContain('2 nodes')
   381→    expect(html).toContain('1 edge')
   382→  })
   383→})
   384→=== C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/links.test.ts ===
   385→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
   386→import { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
   387→import { join } from 'node:path'
   388→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
   389→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'
   390→import { extractLinks, buildLinkGraph } from '../../../src/report/render/links.js'
   391→
   392→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   393→// Helpers
   394→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   395→
   396→let tmpDir: string
   397→
   398→beforeEach(() => {
   399→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'links-test-'))
   400→})
   401→
   402→afterEach(() => {
   403→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
   404→})
   405→
   406→function makeManifest(id: string, stem: string, content?: string): ManifestEntry {
   407→  const cleanRelPath = `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/${stem}.md`
   408→  if (content !== undefined) {
   409→    const fullPath = join(tmpDir, cleanRelPath)
   410→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03'), { recursive: true })
   411→    writeFileSync(fullPath, content, 'utf-8')
   412→  }
   413→  return {
   414→    client: 'codex',
   415→    project: 'test',
   416→    session_id: id,
   417→    raw_sha256: id.slice(0, 8).padEnd(64, '0'),
   418→    title: `Session ${id}`,
   419→    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',
   420→    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',
   421→    cwd: '',
   422→    branch: 'main',
   423→    raw_source_path: '',
   424→    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',
   425→    global_raw_path: '',
   426→    global_clean_path: '',
   427→    global_manifest_path: '',
   428→    repo_raw_path: '',
   429→    repo_clean_path: cleanRelPath,
   430→    repo_manifest_path: '',
   431→    message_count: 1,
   432→    tool_event_count: 0,
   433→    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
   434→    repo_mirror_enabled: true,
   435→  }
   436→}
   437→
   438→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   439→// extractLinks
   440→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   441→
   442→describe('extractLinks', () => {
   443→  it('detects [[id]] references', () => {
   444→    const refs = extractLinks('See [[session-abc]] for details.')
   445→    expect(refs).toContain('session-abc')
   446→  })
   447→
   448→  it('detects multiple references', () => {
   449→    const refs = extractLinks('[[a]] and [[b]] are related.')
   450→    expect(refs).toHaveLength(2)
   451→    expect(refs).toContain('a')
   452→    expect(refs).toContain('b')
   453→  })
   454→
   455→  it('ignores partial brackets: single bracket', () => {
   456→    const refs = extractLinks('[not a link] and [another')
   457→    expect(refs).toHaveLength(0)
   458→  })
   459→
   460→  it('ignores newlines inside brackets', () => {
   461→    const refs = extractLinks('[[line1\nline2]]')
   462→    expect(refs).toHaveLength(0)
   463→  })
   464→
   465→  it('trims whitespace from refs', () => {
   466→    const refs = extractLinks('[[ spaced ]]')
   467→    expect(refs[0]).toBe('spaced')
   468→  })
   469→
   470→  it('returns empty for text with no links', () => {
   471→    expect(extractLinks('No links here.')).toEqual([])
   472→  })
   473→})
   474→
   475→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   476→// buildLinkGraph
   477→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   478→
   479→describe('buildLinkGraph', () => {
   480→  it('builds forward and back links', () => {
   481→    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\nSee [[bbb]]')
   482→    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\nNo links here')
   483→
   484→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)
   485→    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa']).toContain('bbb')
   486→    expect(graph.backlinks['bbb']).toContain('aaa')
   487→  })
   488→
   489→  it('ignores self-references', () => {
   490→    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\nSee [[aaa]]')
   491→
   492→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1], tmpDir)
   493→    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa'] ?? []).toHaveLength(0)
   494→    expect(graph.backlinks['aaa'] ?? []).toHaveLength(0)
   495→  })
   496→
   497→  it('handles manifests with no clean file', () => {
   498→    const m = makeManifest('zzz', 'zzz__00000000') // no content written
   499→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m], tmpDir)
   500→    expect(graph.forwardLinks).toEqual({})
   501→    expect(graph.backlinks).toEqual({})
   502→  })
   503→
   504→  it('deduplicates links', () => {
   505→    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\n[[bbb]] [[bbb]] again')
   506→    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__00000000', '')
   507→
   508→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)
   509→    const fwd = graph.forwardLinks['aaa'] ?? []
   510→    const count = fwd.filter(x => x === 'bbb').length
   511→    expect(count).toBe(1)
   512→  })
   513→
   514→  it('matches by stem as well as session_id', () => {
   515→    // The link text is the stem of the clean file
   516→    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\nSee [[bbb__11111111]]')
   517→    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__11111111', '')
   518→
   519→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)
   520→    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa']).toContain('bbb')
   521→  })
   522→})
   523→=== C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/projects.test.ts ===
   524→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
   525→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'
   526→import { extractProject, renderProjects } from '../../../src/report/render/projects.js'
   527→import { en } from '../../../src/report/i18n/en.js'
   528→import { zhCN } from '../../../src/report/i18n/zh-CN.js'
   529→
   530→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   531→// extractProject
   532→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   533→
   534→describe('extractProject', () => {
   535→  it('returns the last path segment (Unix)', () => {
   536→    expect(extractProject('/home/user/myproject')).toBe('myproject')
   537→  })
   538→
   539→  it('returns the last path segment (Windows)', () => {
   540→    expect(extractProject('C:\\Users\\user\\myproject')).toBe('myproject')
   541→  })
   542→
   543→  it('handles trailing slash', () => {
   544→    // No trailing slash in real use, but be resilient
   545→    expect(extractProject('/home/user/myproject')).toBe('myproject')
   546→  })
   547→
   548→  it('returns "unknown" for empty string', () => {
   549→    expect(extractProject('')).toBe('unknown')
   550→  })
   551→
   552→  it('returns "unknown" for undefined-like input', () => {
   553→    expect(extractProject(undefined as unknown as string)).toBe('unknown')
   554→  })
   555→
   556→  it('handles single segment', () => {
   557→    expect(extractProject('myproject')).toBe('myproject')
   558→  })
   559→
   560→  it('handles deep path', () => {
   561→    expect(extractProject('/a/b/c/d/e')).toBe('e')
   562→  })
   563→})
   564→
   565→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   566→// Manifest fixture builder
   567→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   568→
   569→function makeManifest(overrides: Partial<ManifestEntry> = {}): ManifestEntry {
   570→  return {
   571→    client: 'claude',
   572→    project: 'test',
   573→    session_id: 'sess-001',
   574→    raw_sha256: 'abc123',
   575→    title: 'Test Session',
   576→    started_at: '2026-01-01T10:00:00Z',
   577→    imported_at: '2026-01-01T10:00:00Z',
   578→    cwd: '/home/user/myproject',
   579→    branch: 'main',
   580→    raw_source_path: '',
   581→    raw_upload_permission: 'allowed',
   582→    global_raw_path: '',
   583→    global_clean_path: '',
   584→    global_manifest_path: '',
   585→    repo_raw_path: '',
   586→    repo_clean_path: '',
   587→    repo_manifest_path: '',
   588→    message_count: 5,
   589→    tool_event_count: 2,
   590→    cleaning_mode: 'standard',
   591→    repo_mirror_enabled: false,
   592→    ...overrides,
   593→  }
   594→}
   595→
   596→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   597→// renderProjects — grouping
   598→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   599→
   600→describe('renderProjects — grouping', () => {
   601→  it('groups sessions by last cwd segment', () => {
   602→    const manifests = [
   603→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's1', cwd: '/home/user/alpha' }),
   604→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's2', cwd: '/home/user/alpha' }),
   605→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's3', cwd: '/home/user/beta' }),
   606→    ]
   607→    const html = renderProjects(manifests)
   608→    expect(html).toContain('alpha')
   609→    expect(html).toContain('beta')
   610→    // alpha appears once as a project card name
   611→    const alphaCount = (html.match(/alpha/g) ?? []).length
   612→    expect(alphaCount).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)
   613→  })
   614→
   615→  it('shows session count per project', () => {
   616→    const manifests = [
   617→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's1', cwd: '/home/user/alpha' }),
   618→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's2', cwd: '/home/user/alpha' }),
   619→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's3', cwd: '/home/user/beta' }),
   620→    ]
   621→    const html = renderProjects(manifests)
   622→    expect(html).toContain('2')
   623→    expect(html).toContain('1')
   624→  })
   625→
   626→  it('handles empty manifests list', () => {
   627→    const html = renderProjects([])
   628→    expect(html).toContain('No projects found')
   629→  })
   630→
   631→  it('handles "unknown" cwd gracefully', () => {
   632→    const manifests = [makeManifest({ cwd: '' })]
   633→    const html = renderProjects(manifests)
   634→    expect(html).toContain('unknown')
   635→  })
   636→
   637→  it('shows client badges per project', () => {
   638→    const manifests = [
   639→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's1', cwd: '/home/user/proj', client: 'claude' }),
   640→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's2', cwd: '/home/user/proj', client: 'codex' }),
   641→    ]
   642→    const html = renderProjects(manifests)
   643→    expect(html).toContain('badge-claude')
   644→    expect(html).toContain('badge-codex')
   645→  })
   646→
   647→  it('uses translations when provided (en)', () => {
   648→    const html = renderProjects([], en)
   649→    expect(html).toContain('Projects')
   650→    expect(html).toContain('No projects found')
   651→  })
   652→
   653→  it('uses translations when provided (zh-CN)', () => {
   654→    const html = renderProjects([], zhCN)
   655→    expect(html).toContain('项目')
   656→    expect(html).toContain('未找到项目')
   657→  })
   658→
   659→  it('shows last active date', () => {
   660→    const manifests = [
   661→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's1', cwd: '/home/user/alpha', started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' }),
   662→      makeManifest({ session_id: 's2', cwd: '/home/user/alpha', started_at: '2026-03-16T10:00:00Z' }),
   663→    ]
   664→    const html = renderProjects(manifests)
   665→    expect(html).toContain('2026-03-16')
   666→  })
   667→})
   668→=== C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/rss.test.ts ===
   669→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
   670→import { renderRssFeed } from '../../../src/report/render/rss.js'
   671→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'
   672→
   673→function makeManifest(overrides: Partial<ManifestEntry> = {}): ManifestEntry {
   674→  return {
   675→    client: 'claude',
   676→    project: 'test',
   677→    session_id: 'sess-001',
   678→    raw_sha256: 'abc123',
   679→    title: 'My Session',
   680→    started_at: '2026-03-16T10:00:00Z',
   681→    imported_at: '2026-03-16T10:01:00Z',
   682→    cwd: '/home/user/project',
   683→    branch: 'main',
   684→    raw_source_path: '',
   685→    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',
   686→    global_raw_path: '',
   687→    global_clean_path: '',
   688→    global_manifest_path: '',
   689→    repo_raw_path: '',
   690→    repo_clean_path: 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/claude/2026/03/sess-001.md',
   691→    repo_manifest_path: '',
   692→    message_count: 5,
   693→    tool_event_count: 2,
   694→    cleaning_mode: 'standard',
   695→    repo_mirror_enabled: true,
   696→    ...overrides,
   697→  }
   698→}
   699→
   700→describe('renderRssFeed', () => {
   701→  it('produces valid RSS 2.0 XML header', () => {
   702→    const xml = renderRssFeed([], {}, '')
   703→    expect(xml).toContain('<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>')
   704→    expect(xml).toContain('<rss version="2.0"')
   705→    expect(xml).toContain('<channel>')
   706→    expect(xml).toContain('</channel>')
   707→    expect(xml).toContain('</rss>')
   708→  })
   709→
   710→  it('omits channel link when baseUrl is empty', () => {
   711→    const xml = renderRssFeed([], {}, '')
   712→    expect(xml).not.toContain('<link>')
   713→  })
   714→
   715→  it('includes channel link when baseUrl is set', () => {
   716→    const xml = renderRssFeed([], {}, 'https://memory.example.com')
   717→    expect(xml).toContain('<link>https://memory.example.com</link>')
   718→  })
   719→
   720→  it('renders session as RSS item with title and guid', () => {
   721→    const m = makeManifest()
   722→    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, '')
   723→    expect(xml).toContain('<title>My Session</title>')
   724→    expect(xml).toContain('<guid isPermaLink="false">sess-001</guid>')
   725→  })
   726→
   727→  it('includes absolute link and permalink guid when baseUrl is set', () => {
   728→    const m = makeManifest()
   729→    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, 'https://memory.example.com')
   730→    expect(xml).toContain('<link>https://memory.example.com/transcripts/claude/')
   731→    expect(xml).toContain('isPermaLink="true"')
   732→  })
   733→
   734→  it('includes description from summaries', () => {
   735→    const m = makeManifest()
   736→    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], { 'sess-001': 'This is the summary.' }, '')
   737→    expect(xml).toContain('<description>This is the summary.</description>')
   738→  })
   739→
   740→  it('escapes XML special characters in title', () => {
   741→    const m = makeManifest({ title: 'A & B <test> "quote"' })
   742→    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, '')
   743→    expect(xml).toContain('A &amp; B &lt;test&gt; &quot;quote&quot;')
   744→    expect(xml).not.toContain('<test>')
   745→  })
   746→
   747→  it('limits output to 100 items', () => {
   748→    const manifests = Array.from({ length: 150 }, (_, i) =>
   749→      makeManifest({ session_id: `sess-${i.toString().padStart(3, '0')}`, started_at: `2026-03-${(i % 28 + 1).toString().padStart(2, '0')}T00:00:00Z` })
   750→    )
   751→    const xml = renderRssFeed(manifests, {}, '')
   752→    const itemCount = (xml.match(/<item>/g) ?? []).length
   753→    expect(itemCount).toBe(100)
   754→  })
   755→
   756→  it('includes pubDate in RFC 2822 format', () => {
   757→    const m = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-16T10:00:00Z' })
   758→    const xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, '')
   759→    expect(xml).toContain('<pubDate>')
   760→    // RFC 2822 dates contain weekday abbreviation
   761→    expect(xml).toMatch(/<pubDate>Mon,/)
   762→  })
   763→
   764→  it('handles empty manifests array', () => {
   765→    const xml = renderRssFeed([], {}, 'https://example.com')
   766→    expect(xml).toContain('<channel>')
   767→    expect(xml).not.toContain('<item>')
   768→  })
   769→})
   770→=== C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/search.test.ts ===
   771→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
   772→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'
   773→import { buildSearchIndex, renderSearchPage } from '../../../src/report/render/search.js'
   774→import type { SearchIndexEntry } from '../../../src/types/report.js'
   775→
   776→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   777→// Helpers
   778→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   779→
   780→function makeManifest(id: string, client = 'codex', repoClean = ''): ManifestEntry {
   781→  return {
   782→    client,
   783→    project: 'test',
   784→    session_id: id,
   785→    raw_sha256: id.padEnd(64, '0'),
   786→    title: `Session ${id}`,
   787→    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',
   788→    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',
   789→    cwd: '',
   790→    branch: 'main',
   791→    raw_source_path: '',
   792→    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',
   793→    global_raw_path: '',
   794→    global_clean_path: '',
   795→    global_manifest_path: '',
   796→    repo_raw_path: '',
   797→    repo_clean_path: repoClean,
   798→    repo_manifest_path: '',
   799→    message_count: 5,
   800→    tool_event_count: 2,
   801→    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
   802→    repo_mirror_enabled: true,
   803→  }
   804→}
   805→
   806→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   807→// buildSearchIndex
   808→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   809→
   810→describe('buildSearchIndex', () => {
   811→  it('builds index with correct fields', () => {
   812→    const m = makeManifest('abc', 'codex', 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/abc__deadbeef.md')
   813→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => 'some snippet')
   814→    expect(index).toHaveLength(1)
   815→    const entry = index[0]!
   816→    expect(entry.title).toBe('Session abc')
   817→    expect(entry.client).toBe('codex')
   818→    expect(entry.date).toBe('2026-03-10')
   819→    expect(entry.snippet).toBe('some snippet')
   820→  })
   821→
   822→  it('uses session_id as stem when no clean path', () => {
   823→    const m = makeManifest('myid', 'claude', '')
   824→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => '')
   825→    expect(index[0]!.url).toContain('myid')
   826→  })
   827→
   828→  it('produces correct URL format', () => {
   829→    const m = makeManifest('xyz', 'claude', 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/claude/2026/03/xyz__abc.md')
   830→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => '')
   831→    expect(index[0]!.url).toBe('transcripts/claude/xyz__abc.html')
   832→  })
   833→
   834→  it('truncates snippets to 200 chars', () => {
   835→    const longSnippet = 'x'.repeat(300)
   836→    const m = makeManifest('a')
   837→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => longSnippet)
   838→    expect(index[0]!.snippet.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(200)
   839→  })
   840→
   841→  it('respects 50kB total index budget', () => {
   842→    const manifests: ManifestEntry[] = []
   843→    for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
   844→      manifests.push(makeManifest(`session-${i}`))
   845→    }
   846→    // Give each session a large snippet
   847→    const bigSnippet = 'a'.repeat(200)
   848→    const index = buildSearchIndex(manifests, () => bigSnippet)
   849→    const json = JSON.stringify(index)
   850→    expect(json.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(51_000) // some tolerance
   851→  })
   852→
   853→  it('returns empty array for no manifests', () => {
   854→    expect(buildSearchIndex([], () => '')).toEqual([])
   855→  })
   856→})
   857→
   858→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   859→// renderSearchPage
   860→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   861→
   862→describe('renderSearchPage', () => {
   863→  it('embeds SEARCH_INDEX as inline JS', () => {
   864→    const index: SearchIndexEntry[] = [
   865→      { url: 'transcripts/codex/a.html', title: 'Test', client: 'codex', date: '2026-03-10', snippet: 'hi' },
   866→    ]
   867→    const html = renderSearchPage(index)
   868→    expect(html).toContain('const SEARCH_INDEX =')
   869→    expect(html).toContain('"transcripts/codex/a.html"')
   870→  })
   871→
   872→  it('produces valid HTML structure', () => {
   873→    const html = renderSearchPage([])
   874→    expect(html).toContain('<!DOCTYPE html>')
   875→    expect(html).toContain('<title>')
   876→    expect(html).toContain('search-input')
   877→  })
   878→
   879→  it('embeds valid JSON in the index', () => {
   880→    const index: SearchIndexEntry[] = [
   881→      { url: 'a.html', title: 'Test <session>', client: 'codex', date: '2026-03-10', snippet: 'body' },
   882→    ]
   883→    const html = renderSearchPage(index)
   884→    // Extract the JSON part
   885→    const match = html.match(/const SEARCH_INDEX = (\[[\s\S]*?\]);/)
   886→    expect(match).not.toBeNull()
   887→    expect(() => JSON.parse(match![1]!)).not.toThrow()
   888→  })
   889→
   890→  it('includes vanilla JS search logic', () => {
   891→    const html = renderSearchPage([])
   892→    expect(html).toContain('addEventListener')
   893→    expect(html).toContain('SEARCH_INDEX')
   894→    expect(html).toContain('toLowerCase')
   895→  })
   896→})
   897→=== C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/report/render/toc.test.ts ===
   898→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
   899→import { extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds, prefixTocIds } from '../../../src/report/render/toc.js'
   900→
   901→describe('extractToc', () => {
   902→  it('extracts h2 and h3 headings', () => {
   903→    const md = `# Title\n\n## Section One\n\nSome text\n\n### Sub Section\n\n## Section Two`
   904→    const toc = extractToc(md)
   905→    expect(toc).toHaveLength(3)
   906→    expect(toc[0]).toEqual({ level: 2, text: 'Section One', id: 'section-one' })
   907→    expect(toc[1]).toEqual({ level: 3, text: 'Sub Section', id: 'sub-section' })
   908→    expect(toc[2]).toEqual({ level: 2, text: 'Section Two', id: 'section-two' })
   909→  })
   910→
   911→  it('ignores h1 headings', () => {
   912→    const md = `# Top Level\n\n## Only This`
   913→    const toc = extractToc(md)
   914→    expect(toc).toHaveLength(1)
   915→    expect(toc[0]!.level).toBe(2)
   916→  })
   917→
   918→  it('handles duplicate headings with -N suffix', () => {
   919→    const md = `## Intro\n\n## Intro\n\n## Intro`
   920→    const toc = extractToc(md)
   921→    expect(toc[0]!.id).toBe('intro')
   922→    expect(toc[1]!.id).toBe('intro-1')
   923→    expect(toc[2]!.id).toBe('intro-2')
   924→  })
   925→
   926→  it('returns empty array for content with no headings', () => {
   927→    expect(extractToc('Just some text\n\nNo headings here.')).toEqual([])
   928→  })
   929→
   930→  it('returns empty array for empty string', () => {
   931→    expect(extractToc('')).toEqual([])
   932→  })
   933→
   934→  it('slugifies special characters in heading text', () => {
   935→    const md = `## Hello, World! (2026)`
   936→    const toc = extractToc(md)
   937→    expect(toc[0]!.id).toBe('hello-world-2026')
   938→  })
   939→})
   940→
   941→describe('renderToc', () => {
   942→  it('returns empty string for empty entries', () => {
   943→    expect(renderToc([])).toBe('')
   944→  })
   945→
   946→  it('renders h2 items without indent', () => {
   947→    const entries = [{ level: 2 as const, text: 'Section', id: 'section' }]
   948→    const html = renderToc(entries)
   949→    expect(html).toContain('<a href="#section">Section</a>')
   950→    expect(html).not.toContain('padding-left')
   951→  })
   952→
   953→  it('renders h3 items with indent style', () => {
   954→    const entries = [{ level: 3 as const, text: 'Sub', id: 'sub' }]
   955→    const html = renderToc(entries)
   956→    expect(html).toContain('padding-left')
   957→    expect(html).toContain('<a href="#sub">Sub</a>')
   958→  })
   959→
   960→  it('escapes HTML in heading text', () => {
   961→    const entries = [{ level: 2 as const, text: '<script>alert(1)</script>', id: 'xss' }]
   962→    const html = renderToc(entries)
   963→    expect(html).not.toContain('<script>')
   964→    expect(html).toContain('&lt;script&gt;')
   965→  })
   966→
   967→  it('wraps in nav.toc with toggle button', () => {
   968→    const entries = [{ level: 2 as const, text: 'A', id: 'a' }]
   969→    const html = renderToc(entries)
   970→    expect(html).toContain('class="toc"')
   971→    expect(html).toContain('toc-toggle')
   972→  })
   973→})
   974→
   975→describe('injectHeadingIds', () => {
   976→  it('injects id attributes into h2 and h3 tags', () => {
   977→    const html = '<h2>Section One</h2><p>text</p><h3>Sub Section</h3>'
   978→    const entries = [
   979→      { level: 2 as const, text: 'Section One', id: 'section-one' },
   980→      { level: 3 as const, text: 'Sub Section', id: 'sub-section' },
   981→    ]
   982→    const result = injectHeadingIds(html, entries)
   983→    expect(result).toContain('<h2 id="section-one">Section One</h2>')
   984→    expect(result).toContain('<h3 id="sub-section">Sub Section</h3>')
   985→  })
   986→
   987→  it('returns original html when entries is empty', () => {
   988→    const html = '<h2>Test</h2>'
   989→    expect(injectHeadingIds(html, [])).toBe(html)
   990→  })
   991→
   992→  it('leaves unmatched headings unchanged', () => {
   993→    const html = '<h2>Unknown Heading</h2>'
   994→    const entries = [{ level: 2 as const, text: 'Different', id: 'different' }]
   995→    const result = injectHeadingIds(html, entries)
   996→    expect(result).toBe('<h2>Unknown Heading</h2>')
   997→  })
   998→})
   999→
  1000→describe('prefixTocIds', () => {
  1001→  it('prefixes ids to keep anchors unique across merged documents', () => {
  1002→    const entries = [
  1003→      { level: 2 as const, text: 'Overview', id: 'overview' },
  1004→      { level: 3 as const, text: 'Details', id: 'details' },
  1005→    ]
  1006→    expect(prefixTocIds(entries, 'goal-a-md')).toEqual([
  1007→      { level: 2, text: 'Overview', id: 'goal-a-md-overview' },
  1008→      { level: 3, text: 'Details', id: 'goal-a-md-details' },
  1009→    ])
  1010→  })
  1011→
  1012→  it('returns original ids when prefix is empty', () => {
  1013→    const entries = [{ level: 2 as const, text: 'Overview', id: 'overview' }]
  1014→    expect(prefixTocIds(entries, '')).toEqual(entries)
  1015→  })
  1016→})
  1017→

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts",
  "old_string": "  if (importedCount === 0) {\n    logger.info(`[${projectName}] No new transcripts to import.`)\n    return\n  }\n\n  logger.info(`[${projectName}] Imported ${importedCount} transcript(s).`)\n  gitCommitAndPush(config, projectPath, projectName, importedCount)\n}",
  "new_string": "  if (importedCount === 0) {\n    logger.info(`[${projectName}] No new transcripts to import.`)\n  } else {\n    logger.info(`[${projectName}] Imported ${importedCount} transcript(s).`)\n    gitCommitAndPush(config, projectPath, projectName, importedCount)\n  }\n\n  if (config.generate_report) {\n    await runBuildReport(config, projectPath, projectName)\n  }\n}"
}

Now add the runBuildReport helper before the git helpers:

Tool Call #251 Edit toolu_01GnupV2UhmrDXGJ68jmj8Az 12:03:38
Edit input={"file_path":"D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts","new_string":"// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Report generati...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts",
  "old_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Sensitive pattern detection\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------",
  "new_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Report generation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync function runBuildReport(config: Config, projectPath: string, projectName: string): Promise<void> {\n  const logger = getLogger()\n  const output = join(projectPath, 'Memory', '07_reports')\n  try {\n    const { buildReport } = await import('@openmnemo/report')\n    await buildReport({\n      root: projectPath,\n      output,\n      noAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'],\n      model: config.ai_summary_model,\n      locale: config.locale,\n      ghPagesBranch: config.gh_pages_branch,\n      cname: config.cname,\n      webhookUrl: config.webhook_url,\n      reportBaseUrl: config.report_base_url,\n    })\n    logger.info(`[${projectName}] Report generated at ${output}.`)\n  } catch (err: unknown) {\n    logger.error(`[${projectName}] Report generation failed: ${String(err)}`)\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Sensitive pattern detection\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------"
}

Read output=1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest' 2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs' 3→import { join } from 'node:path' 4→import { tmpdir }...
Show output payload 
1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so any internal path helpers (logging, alerts, config, lock)
     8→// all point to a temp directory and don't pollute the real home.
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→
    11→let tmpDir: string
    12→
    13→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
    14→  const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
    15→  return {
    16→    ...actual,
    17→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    18→  }
    19→})
    20→
    21→import { scanSensitive } from '../../src/heartbeat.js'
    22→import { _resetLogger, setupLogging } from '../../src/log.js'
    23→import { readAlerts, clearAlerts } from '../../src/alert.js'
    24→import type { ParsedTranscript } from '@openmnemo/types'
    25→
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→// Setup / Teardown
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→beforeEach(() => {
    31→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'hb-test-'))
    32→  _resetLogger()
    33→  setupLogging('warn') // suppress info/debug noise during tests
    34→})
    35→
    36→afterEach(() => {
    37→  clearAlerts()
    38→  _resetLogger()
    39→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    40→})
    41→
    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→// Helpers
    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→
    46→function makeTranscript(texts: string[]): ParsedTranscript {
    47→  return {
    48→    client: 'claude',
    49→    session_id: 'test-session',
    50→    title: 'Test',
    51→    started_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z',
    52→    cwd: '/tmp/project',
    53→    branch: 'main',
    54→    messages: texts.map(text => ({ role: 'user', text, timestamp: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' })),
    55→    tool_events: [],
    56→    source_path: '/tmp/transcript.json',
    57→  }
    58→}
    59→
    60→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    61→// scanSensitive — detection
    62→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    63→
    64→describe('scanSensitive', () => {
    65→  it('detects API key pattern: api_key = sk-1234567890', () => {
    66→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['Set api_key = sk-1234567890abcdef'])
    67→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
    68→
    69→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    70→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    71→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
    72→  })
    73→
    74→  it('detects password pattern: password = secret123', () => {
    75→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['password = secret123'])
    76→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
    77→
    78→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    79→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    80→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
    81→  })
    82→
    83→  it('detects token pattern: token = abc123xyz', () => {
    84→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['secret = my-secret-value'])
    85→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
    86→
    87→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    88→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    89→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
    90→  })
    91→
    92→  it('detects known secret prefixes: sk-xxxx, ghp_xxxx', () => {
    93→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['Use sk-abcdef1234567890extra'])
    94→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
    95→
    96→    const alerts = readAlerts()
    97→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
    98→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
    99→  })
   100→
   101→  it('detects GitHub PAT prefix: ghp_xxxx', () => {
   102→    clearAlerts()
   103→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['Token ghp_abcdef1234567890extraextra'])
   104→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   105→
   106→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   107→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   108→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  it('detects Bearer token pattern', () => {
   112→    clearAlerts()
   113→    const parsed = makeTranscript(['Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.abcdefghij'])
   114→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   115→
   116→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   117→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   118→    expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
   119→  })
   120→
   121→  it('does not flag normal text without sensitive patterns', () => {
   122→    const parsed = makeTranscript([
   123→      'This is a normal conversation about code.',
   124→      'We discussed the API design for the project.',
   125→      'The function returns a list of tokens from the parser.',
   126→    ])
   127→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   128→
   129→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   130→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(0)
   131→  })
   132→
   133→  it('stops after first match (returns early)', () => {
   134→    const parsed = makeTranscript([
   135→      'api_key = first-key',
   136→      'password = second-secret',
   137→    ])
   138→    scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   139→
   140→    // Should produce exactly one alert (returns after first sensitive match)
   141→    const alerts = readAlerts()
   142→    expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   143→  })
   144→})
   145→
   146→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   147→// SENSITIVE_PATTERNS — individual regex verification
   148→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   149→
   150→describe('SENSITIVE_PATTERNS individual patterns', () => {
   151→  it('api_key pattern matches various forms', () => {
   152→    const apiKeyPattern = /(?:api[_-]?key|apikey)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
   153→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('api_key = abc123')).toBe(true)
   154→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('api-key: abc123')).toBe(true)
   155→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('apikey=abc123')).toBe(true)
   156→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('APIKEY = ABC')).toBe(true)
   157→    expect(apiKeyPattern.test('no match here')).toBe(false)
   158→  })
   159→
   160→  it('password pattern matches password/passwd/pwd', () => {
   161→    const pwdPattern = /(?:password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
   162→    expect(pwdPattern.test('password = secret')).toBe(true)
   163→    expect(pwdPattern.test('passwd: hidden')).toBe(true)
   164→    expect(pwdPattern.test('PWD=test123')).toBe(true)
   165→    expect(pwdPattern.test('no match')).toBe(false)
   166→  })
   167→
   168→  it('secret/token pattern', () => {
   169→    const secretPattern = /(?:secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
   170→    expect(secretPattern.test('secret = mysecret')).toBe(true)
   171→    expect(secretPattern.test('TOKEN: mytoken')).toBe(true)
   172→    expect(secretPattern.test('no match')).toBe(false)
   173→  })
   174→
   175→  it('known prefix pattern matches sk-, ghp_, glpat-, etc.', () => {
   176→    const prefixPattern = /(?:sk-|pk_live_|sk_live_|ghp_|gho_|glpat-)\S{10,}/
   177→    expect(prefixPattern.test('sk-abcdefghij1234567890')).toBe(true)
   178→    expect(prefixPattern.test('ghp_1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
   179→    expect(prefixPattern.test('pk_live_xxxxxxxxxx')).toBe(true)
   180→    expect(prefixPattern.test('sk_live_1234567890')).toBe(true)
   181→    expect(prefixPattern.test('gho_1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
   182→    expect(prefixPattern.test('glpat-1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
   183→    expect(prefixPattern.test('sk-short')).toBe(false) // too short
   184→    expect(prefixPattern.test('normal text')).toBe(false)
   185→  })
   186→
   187→  it('Bearer token pattern matches long bearer strings', () => {
   188→    const bearerPattern = /Bearer\s+\S{20,}/i
   189→    expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9abc')).toBe(true)
   190→    expect(bearerPattern.test('bearer AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA')).toBe(true)
   191→    expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer short')).toBe(false)
   192→    expect(bearerPattern.test('no bearer here')).toBe(false)
   193→  })
   194→})
   195→

  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/tests/heartbeat/heartbeat.test.ts",
  "old_string": "  it('Bearer token pattern matches long bearer strings', () => {\n    const bearerPattern = /Bearer\\s+\\S{20,}/i\n    expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9abc')).toBe(true)\n    expect(bearerPattern.test('bearer AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA')).toBe(true)\n    expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer short')).toBe(false)\n    expect(bearerPattern.test('no bearer here')).toBe(false)\n  })\n})",
  "new_string": "  it('Bearer token pattern matches long bearer strings', () => {\n    const bearerPattern = /Bearer\\s+\\S{20,}/i\n    expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9abc')).toBe(true)\n    expect(bearerPattern.test('bearer AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA')).toBe(true)\n    expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer short')).toBe(false)\n    expect(bearerPattern.test('no bearer here')).toBe(false)\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// processProject — generate_report integration\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('processProject — generate_report', () => {\n  const buildReportMock = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)\n\n  vi.mock('@openmnemo/report', () => ({\n    buildReport: buildReportMock,\n  }))\n\n  function makeConfig(generate_report: boolean) {\n    return {\n      heartbeat_interval: '5m',\n      watch_dirs: [],\n      projects: [],\n      auto_push: false,\n      log_level: 'warn',\n      generate_report,\n      locale: 'en',\n      ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n      gh_pages_branch: '',\n      cname: '',\n      webhook_url: '',\n      report_base_url: '',\n    }\n  }\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    buildReportMock.mockClear()\n  })\n\n  it('calls buildReport when generate_report=true', async () => {\n    const projectPath = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'proj-'))\n    mkdirSync(join(projectPath, 'Memory'), { recursive: true })\n    try {\n      await processProject(makeConfig(true), projectPath, 'test-project')\n      expect(buildReportMock).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()\n      const opts = buildReportMock.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as { root: string; output: string }\n      expect(opts.root).toBe(projectPath)\n      expect(opts.output).toContain('07_reports')\n    } finally {\n      rmSync(projectPath, { recursive: true, force: true })\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('does not call buildReport when generate_report=false', async () => {\n    const projectPath = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'proj-'))\n    mkdirSync(join(projectPath, 'Memory'), { recursive: true })\n    try {\n      await processProject(makeConfig(false), projectPath, 'test-project')\n      expect(buildReportMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled()\n    } finally {\n      rmSync(projectPath, { recursive: true, force: true })\n    }\n  })\n})"
}

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: - **Primary**: Implement ...

Expand full message

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
- Primary: Implement the full porting plan for packages/report/ to the openmnemo monorepo, consisting of 5 steps: update types, update sync config, create report package, update CLI, build/verify.
- Secondary: Run sync config tests to confirm no regression.
- Tertiary: Confirm and delete the chat-memo_61_20260316154801 staging directory (already-imported doubao transcripts).
- Latest (two-part sequential task):
1. Port skill's tests/report/* test suite to packages/report/tests/
2. Integrate buildReport into heartbeat.ts so that when generate_report: true, each heartbeat automatically generates the report after processing transcripts.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- pnpm workspaces + turbo monorepo build system (turbo.json with dependsOn: ["^build"])
- NodeNext ESM TypeScript with .js extensions in all imports, strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess + exactOptionalPropertyTypes
- tsup for building packages (--format esm --dts)
- vitest for testing
- Dynamic import (await import('@openmnemo/report')) in heartbeat.ts to keep the dependency optional and avoid circular import issues
- @openmnemo/report — new standalone package exporting buildReport and BuildReportOptions
- Import depth arithmetic: tests/render/ is 2 levels from src/ (not 3), so paths must be ../../src/ not ../../../src/
- workspace:* dependency protocol for cross-package refs in pnpm monorepo
- Turbo topology: types → core → report (parallel with core) → sync → cli

3. Files and Code Sections:

- packages/types/src/index.ts — Added 'doubao' to Client union:

     export type Client = 'codex' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'doubao'

- packages/sync/src/config.ts — Added 7 fields to Config interface and defaultConfig()/parseRaw():

     readonly generate_report: boolean     // default false
     readonly locale: string               // default 'en'
     readonly ai_summary_model: string     // default 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'
     readonly gh_pages_branch: string      // default ''
     readonly cname: string                // default ''
     readonly webhook_url: string          // default ''
     readonly report_base_url: string      // default ''

- packages/sync/package.json — Added "@openmnemo/report": "workspace:*" to dependencies.

- packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts — Key changes:
- Added join to path import
- Restructured processProject() to remove early return on importedCount === 0, instead conditionally calling git commit only when imports happened, then always checking config.generate_report
- Added runBuildReport() helper using dynamic import:

     async function runBuildReport(config: Config, projectPath: string, projectName: string): Promise<void> {
       const logger = getLogger()
       const output = join(projectPath, 'Memory', '07_reports')
       try {
         const { buildReport } = await import('@openmnemo/report')
         await buildReport({
           root: projectPath,
           output,
           noAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'],
           model: config.ai_summary_model,
           locale: config.locale,
           ghPagesBranch: config.gh_pages_branch,
           cname: config.cname,
           webhookUrl: config.webhook_url,
           reportBaseUrl: config.report_base_url,
         })
         logger.info(`[${projectName}] Report generated at ${output}.`)
       } catch (err: unknown) {
         logger.error(`[${projectName}] Report generation failed: ${String(err)}`)
       }
     }

- New processProject() tail:

     if (importedCount === 0) {
       logger.info(`[${projectName}] No new transcripts to import.`)
     } else {
       logger.info(`[${projectName}] Imported ${importedCount} transcript(s).`)
       gitCommitAndPush(config, projectPath, projectName, importedCount)
     }
     if (config.generate_report) {
       await runBuildReport(config, projectPath, projectName)
     }

- packages/sync/tests/heartbeat/heartbeat.test.ts — Added generate_report integration tests:
- Added import { mkdirSync } and processProject import
- Added vi.mock('@openmnemo/report', ...) block
- Two new tests: "calls buildReport when generate_report=true" and "does not call buildReport when generate_report=false"

- packages/report/package.json — New package, key fields:

     {
       "name": "@openmnemo/report",
       "version": "0.1.0",
       "type": "module",
       "main": "dist/index.js",
       "scripts": { "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts", "test": "vitest run", "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit" },
       "dependencies": { "@openmnemo/types": "workspace:*", "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.39.0" },
       "devDependencies": { "@types/node": "^25.5.0", "tsup": "^8.4.0", "typescript": "^5.7.3", "vitest": "^3.1.1" }
     }

- packages/report/tsconfig.json — NodeNext, strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes.

- packages/report/vitest.config.ts — Created:

     import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'
     export default defineConfig({
       test: { globals: true, include: ['tests/**/*.test.ts'] },
     })

- packages/report/src/ — 39 source files created:
- index.ts — exports buildReport and BuildReportOptions
- log.ts — minimal logger (no external deps), exports getLogger() and Logger
- types.ts — re-exports ManifestEntry from @openmnemo/types, defines ReportStats, SummaryCache, LinkGraph, SearchIndexEntry, BuildReportOptions
- build.ts — main orchestrator; imports from ./types.js, @openmnemo/types, ./log.js
- stats.ts, summarize.ts, tags.ts — ported with updated imports
- i18n/ — 4 files (types.ts, index.ts, en.ts, zh-CN.ts), no import changes needed
- render/ — 18 files; ../../types/transcript.js → @openmnemo/types; ../../types/report.js → ../types.js; KNOWN_CLIENTS in layout.ts updated to include 'doubao'
- deploy/github-pages.ts, deploy/webhook.ts — ../../heartbeat/log.js → ../log.js

- packages/cli/src/cmd-report.ts — New file; imports buildReport from @openmnemo/report (dynamic import)

- packages/cli/src/main.ts — Added report parent command with build and serve subcommands before program.parse()

- packages/cli/package.json — Added "@openmnemo/report": "workspace:*" dependency

- packages/report/tests/ — 15 test files ported by background Agent:
- tests/build.test.ts, tests/stats.test.ts, tests/summarize.test.ts, tests/tags.test.ts, tests/i18n.test.ts
- tests/render/ — 8 files (breadcrumb, charts, graph, links, projects, rss, search, toc)
- tests/deploy/ — 2 files (github-pages, webhook)

4. Errors and fixes:
- Wrong import depth in render/deploy test files: Agent used ../../../src/ (3 levels up) in tests/render/.test.ts and tests/deploy/.test.ts, but from packages/report/tests/render/, the source is only ../../src/ (2 levels up). This caused "Cannot find module" errors for all 10 files under tests/render/ and tests/deploy/.
- Status: Identified, grep confirmed 10 affected files, fix (replacing ../../../src/ → ../../src/) was in progress when summary was requested.
- The fix is: sed -i "s|from '\.\./\.\./\.\./src/|from '../../src/|g" on all 10 files, or use Edit tool per-file.

5. Problem Solving:
- Solved: Full 5-step plan execution — build clean, typecheck clean, 391 tests passing, report build --no-ai produces correct output with all directories including doubao/.
- Solved: Import path mapping from skill's nested structure to flat monorepo package structure.
- Solved: saveConfig() didn't need updating for new fields (only defaultConfig() and parseRaw() needed changes); existing sync tests all passed.
- Ongoing: The ../../../src/ → ../../src/ fix in 10 test files under tests/render/ and tests/deploy/.

6. All user messages:
- "Implement the following plan: [full plan text with 5 steps and file mappings]" (initial request)
- "请继续" (please continue — after plan implementation was complete, triggering build/verify step)
- "跑一下现有的 sync config 测试，确认没有回归" (run existing sync config tests, confirm no regression)
- "我们的计划进行到什么阶段了呢" (what stage is the plan at?)
- "d:\demo1\openmnemo\chat-memo_61_20260316154801这些内容已经导入了吗" (have these been imported?)
- "我删除可以吗" (can I delete it?)
- "好的 请删除" (please delete it)
- "下一步候选：1. 移植 skill 的 report 测试… 2. heartbeat.ts 接入… 请依次执行" (execute both next steps in order)

7. Pending Tasks:
- Fix import depth in test files: In all 10 files under packages/report/tests/render/ and packages/report/tests/deploy/, replace from '../../../src/ with from '../../src/. The 10 affected files are:
- tests/render/breadcrumb.test.ts, charts.test.ts, graph.test.ts, links.test.ts, projects.test.ts, rss.test.ts, search.test.ts, toc.test.ts
- tests/deploy/github-pages.test.ts, webhook.test.ts
- After fixing, run pnpm test to confirm all tests pass (target: 391 existing + new report tests).
- Commit all changes (heartbeat integration + test suite).

8. Current Work:
The last action before the summary was identifying the import depth error in the ported test files. The pnpm test run failed with errors like:

   Error: Cannot find module '../../../src/render/links.js' imported from 'D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/tests/render/links.test.ts'

The grep confirmed 10 files need the fix:

   tests/render/breadcrumb.test.ts
   tests/render/charts.test.ts
   tests/render/graph.test.ts
   tests/render/links.test.ts
   tests/render/projects.test.ts
   tests/render/rss.test.ts
   tests/render/search.test.ts
   tests/render/toc.test.ts
   tests/deploy/github-pages.test.ts
   tests/deploy/webhook.test.ts

The fix is to replace from '../../../src/ with from '../../src/ in all 10 files.

9. Optional Next Step:
Fix the import depth error in the 10 affected test files. The conversation ended mid-fix with the grep result:

   cd D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report && grep -rl "from '\.\./\.\./\.\./src/" tests/render/ tests/deploy/ 2>/dev/null

The next step is to run a batch replacement on all 10 files:

   sed -i "s|from '\.\./\.\./\.\./src/|from '../../src/|g" \
     packages/report/tests/render/breadcrumb.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/render/charts.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/render/graph.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/render/links.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/render/projects.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/render/rss.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/render/search.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/render/toc.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/deploy/github-pages.test.ts \
     packages/report/tests/deploy/webhook.test.ts

Then re-run pnpm test to confirm all tests pass.

196→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   197→// processProject — generate_report integration
   198→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   199→
   200→describe('processProject — generate_report', () => {
   201→  const buildReportMock = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)
   202→
   203→  vi.mock('@openmnemo/report', () => ({
   204→    buildReport: buildReportMock,
   205→  }))
   206→
   207→  function makeConfig(generate_report: boolean) {
   208→    return {
   209→      heartbeat_interval: '5m',
   210→      watch_dirs: [],
   211→      projects: [],
   212→      auto_push: false,
   213→      log_level: 'warn',
   214→      generate_report,
   215→      locale: 'en',
   216→      ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   217→      gh_pages_branch: '',
   218→      cname: '',
   219→      webhook_url: '',
   220→      report_base_url: '',
   221→    }
   222→  }
   223→
   224→  beforeEach(() => {
   225→    buildReportMock.mockClear()
   226→  })
   227→
   228→  it('calls buildReport when generate_report=true', async () => {
   229→    const projectPath = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'proj-'))
   230→    mkdirSync(join(projectPath, 'Memory'), { recursive: true })
   231→    try {
   232→      await processProject(makeConfig(true), projectPath, 'test-project')
   233→      expect(buildReportMock).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
   234→      const opts = buildReportMock.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as { root: string; output: string }
   235→      expect(opts.root).toBe(projectPath)
   236→      expect(opts.output).toContain('07_reports')
   237→    } finally {
   238→      rmSync(projectPath, { recursive: true, force: true })
   239→    }
   240→  })
   241→
   242→  it('does not call buildReport when generate_report=false', async () => {
   243→    const projectPath = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'proj-'))
   244→    mkdirSync(join(projectPath, 'Memory'), { recursive: true })
   245→    try {
   246→      await processProject(makeConfig(false), projectPath, 'test-project')
   247→      expect(buildReportMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   248→    } finally {
   249→      rmSync(projectPath, { recursive: true, force: true })
   250→    }
   251→  })
   252→})
   253→
assistant #310 12:14:31

Classic vitest hoisting issue — vi.mock is hoisted before const buildReportMock, so the closure captures undefined. Fix with vi.hoisted() at file top level:

Tool Call #311 Edit toolu_011PkhSDPuJFU3BJ3538vDZX 12:14:33
Edit input={"file_path":"D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/tests/heartbeat/heartbeat.test.ts","new_string":"// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n/...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/tests/heartbeat/heartbeat.test.ts",
  "old_string": "import { scanSensitive, processProject } from '../../src/heartbeat.js'",
  "new_string": "// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Hoisted mocks (must be at top level so vi.mock factory captures them)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst buildReportMock = vi.hoisted(() => vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined))\n\nvi.mock('@openmnemo/report', () => ({\n  buildReport: buildReportMock,\n}))\n\nimport { scanSensitive, processProject } from '../../src/heartbeat.js'"
}
Tool Result #312 Edit toolu_011PkhSDPuJFU3BJ3538vDZX 12:14:33

Show output payload

assistant #332 12:16:18

Done. Summary of what was fixed and committed:

Root cause of failures: Two import depth bugs in the ported tests:
1. tests/render/.test.ts and tests/deploy/.test.ts (2 levels deep) had ../../../src/ — should be ../../src/
2. tests/stats.test.ts, tests/summarize.test.ts, tests/tags.test.ts, tests/build.test.ts, tests/i18n.test.ts (1 level deep) had ../../src/ — should be ../src/
3. vi.mock('@openmnemo/report') inside a describe block with a const buildReportMock in the same block — vi.mock is hoisted before the const, so the factory captured undefined. Fixed with vi.hoisted() at file top level.

Final test counts: 589 total — core: 223, report: 196, sync: 71, cli: 99. All green.

The completed commit 47e402d already contains everything correctly. The background agent's notification is just stale — it was launched before our fixes and its output was superseded by our work.

}
