Agent output=[{"text":"I have now read all 18 files in full. Here is the complete review.\n\n---\n\n## Security and Architecture Review\n\n---\n\n### SECURITY\n\n**S-1 — `heartbeat.ts:29`: O...
Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "I have now read all 18 files in full. Here is the complete review.\n\n---\n\n## Security and Architecture Review\n\n---\n\n### SECURITY\n\n**S-1 — `heartbeat.ts:29`: Output path constructed without traversal validation**\n\n```ts\nconst output = join(projectPath, 'Memory', '07_reports')\n```\n\n`projectPath` arrives from `config.projects[].path` which is user-controlled (config.toml). `resolve(entry.path)` normalises the path at line 98 of `runHeartbeat`, but there is no assertion that the resolved path is still a descendent of a trusted root. An entry like `path = \"/etc\"` resolves cleanly, and `buildReport` would then write files under `/etc/Memory/07_reports`. This is a low-severity file-write issue but is still a path-boundary violation.\n\n**S-2 — `heartbeat.ts:35`: Presence-check instead of value check on `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`**\n\n```ts\nnoAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'],\n```\n\nAny non-empty value—including `\"false\"`, `\"0\"`, or a whitespace string—enables AI calls. The check should be `!(process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']?.trim())`.\n\n**S-3 — `summarize.test.ts:135`: Real-looking secret in test body**\n\n```ts\nprocess.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = 'sk-test-key'\n```\n\n`sk-test-key` superficially matches the `sk-` prefix checked by `SENSITIVE_PATTERNS` in `heartbeat.ts`. Any secret-scanning tool running on the repo (e.g. `gitleaks`, `truffleHog`) may flag this as a leaked key. Use a clearly synthetic value such as `'test-api-key-not-real'` or `'sk_dummy_no_real_secret'`.\n\n**S-4 — `tags.test.ts:96`: Same secret-scanner false positive**\n\n```ts\nprocess.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = 'fake-key'\n```\n\nLower risk than S-3 (no `sk-` prefix), but the test infrastructure in `heartbeat.ts` would not flag this. Still, per the project's own sensitive-pattern standard, any key/token value in source should be clearly non-credentialised.\n\n**S-5 — `webhook.ts:131`: IPv6 link-local regex is incomplete**\n\n```ts\nif (/^fe[89ab]/i.test(h)) return true  // fe80::/10 link-local\n```\n\n`fe80::/10` covers `fe80`–`febf`. The regex `^fe[89ab]` matches `fe80`–`febf` in the second hex nibble. However, it also matches `fe90`, `fea0`, `feb0` which are all within the correct range, so the intent is correct. What is missing is `fe8`, `fe9`, `fea`, `feb` with subsequent digits. The current regex matches exactly one hex digit after `fe`, meaning it does not match e.g. `fe8::1` (which would be written `fe80::1` in practice). This is fine in practice because the Node.js `URL` parser always expands the canonical form, but the regex comment says `fe80::/10` while it actually accepts anything starting with `fe[89ab]`, which includes `fe90::/10` and beyond. This is not a bug but the comment is misleading and the code relies on Node.js URL normalisation to be safe.\n\n**S-6 — `github-pages.ts:84`: git config values are not quoted**\n\n```ts\ngit(publishRoot, '-c', `user.name=${COMMITTER_NAME}`, '-c', `user.email=${COMMITTER_EMAIL}`, ...)\n```\n\nBecause `execFileSync` is used (not a shell), argument injection is not possible here and this is safe. However, if `COMMITTER_NAME` ever contains `=` it could be mis-parsed by git. Since these are constants this is theoretical only.\n\n**S-7 — `github-pages.ts:90`: Branch name injected into push refspec via template literal**\n\n```ts\ngit(publishRoot, 'push', 'origin', `HEAD:${branch}`)\n```\n\n`BRANCH_RE` already validates the branch at line 36, so no injection is possible. This is correct. No issue.\n\n---\n\n### TYPE SAFETY\n\n**T-1 — `heartbeat.test.ts:238`: Unsafe cast with no narrowing**\n\n```ts\nconst opts = buildReportMock.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as { root: string; output: string }\n```\n\nThis silently succeeds even if the argument shape changes. Because `buildReportMock` is `vi.fn()`, the type of `calls[0][0]` is `unknown`. The cast should be preceded by at minimum a `expect(opts).toMatchObject({...})` or use a proper type guard. As written, if `buildReport` is called with no arguments `opts` would be `undefined` and the subsequent `.root` and `.output` assertions would throw an unhelpful error.\n\n**T-2 — `webhook.test.ts:42`: `opts: unknown` cast chain**\n\n```ts\nmockHttpsRequest.mockImplementation((opts: unknown, cb: RequestCallback) => {\n```\n\n`cb` is typed as `RequestCallback` which is `(res: Partial<IncomingMessage>) => void`. The mock calls `cb({ statusCode, resume: vi.fn() })`, passing `resume` as a `vi.fn()`. `IncomingMessage.resume` is `() => this` (returns `this`). While this works at runtime, the mock silently satisfies a looser type than the real interface. The `Partial<IncomingMessage>` type allows this, so there is no compile error—but it means the test can never catch a production bug where `resume` is expected to return the stream.\n\n**T-3 — `github-pages.test.ts:162-163`: Double `as unknown` cast on execFileSync arguments**\n\n```ts\nconst args = [...(rawArgs as string[])]\nconst cwd = (rawOptions as { cwd?: string } | undefined)?.cwd\n```\n\n`rawArgs` and `rawOptions` are `unknown` (from the mock implementation signature). The casts are necessary here given the Vitest `mockImplementation` signature, but they suppress TypeScript's ability to catch changes in how the real `execFileSync` is called. A type predicate or `z.parse` would be safer; this is an acceptable test-code compromise but should be noted.\n\n**T-4 — `summarize.ts:100`: `as NodeJS.ErrnoException` cast on arbitrary caught value**\n\n```ts\nconst code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code\n```\n\n`err` is `unknown`. The cast is applied before checking that `err` is an object with a `code` property. If `err` is a string or null this will silently give `undefined` for `code` (not throw), so it doesn't crash, but it is technically incorrect. The safe pattern is `err instanceof Error && 'code' in err`.\n\n**T-5 — `i18n.test.ts:11-59`: `hasAllKeys` is a structural list, not exhaustive**\n\n```ts\nfunction hasAllKeys(obj: Translations): boolean {\n  return typeof obj.nav.dashboard === 'string' && ...\n}\n```\n\nThe function hard-codes a list of keys that existed at time of writing. When new i18n keys are added to `Translations`, `hasAllKeys` will continue to return `true` without checking the new keys. A compile-time exhaustive check via `Record<keyof Translations, unknown>` destructuring or `Object.keys(en).every(k => k in obj)` per sub-namespace would be more robust.\n\n---\n\n### ARCHITECTURE\n\n**A-1 — `heartbeat.ts:160-162`: Report generation not awaited before `processProject` returns in error-tolerant path**\n\n```ts\nif (config.generate_report) {\n  await runBuildReport(config, projectPath, projectName)\n}\n```\n\nThis is correctly `await`-ed. However, `runBuildReport` swallows all errors internally (try/catch with only a `logger.error`). If the build report throws and is silenced, the calling code cannot distinguish success from failure, and there is no alert written to the alert file (unlike the push-failed path which calls `writeAlertWithThreshold`). The report failure is invisible to monitoring.\n\n**A-2 — `heartbeat.ts:157`: `gitCommitAndPush` is called without `await` — fire-and-forget on synchronous code**\n\n```ts\ngitCommitAndPush(config, projectPath, projectName, importedCount)\n```\n\n`gitCommitAndPush` is synchronous (returns `void`, not `Promise<void>`). This is intentional since `git()` calls `execFileSync`. It is architecturally inconsistent that push is synchronous while `runBuildReport` is async, meaning a very slow git push blocks the event loop. For a heartbeat daemon this is acceptable but worth flagging.\n\n**A-3 — `summarize.ts:33-43`: Module-level mutable state in semaphore**\n\n```ts\nlet activeCalls = 0\n```\n\n`activeCalls` is module-level mutable state. In the test environment, if `summarize.ts` is imported across multiple test files that run in the same worker, this counter is shared. The tests do not reset it. If a test were to interrupt a semaphore hold (e.g., via `vi.useFakeTimers()`), `activeCalls` could be leaked. This is a hidden coupling between the module singleton and the test harness. `summarize.test.ts` avoids this entirely by setting `noAi: true` or having no API key, so the semaphore is never entered — but the risk exists for future tests that mock the API call.\n\n**A-4 — `tags.test.ts:10-14`: `makeTmpDir` uses `Math.random()` instead of `mkdtempSync`**\n\n```ts\nfunction makeTmpDir(): string {\n  const dir = join(os.tmpdir(), `mt-tags-test-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`)\n  mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })\n  return dir\n}\n```\n\nAll other test files use `mkdtempSync` (the correct POSIX atomic-create primitive). `Math.random()` has a ~1 in 2 billion collision probability and a very small but non-zero race condition window. More importantly, these temp directories are **never cleaned up** — there is no `afterEach` that calls `rmSync`. This pollutes the OS temp directory with one directory per test run of the `getTags` suite.\n\n**A-5 — `vitest.config.ts` (report): No `coverage` section, no `environment` set**\n\nBoth `packages/report/vitest.config.ts` and `packages/sync/vitest.config.ts` define `globals: true` but have no `environment`, `coverage`, or `reporters` configuration. The `globals: true` flag makes `describe`/`it`/`expect` available without imports, but the test files already import them explicitly from `vitest`. The `globals: true` setting is therefore redundant and misleading — it creates an implicit global namespace that is never used, which could confuse contributors.\n\n**A-6 — `github-pages.test.ts:77-100`: Temp directory for the publish step is created inside `deployGithubPages` but the test's `tmpDir` is the repo root**\n\nThe test verifies that `git init` is called without a `cwd` argument matching `tmpDir`. The `publishRoot` (the temp dir created inside the implementation for the intermediate git repo) is not observable from the test. The test checks `hasCommand(calls, ['init'])` without a `cwd` filter, so the assertion passes as long as any `git init` is called, regardless of where. This means the test would pass even if `git init` were called in the wrong directory.\n\n---\n\n### TEST QUALITY\n\n**Q-1 — `heartbeat.test.ts:93`: Test name does not match test body**\n\n```ts\nit('detects token pattern: token = abc123xyz', () => {\n  const parsed = makeTranscript(['secret = my-secret-value'])\n  // ...\n})\n```\n\nThe test is named \"detects token pattern: token = abc123xyz\" but the actual input is `'secret = my-secret-value'`. This is a copy-paste naming error. The intent is correct (the `secret|token` regex matches `secret =`), but the misleading name will confuse future maintainers.\n\n**Q-2 — `heartbeat.test.ts:111-118`: `clearAlerts()` called in `beforeEach` already clears state, redundant calls in individual tests**\n\nTests at lines 111 and 121 each call `clearAlerts()` at their start. The `beforeEach`/`afterEach` pair at lines 40-50 already calls `clearAlerts()`. The redundant `clearAlerts()` calls suggest these tests were written defensively due to uncertainty about the test isolation model, which indicates the teardown logic may not be clearly understood.\n\n**Q-3 — `charts.test.ts:133-135`: `rectCount` assertion does not verify correct content**\n\n```ts\nconst rectCount = (svg.match(/<rect/g) ?? []).length\nexpect(rectCount).toBeLessThanOrEqual(10)\n```\n\nThis checks that there are at most 10 `<rect>` elements, but `<rect>` elements are also used in the heatmap and other chart primitives. A false-negative is likely: if the tool bar renders 10 data bars plus 1 background rect or legend rect, the test would fail even though the \"top 10\" limit is correctly enforced. The assertion should count `<rect class=\"bar\"` or an equivalent semantic selector.\n\n**Q-4 — `search.test.ts:71-81`: Budget test is tautologically weak**\n\n```ts\nconst bigSnippet = 'a'.repeat(200)\nconst index = buildSearchIndex(manifests, () => bigSnippet)\nconst json = JSON.stringify(index)\nexpect(json.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(51_000)\n```\n\nWith 100 sessions and 200-char snippets, each entry is roughly `200 + ~100` (metadata) = ~300 bytes, totalling ~30kB, well below the 50kB budget. The test would pass even if the budget guard were removed entirely. To be meaningful the test should use enough sessions with large enough snippets that the budget is actually hit, and then verify that fewer than all sessions appear in the index.\n\n**Q-5 — `rss.test.ts:88-94`: Date-dependent assertion**\n\n```ts\nconst m = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-16T10:00:00Z' })\nconst xml = renderRssFeed([m], {}, '')\nexpect(xml).toMatch(/<pubDate>Mon,/)\n```\n\nThis asserts that `2026-03-16` is a Monday. If the `toRfc2822` implementation ever changes its timezone handling (UTC vs. local time), the day name could shift. This is brittle. The test should either verify the format with a general regex (`/<pubDate>\\w{3},/`) and separately verify the correct date string, or mock the `Date` constructor.\n\n**Q-6 — `build.test.ts:196`: SVG assertion is too coarse**\n\n```ts\nexpect(dashHtml).toContain('<svg')\n```\n\nThis passes if any `<svg` element appears anywhere in the HTML including in an inline script or in an attribute value. It provides no guarantee that charts are correctly embedded. It should check for `<svg viewBox=` or a specific chart `id=` attribute.\n\n**Q-7 — `graph.test.ts:148-156`: XSS injection test is a true-positive but incomplete**\n\n```ts\nconst manifests = [makeManifest({ session_id: 's1', title: 'foo</script>bar' })]\nconst html = renderGraph(manifests, [], emptyLinkGraph())\nexpect(html).not.toContain('foo</script>bar')\n```\n\nThe test verifies that the raw `</script>` is not present, but does not verify that the escaped form (`foo<\\/script>bar`) is present. If the title were dropped entirely (sanitised by removal rather than escaping), the test would still pass. Add `expect(html).toContain('foo<\\\\/script>bar')` or check that `unique-sess-id` still appears after escaping.\n\n**Q-8 — `webhook.test.ts:196-201`: Network error test has no assertion on warning log**\n\n```ts\nit('resolves even when HTTP request triggers error event', async () => {\n  mockNetworkError()\n  await expect(sendWebhook(...)).resolves.not.toThrow()\n})\n```\n\nThe test verifies the promise resolves but does not assert that `logger.warn` was called. The contract \"never throws but always logs\" is only half-tested. Add `expect(logger.warn).toHaveBeenCalled()`.\n\n**Q-9 — `github-pages.test.ts:132-145`: \"does not throw\" test passes even if `deployGithubPages` silently no-ops**\n\n```ts\nvi.mocked(execFileSync).mockImplementation(() => { throw new Error('git failure') })\nawait expect(deployGithubPages({...})).resolves.not.toThrow()\n```\n\nBecause `deployGithubPages` wraps everything in `try/catch` and the branch validation runs first (`BRANCH_RE.test('gh-pages')` passes), the function will reach the git call, catch the error, log it, and resolve. The test verifies resolve-without-throw, which would also pass if the function returned immediately without ever calling git. Add a check that `logger.warn` was called with a string containing `'Deploy failed'`.\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| ID | Category | File | Line | Severity |\n|----|----------|------|------|----------|\n| S-1 | Security | `heartbeat.ts` | 29 | Medium |\n| S-2 | Security | `heartbeat.ts` | 35 | Low |\n| S-3 | Security | `summarize.test.ts` | 135 | Low |\n| S-4 | Security | `tags.test.ts` | 96 | Low |\n| T-1 | Type Safety | `heartbeat.test.ts` | 238 | Medium |\n| T-2 | Type Safety | `webhook.test.ts` | 42 | Low |\n| T-3 | Type Safety | `github-pages.test.ts` | 162 | Low |\n| T-4 | Type Safety | `summarize.ts` | 100 | Low |\n| T-5 | Type Safety | `i18n.test.ts` | 11 | Medium |\n| A-1 | Architecture | `heartbeat.ts` | 162 | Medium |\n| A-2 | Architecture | `heartbeat.ts` | 157 | Low |\n| A-3 | Architecture | `summarize.ts` | 33 | Low |\n| A-4 | Architecture | `tags.test.ts` | 10 | Medium |\n| A-5 | Architecture | `vitest.config.ts` | — | Low |\n| A-6 | Architecture | `github-pages.test.ts` | 87 | Medium |\n| Q-1 | Test Quality | `heartbeat.test.ts` | 93 | Low |\n| Q-2 | Test Quality | `heartbeat.test.ts` | 111, 121 | Low |\n| Q-3 | Test Quality | `charts.test.ts` | 133 | Medium |\n| Q-4 | Test Quality | `search.test.ts` | 71 | Medium |\n| Q-5 | Test Quality | `rss.test.ts` | 93 | Low |\n| Q-6 | Test Quality | `build.test.ts` | 196 | Low |\n| Q-7 | Test Quality | `graph.test.ts` | 148 | Medium |\n| Q-8 | Test Quality | `webhook.test.ts` | 196 | Low |\n| Q-9 | Test Quality | `github-pages.test.ts` | 132 | Low |\n\n**No CRITICAL issues found.** The most important items to address are S-1 (path-boundary write on user-controlled project path), T-1 (unchecked cast on mock call args), A-4 (temp directory leak in tags tests), and Q-4 (budget test that can never fail).",

Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "Now I have enough to write the complete, precise review.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review\n\n### CRITICAL Issues\n\n**heartbeat.test.ts:93-100 — Test description does not match what the test actually exercises**\n\nThe test is named `'detects token pattern: token = abc123xyz'` but the transcript text it creates is `'secret = my-secret-value'`, not a token pattern. This is a straight copy-paste error. The test proves the secret pattern fires but it claims to verify the token pattern. If the secret pattern were ever removed while the token pattern was kept, the test would still pass (incorrectly).\n\n```\n// heartbeat.test.ts line 94\nit('detects token pattern: token = abc123xyz', () => {\n  const parsed = makeTranscript(['secret = my-secret-value'])  // ← tests \"secret\", not \"token\"\n```\n\n**heartbeat.test.ts:111-119 — Redundant `clearAlerts()` in the middle of a `describe` block pollutes test semantics**\n\nTests at lines 111, 121 call `clearAlerts()` at the start of the test body, while the `afterEach` at line 47 already clears them. The extra manual clears are defensive but misleading: they suggest the previous test could have left state that must be undone manually, which implies brittle ordering. The real issue is that both tests (`detects known secret prefixes` and `detects GitHub PAT prefix`) will silently double-count if the `afterEach` were ever removed. This is not a bug today, but combined with the naming confusion above, it makes these tests hard to trust.\n\n**webhook.test.ts:196-208 — `mockNetworkError` does not interact with `mockRequest.on`; the mock's promise is never settled, so the test may hang or time out in some configurations**\n\n`mockNetworkError` creates a local `req` object that registers an `'error'` event synchronously via `on`. However, `sendWebhook` attaches `req.on('error', reject)` to the object returned by `https.request`. The mock returns `req as unknown as ClientRequest`, so `req.on` is the local `vi.fn().mockImplementation(…)`. That means the `error` handler fires during the `.mockImplementation` callback, which runs before `sendWebhook` can attach the `setTimeout`. So `reject` is called but the Promise is still constructed — this only works because the rejection is caught inside the outer `try/catch`. In practice the test passes today, but `mockRequest.setTimeout` is not present on `req`, so if `sendWebhook` calls `req.setTimeout(...)` on the mock returned by `mockNetworkError`, it will throw `TypeError: req.setTimeout is not a function`. This is a latent error that will surface if anyone calls a network-error path that also hits the `setTimeout` line.\n\n---\n\n### HIGH Issues\n\n**stats.test.ts:163-164 — `extractToolNames` regex does not match hyphens the test claims it should**\n\nThe source regex at `stats.ts:100` is `/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_:-]*)/ `. The character class is `[A-Za-z0-9_:-]` — it includes colon and hyphen. So `custom-tool` is indeed captured as `custom-tool`. The test at line 162 passes today. However the companion test at line 160 claims `function_call input=...` produces `function_call`, which is correct. But both tests share no assertion about the `space` boundary: the regex stops at any character not in `[A-Za-z0-9_:-]`, including a space — so the `input=...` suffix is not captured. The tests are correct but they fail to cover the critical boundary case where a tool name contains only `-` after the initial char (e.g., `'-tool'` should return `'unknown'` since `-` is not a valid first char). There is no test for a string starting with a hyphen.\n\n**heartbeat.test.ts:232-243 — `processProject` test with `generate_report=true` does not verify that `buildReport` receives the correct `noAi`, `model`, `locale`, `ghPagesBranch`, `cname`, `webhookUrl`, `reportBaseUrl` fields**\n\nThe test only checks `opts.root` and `opts.output`. The `runBuildReport` function passes all six additional config fields to `buildReport`. None of them are validated. If a field were misspelled in `heartbeat.ts` (e.g., `webhookUrl: config.webhook_urL`), the test would not catch it.\n\n**rss.test.ts:88-94 — Time-zone fragility in `pubDate` assertion**\n\nThe test at line 93 asserts `/<pubDate>Mon,/` for `started_at: '2026-03-16T10:00:00Z'`. The implementation at `rss.ts:84` uses `new Date(iso).toUTCString()`. In UTC, 2026-03-16 is indeed a Monday, so the assertion is correct. But `toUTCString()` is not guaranteed by any ECMAScript spec to produce exactly `Mon,` — it is implementation-defined. The V8 engine used by Node.js does produce this, but it is a fragile assumption. The test should either parse the date independently or loosen the assertion to `/<pubDate>/.test(xml)`.\n\n**charts.test.ts:87-108 — `renderWeeklyLine` with empty input produces `<polyline>` but the test does not validate points are well-formed**\n\nThe source at `charts.ts:199-213` builds `weeks = getLast52Weeks()` (always 52 entries) even for empty input, then `data = weeks.map(w => weekBuckets[w] ?? 0)` producing 52 zeros. `maxVal = Math.max(...data, 1) = 1`. With 52 data points and `data.length > 1`, the `x` formula is well-defined so there is no NaN. The `not.toContain('NaN')` assertion is valid, but the test at line 89 asserts `toContain('<polyline')`, which relies on `getLast52Weeks()` always returning at least two entries. This is fine today but fails silently if `getLast52Weeks` ever returns `[]` (which would make `data.length = 0`, hitting the `data.length > 1` false branch and using `padL + plotW / 2` for a single point — still no NaN, but only a single point in the polyline).\n\n**github-pages.test.ts:93-100 — `hasCommand` for the commit is checked by matching only partial args**\n\nAt line 94-98, the test assembles the expected args array for the commit command exactly. The production code at `github-pages.ts:82-89` builds:\n```\ngit(publishRoot, '-c', `user.name=${COMMITTER_NAME}`, '-c', `user.email=${COMMITTER_EMAIL}`, 'commit', '-m', COMMIT_MESSAGE)\n```\nwhich `git()` passes to `execFileSync('git', args, ...)`. The `mockGit` implementation captures `rawArgs`. So `args` includes `['-c', 'user.name=MemoryTree', '-c', 'user.email=memorytree@local.invalid', 'commit', '-m', 'chore: publish memorytree report']`. The test asserts exactly that at lines 95-98, and the `matches` function does `actual.length !== expected.length`, so this is a strict full-match. This is actually correct. No issue here — I'm noting it as clear.\n\nHowever, the test at line 129 checks `not.toHaveBeenCalled` for the commit by matching `['commit', '-m', 'chore: publish memorytree report']` (3 elements). The production commit call has 7 args. `matches` will return `false` because `7 !== 3`. So the assertion `hasCommand(calls, ['commit', '-m', …])` is `false` regardless of whether a commit was issued. This means the \"skips commit\" test will pass even if a commit actually happened, because `hasCommand` with the short array can never match the 7-arg commit invocation. This is a real testing gap — the \"no commit\" case is not actually being verified.\n\n**build.test.ts:100-104 — `parseMessagesFromMarkdown` timestamp test uses only a single-element array**\n\n`messages[0]!.timestamp` is fine, but there is no test that verifies messages beyond the first also have their timestamps parsed. If the parser only populated the timestamp on the first message (a latent bug), this would not be caught.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM Issues\n\n**tags.test.ts:10-14 — `makeTmpDir` creates directories that are never cleaned up**\n\nThe helper at line 10-13 creates a temp directory without any cleanup in `afterEach`. Every test run accumulates directories under the system temp. This is a test isolation issue. There is no `beforeEach`/`afterEach` setup in the `getTags` describe blocks, unlike the `summarize.test.ts` which correctly uses `beforeEach`/`afterEach` with `rmSync`.\n\n**webhook.test.ts:20-25 — `mockRequest` is module-level state shared across all tests without being recreated**\n\n`mockRequest` is a plain object at the top level of the file. `vi.clearAllMocks()` in `beforeEach` clears call counts, but properties like `write.mock.calls` accumulate. If tests run out of order or a future test modifies `mockRequest` structurally, they will bleed. The cleaner pattern is `const mockRequest = { write: vi.fn(), end: vi.fn(), on: vi.fn(), setTimeout: vi.fn() }` created fresh inside each `beforeEach`.\n\n**webhook.test.ts:7-13 — Logger mock recreated on every test via factory but not cleared**\n\n`getLogger` returns a fresh object literal on every call because the factory `() => ({ info: vi.fn(), ... })` creates a new object each time. This means `logger.warn` captured at line 9 is never the same instance as the one called inside `sendWebhook`, so any assertion like `expect(logger.warn).toHaveBeenCalled()` would always fail. The current tests do not attempt to assert on the logger mock, so this is not a live bug — but the logger mock setup creates false confidence about observability testing.\n\n**heartbeat.test.ts:13-19 — `vi.mock('node:os')` uses a closure over `tmpDir` that is not yet initialized at module evaluation time**\n\n`tmpDir` is declared at line 11 with `let tmpDir: string` (no initializer). The `homedir` function closes over `tmpDir`. When Vitest hoists the `vi.mock` call and evaluates the factory, `tmpDir` is `undefined`. The first call to `homedir()` before `beforeEach` assigns it will return `undefined`. This is fine as long as no code calls `homedir()` during module initialization of the imported subjects. Currently this works because `_resetLogger()` and `setupLogging()` are called after `tmpDir` is set in `beforeEach`. But it's fragile — any change to the import order or a new module-level call to `homedir()` in the subjects would silently use `undefined` as the home directory path.\n\n**build.test.ts — No test for `parseMessagesFromMarkdown` with a `### N. assistant` message that has no blank line before text**\n\nThe clean markdown format (`Memory.md`: \"per message: `### N. role\\n- Timestamp: ISO\\n\\ntext`\") requires a blank line between the timestamp line and the message body. There is no test verifying the parser's behaviour when this blank line is absent.\n\n**github-pages.test.ts:55-64 — Security test for branch name injection does not assert logger warning was called**\n\nThe test verifies `execFileSync` is not called, which is the correct safety outcome. But it does not verify that the rejection was actually logged (`logger.warn` with the \"Invalid branch name\" message). This means a future refactor that silently drops invalid branches (without logging) would still pass.\n\n---\n\n### LOW Issues\n\n**heartbeat.test.ts:1 — `describe` block imports `vi` at top level but uses `buildReportMock` via `vi.hoisted()`**\n\nThe pattern is correct (hoisted mocks must precede `vi.mock` factories), but the comment at line 23-29 says \"must be at top level so vi.mock factory captures them\", which is slightly imprecise. The actual requirement is that `vi.hoisted()` runs before the `vi.mock()` factory. The comment could mislead future contributors into thinking the variable declaration position in source matters, when the important thing is Vitest's static hoisting behavior.\n\n**stats.test.ts:136-146 — `buildWeekBuckets` test comment says \"2026-03-10 is in week 2026-W11\" but does not actually assert this**\n\nThe test retrieves `weekKey = Object.keys(buckets)[0]` and asserts it matches `/^\\d{4}-W\\d{2}$/`. It never checks that both dates land in the same week bucket (`W11`). If the implementation put them in separate buckets, `val` would be 5 (not 8) but `weekKey` would still match the regex — meaning the test would pass with incorrect behavior.\n\n**summarize.test.ts:46-53 — Off-by-one in line length assertion**\n\nThe assertion at line 52 is `expect(line.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(506)`. The comment says `\"USER: \" + 500 chars = 506 chars max`. `\"USER: \"` is 6 chars. But the implementation at `summarize.ts:116-117` is:\n```ts\nconst text = m.text.slice(0, 500)\nreturn `${m.role.toUpperCase()}: ${text}`\n```\nFor role `'user'`, `'USER'.length = 4`, so the prefix is `'USER: '` = 6 chars, giving 506 total. The arithmetic in the comment is correct. The assertion bound of 506 is right. No bug, but the comment is confusingly written — it says `\"USER: \"` is 6 chars then writes the assertion without explicitly stating so.\n\n**vitest.config.ts — No `environment` key set**\n\nThe config at `packages/report/vitest.config.ts` does not specify `environment`. The default is `'node'`, which is correct for this package. However, `globals: true` is set without a corresponding TypeScript declaration augmentation file (no `/// <reference types=\"vitest/globals\" />` or `types: ['vitest/globals']` in tsconfig). This is low risk since the tests use explicit imports from `vitest`, but it creates a discrepancy between the `globals: true` config and the actual import style used throughout the test files.\n\n**projects.test.ts:30-31 — Test name says \"undefined-like input\" but passes `undefined as unknown as string`**\n\nThis tests the runtime guard for `undefined` input. The test is valid, but casting `undefined as unknown as string` explicitly defeats TypeScript's type system — the test can only exist because the production code is reached via a real JavaScript invocation pathway. A cleaner signal of intent would be `extractProject(undefined as unknown as string)` with a comment explaining this is a defensive test for JS callers or partial data.\n\n**search.test.ts:71-81 — Budget test tolerance is loose**\n\nThe assertion at line 80 checks `json.length <= 51_000` with 100 sessions each having 200-char snippets. The computed budget is `MAX_INDEX_BYTES = 50_000`. Allowing 1,000 bytes of tolerance (2%) is fine, but the comment should acknowledge this is intentional. Without a comment, a reviewer might tighten this to 50_000 and cause flakiness.\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| Severity | File | Line | Issue |\n|----------|------|------|-------|\n| CRITICAL | heartbeat.test.ts | 93-94 | Test description says \"token\" but text tests \"secret\" pattern |\n| CRITICAL | webhook.test.ts | 49-58 | `mockNetworkError` mock missing `setTimeout` — `TypeError` on network error path |\n| HIGH | heartbeat.test.ts | 232-243 | `generate_report=true` test only checks `root`/`output`; 6 config fields go unverified |\n| HIGH | github-pages.test.ts | 129 | \"skips commit\" assertion uses 3-arg array against 7-arg call; `hasCommand` always returns false — test vacuously passes |\n| HIGH | rss.test.ts | 93 | `/<pubDate>Mon,/` relies on `toUTCString()` format which is implementation-defined |\n| MEDIUM | tags.test.ts | 10-14 | `makeTmpDir` never cleans up; temp dirs accumulate across runs |\n| MEDIUM | webhook.test.ts | 20-25 | Module-level `mockRequest` shared across all tests; structural mutations persist through `clearAllMocks()` |\n| MEDIUM | heartbeat.test.ts | 11-19 | `tmpDir` is `undefined` when `vi.mock` factory first runs; fragile if any import triggers `homedir()` at init time |\n| MEDIUM | heartbeat.test.ts | 111, 121 | Manual `clearAlerts()` inside test body is redundant with `afterEach` and implies ordering dependence |\n| LOW | stats.test.ts | 136-146 | `buildWeekBuckets` test never verifies both dates are in the same week bucket |\n| LOW | summarize.test.ts | 46-53 | Test description arithmetic is confusingly phrased |\n| LOW | vitest.config.ts | 4-5 | `globals: true` without matching tsconfig types entry |",
    "type": "text"

两轮 review 完成。以下是合并后的关键发现：

---

## Review 1 — 正确性 / 边界情况

### CRITICAL
| 文件 | 行 | 问题 |
|------|-----|------|
| heartbeat.test.ts | 93–94 | 测试名称写 "token pattern" 但实际 input 是 'secret = my-secret-value'，名实不符 |
| webhook.test.ts | 196–208 | mockNetworkError 缺少 setTimeout 属性——如果生产代码在 error path 上调用 req.setTimeout() 会抛 TypeError |

### HIGH
| 文件 | 行 | 问题 |
|------|-----|------|
| heartbeat.test.ts | 232–243 | generate_report=true 只检查 root/output，6 个 config 字段（locale、model、webhookUrl…）完全没有断言 |
| github-pages.test.ts | 129 | "skips commit" 断言用 3-arg 数组匹配 7-arg 实际调用，hasCommand 永远返回 false，测试永远通过（即使发生了 commit 也不会被发现）|
| rss.test.ts | 93 | /<pubDate>Mon,/ 依赖 toUTCString() 格式，V8 能过但 ECMAScript 未保证 |

### MEDIUM
| 文件 | 行 | 问题 |
|------|-----|------|
| tags.test.ts | 10–14 | makeTmpDir 用 Math.random() 而非 mkdtempSync，且从不清理 — 每次跑测试都积累临时目录 |
| webhook.test.ts | 20–25 | 模块级 mockRequest 跨测试共享，clearAllMocks() 不重置结构性属性 |

---

## Review 2 — 安全 / 架构 / 类型安全

### SECURITY
| ID | 文件 | 行 | 问题 |
|----|------|-----|------|
| S-1 | heartbeat.ts | 29 | projectPath 来自用户 config，没有验证是否在合法根目录内 — path = "/etc" 会写 /etc/Memory/07_reports |
| S-2 | heartbeat.ts | 35 | !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] 不过滤空白字符串和 "false" |
| S-3 | summarize.test.ts | 135 | 'sk-test-key' 触发 gitleaks / truffleHog 告警，应改用明显假值如 'test-key-not-real' |

### TYPE SAFETY
| ID | 文件 | 行 | 问题 |
|----|------|-----|------|
| T-1 | heartbeat.test.ts | 238 | buildReportMock.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as { root, output } 裸 cast，若参数为空则后续断言会抛出晦涩错误 |
| T-4 | summarize.ts | 100 | (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code — err 是 unknown，应先 instanceof 检查 |

### ARCHITECTURE
| ID | 文件 | 行 | 问题 |
|----|------|-----|------|
| A-1 | heartbeat.ts | 162 | runBuildReport 内部吞掉所有错误，报告生成失败不写 alert（push 失败有 writeAlertWithThreshold，但报告失败没有）|
| A-4 | tags.test.ts | 10 | 同上临时目录不清理（同 Review 1 MEDIUM）|

### TEST QUALITY
| ID | 文件 | 行 | 问题 |
|----|------|-----|------|
| Q-7 | graph.test.ts | 148 | XSS 测试只检查原始字符串不存在，不验证转义后的形式存在 — 如果 title 被完全丢弃也通过 |
| Q-8 | webhook.test.ts | 196 | 网络错误测试不断言 logger.warn 被调用 |
| Q-9 | github-pages.test.ts | 132 | "git failure 时 resolves" 测试不断言 logger.warn 被调用，函数如果立即 no-op 也能通过 |

---

需要立即修复的（行为正确性风险）：
1. github-pages.test.ts:129 — "skips commit" 断言是空判断，需要用完整 7-arg 调用
2. heartbeat.test.ts:93 — 测试描述和 input 不一致
3. tags.test.ts — 临时目录泄漏，改用 mkdtempSync + afterEach 清理
4. heartbeat.ts:29 — 报告生成失败应写 alert

Read output=1→import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest' 2→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs' 3→...
Show output payload 
1→import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     4→import { join } from 'node:path'
     5→
     6→const logger = {
     7→  info: vi.fn(),
     8→  warn: vi.fn(),
     9→  debug: vi.fn(),
    10→  error: vi.fn(),
    11→}
    12→
    13→vi.mock('node:child_process', () => ({
    14→  execFileSync: vi.fn(),
    15→}))
    16→
    17→vi.mock('../../../src/log.js', () => ({
    18→  getLogger: () => logger,
    19→}))
    20→
    21→import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process'
    22→import { deployGithubPages } from '../../src/deploy/github-pages.js'
    23→
    24→let tmpDir: string
    25→let outputDir: string
    26→
    27→beforeEach(() => {
    28→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'ghpages-test-'))
    29→  outputDir = join(tmpDir, 'output')
    30→  mkdirSync(outputDir, { recursive: true })
    31→  writeFileSync(join(outputDir, 'index.html'), '<h1>report</h1>\n', 'utf-8')
    32→  vi.clearAllMocks()
    33→})
    34→
    35→afterEach(() => {
    36→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    37→})
    38→
    39→describe('deployGithubPages', () => {
    40→  it('writes CNAME when cname is set', async () => {
    41→    mockGit()
    42→
    43→    await deployGithubPages({
    44→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
    45→      outputDir,
    46→      branch: 'gh-pages',
    47→      cname: 'memory.example.com',
    48→    })
    49→
    50→    const cnamePath = join(outputDir, 'CNAME')
    51→    expect(existsSync(cnamePath)).toBe(true)
    52→    expect(readFileSync(cnamePath, 'utf-8').trim()).toBe('memory.example.com')
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  it('skips deploy for branch with shell meta-chars', async () => {
    56→    await deployGithubPages({
    57→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
    58→      outputDir,
    59→      branch: 'gh-pages; rm -rf /',
    60→      cname: '',
    61→    })
    62→
    63→    expect(execFileSync).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    64→  })
    65→
    66→  it('does nothing when branch is empty string', async () => {
    67→    await deployGithubPages({
    68→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
    69→      outputDir,
    70→      branch: '',
    71→      cname: '',
    72→    })
    73→
    74→    expect(execFileSync).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    75→  })
    76→
    77→  it('publishes to an existing remote branch via a temporary repo', async () => {
    78→    const calls = mockGit({ branchExists: true })
    79→
    80→    await deployGithubPages({
    81→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
    82→      outputDir,
    83→      branch: 'gh-pages',
    84→      cname: '',
    85→    })
    86→
    87→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['remote', 'get-url', 'origin'], tmpDir)).toBe(true)
    88→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['init'])).toBe(true)
    89→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['remote', 'add', 'origin', 'https://example.com/repo.git'])).toBe(true)
    90→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['fetch', '--depth', '1', 'origin', 'gh-pages'])).toBe(true)
    91→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['checkout', '-B', 'gh-pages', 'FETCH_HEAD'])).toBe(true)
    92→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['add', '--all'])).toBe(true)
    93→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['status', '--short'])).toBe(true)
    94→    expect(hasCommand(calls, [
    95→      '-c', 'user.name=MemoryTree',
    96→      '-c', 'user.email=memorytree@local.invalid',
    97→      'commit', '-m', 'chore: publish memorytree report',
    98→    ])).toBe(true)
    99→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['push', 'origin', 'HEAD:gh-pages'])).toBe(true)
   100→    expect(calls.some(call => call.args.includes('subtree'))).toBe(false)
   101→  })
   102→
   103→  it('creates an orphan branch when the remote branch does not exist', async () => {
   104→    const calls = mockGit({ branchExists: false })
   105→
   106→    await deployGithubPages({
   107→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
   108→      outputDir,
   109→      branch: 'gh-pages',
   110→      cname: '',
   111→    })
   112→
   113→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['checkout', '--orphan', 'gh-pages'])).toBe(true)
   114→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['push', 'origin', 'HEAD:gh-pages'])).toBe(true)
   115→  })
   116→
   117→  it('skips commit and push when there is nothing new to publish', async () => {
   118→    const calls = mockGit({ branchExists: true, statusOutput: '' })
   119→
   120→    await deployGithubPages({
   121→      repoRoot: tmpDir,
   122→      outputDir,
   123→      branch: 'gh-pages',
   124→      cname: '',
   125→    })
   126→
   127→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['status', '--short'])).toBe(true)
   128→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['push', 'origin', 'HEAD:gh-pages'])).toBe(false)
   129→    expect(hasCommand(calls, ['commit', '-m', 'chore: publish memorytree report'])).toBe(false)
   130→  })
   131→
   132→  it('does not throw when git commands fail', async () => {
   133→    vi.mocked(execFileSync).mockImplementation(() => {
   134→      throw new Error('git failure')
   135→    })
   136→
   137→    await expect(
   138→      deployGithubPages({
   139→        repoRoot: tmpDir,
   140→        outputDir,
   141→        branch: 'gh-pages',
   142→        cname: '',
   143→      }),
   144→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   145→  })
   146→})
   147→
   148→interface GitCall {
   149→  cwd?: string
   150→  args: string[]
   151→}
   152→
   153→function mockGit(options: {
   154→  branchExists?: boolean
   155→  remoteUrl?: string
   156→  statusOutput?: string
   157→} = {}): GitCall[] {
   158→  const calls: GitCall[] = []
   159→  const mockExec = vi.mocked(execFileSync)
   160→
   161→  mockExec.mockImplementation((_command, rawArgs, rawOptions) => {
   162→    const args = [...(rawArgs as string[])]
   163→    const cwd = (rawOptions as { cwd?: string } | undefined)?.cwd
   164→    calls.push({ cwd, args })
   165→
   166→    if (matches(args, ['remote', 'get-url', 'origin'])) {
   167→      return Buffer.from(options.remoteUrl ?? 'https://example.com/repo.git')
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    if (matches(args, ['ls-remote', '--exit-code', '--heads', 'origin', 'gh-pages'])) {
   171→      if (options.branchExists === false) {
   172→        throw new Error('branch not found')
   173→      }
   174→      return Buffer.from('refs/heads/gh-pages\n')
   175→    }
   176→
   177→    if (matches(args, ['status', '--short'])) {
   178→      return Buffer.from(options.statusOutput ?? 'A  index.html\n')
   179→    }
   180→
   181→    return Buffer.from('')
   182→  })
   183→
   184→  return calls
   185→}
   186→
   187→function hasCommand(calls: GitCall[], expected: string[], cwd?: string): boolean {
   188→  return calls.some(call => matches(call.args, expected) && (cwd === undefined || call.cwd === cwd))
   189→}
   190→
   191→function matches(actual: string[], expected: string[]): boolean {
   192→  if (actual.length !== expected.length) return false
   193→  return actual.every((value, index) => value === expected[index])
   194→}
   195→
Tool Result #352 Read toolu_01GGD9CsxL5wzZ5rhWYL7Fm9 12:24:29
Read output=1→import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' 2→import type { ClientRequest, IncomingMessage } from 'node:https' 3→ 4→// Mock getLogger 5→vi.mock('../../../src...
Show output payload 
1→import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import type { ClientRequest, IncomingMessage } from 'node:https'
     3→
     4→// Mock getLogger
     5→vi.mock('../../../src/log.js', () => ({
     6→  getLogger: () => ({
     7→    info: vi.fn(),
     8→    warn: vi.fn(),
     9→    debug: vi.fn(),
    10→    error: vi.fn(),
    11→  }),
    12→}))
    13→
    14→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→// Mock node:https so no real network calls are made
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→type RequestCallback = (res: Partial<IncomingMessage>) => void
    19→
    20→const mockRequest = {
    21→  write: vi.fn(),
    22→  end: vi.fn(),
    23→  on: vi.fn(),
    24→  setTimeout: vi.fn(),
    25→}
    26→
    27→const mockHttpsRequest = vi.fn()
    28→
    29→vi.mock('node:https', () => ({
    30→  default: { request: mockHttpsRequest },
    31→}))
    32→
    33→import { sendWebhook } from '../../src/deploy/webhook.js'
    34→
    35→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→// Helpers
    37→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→
    39→/** Make mockHttpsRequest resolve immediately with the given status code. */
    40→function mockSuccess(statusCode = 200) {
    41→  mockHttpsRequest.mockImplementation((opts: unknown, cb: RequestCallback) => {
    42→    cb({ statusCode, resume: vi.fn() })
    43→    return mockRequest as unknown as ClientRequest
    44→  })
    45→}
    46→
    47→/** Make mockHttpsRequest trigger the 'error' event. */
    48→function mockNetworkError() {
    49→  mockHttpsRequest.mockImplementation((_opts: unknown, _cb: RequestCallback) => {
    50→    const req = {
    51→      ...mockRequest,
    52→      on: vi.fn().mockImplementation((event: string, handler: (e: Error) => void) => {
    53→        if (event === 'error') handler(new Error('network failure'))
    54→        return req
    55→      }),
    56→    }
    57→    return req as unknown as ClientRequest
    58→  })
    59→}
    60→
    61→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→// Setup
    63→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→
    65→beforeEach(() => {
    66→  vi.clearAllMocks()
    67→  mockRequest.on.mockReturnValue(mockRequest)
    68→  mockRequest.setTimeout.mockReturnValue(mockRequest)
    69→})
    70→
    71→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→// No-op on empty URL
    73→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→describe('sendWebhook — no-op on empty URL', () => {
    76→  it('resolves immediately when url is empty', async () => {
    77→    await expect(sendWebhook({ url: '', sessionCount: 10 })).resolves.not.toThrow()
    78→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    79→  })
    80→})
    81→
    82→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    83→// SSRF protection
    84→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    85→
    86→describe('sendWebhook — SSRF protection', () => {
    87→  it('rejects http:// URLs (only https allowed)', async () => {
    88→    await expect(
    89→      sendWebhook({ url: 'http://example.com/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
    90→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
    91→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    92→  })
    93→
    94→  it('rejects localhost', async () => {
    95→    await expect(
    96→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://localhost/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
    97→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
    98→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    99→  })
   100→
   101→  it('rejects 127.x.x.x', async () => {
   102→    await expect(
   103→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://127.0.0.1/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   104→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   105→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   106→  })
   107→
   108→  it('rejects 169.254.x.x (link-local / metadata endpoint)', async () => {
   109→    await expect(
   110→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/', sessionCount: 5 })
   111→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   112→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   113→  })
   114→
   115→  it('rejects 10.x.x.x (RFC 1918)', async () => {
   116→    await expect(
   117→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://10.0.0.1/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   118→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   119→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   120→  })
   121→
   122→  it('rejects IPv6 loopback ::1', async () => {
   123→    await expect(
   124→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[::1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   125→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   126→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   127→  })
   128→
   129→  it('rejects IPv6 ULA fc00::/7 (fc range)', async () => {
   130→    await expect(
   131→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[fc00::1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   132→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   133→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   134→  })
   135→
   136→  it('rejects IPv6 ULA fc00::/7 (fd range)', async () => {
   137→    await expect(
   138→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[fd12:3456::1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   139→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   140→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   141→  })
   142→
   143→  it('rejects IPv6 link-local fe80::/10', async () => {
   144→    await expect(
   145→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[fe80::1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   146→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   147→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   148→  })
   149→
   150→  it('rejects IPv4-mapped IPv6 ::ffff:127.0.0.1', async () => {
   151→    await expect(
   152→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://[::ffff:127.0.0.1]/webhook', sessionCount: 5 })
   153→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   154→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
   155→  })
   156→})
   157→
   158→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   159→// Platform detection — hostname spoofing resistance
   160→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   161→
   162→describe('sendWebhook — platform detection spoofing resistance', () => {
   163→  it('treats subdomain-spoof feishu URL as generic, not feishu', async () => {
   164→    mockSuccess()
   165→    await sendWebhook({
   166→      url: 'https://my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com/hook/test',
   167→      sessionCount: 3,
   168→    })
   169→
   170→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   171→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { event?: string; msg_type?: string }
   172→    // Generic payload has 'event' field; Feishu has 'msg_type'
   173→    expect(parsed.event).toBe('memorytree.report.updated')
   174→    expect(parsed.msg_type).toBeUndefined()
   175→  })
   176→
   177→  it('treats path-spoof telegram URL as generic', async () => {
   178→    mockSuccess()
   179→    await sendWebhook({
   180→      url: 'https://evil.com/api.telegram.org/botTEST/sendMessage',
   181→      sessionCount: 1,
   182→    })
   183→
   184→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   185→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { event?: string; text?: string }
   186→    expect(parsed.event).toBe('memorytree.report.updated')
   187→    expect(parsed.text).toBeUndefined()
   188→  })
   189→})
   190→
   191→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   192→// Network failure does not throw
   193→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   194→
   195→describe('sendWebhook — network failure does not throw', () => {
   196→  it('resolves even when HTTP request triggers error event', async () => {
   197→    mockNetworkError()
   198→    await expect(
   199→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 5 })
   200→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   201→  })
   202→
   203→  it('resolves on HTTP 4xx response', async () => {
   204→    mockSuccess(400)
   205→    await expect(
   206→      sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 5 })
   207→    ).resolves.not.toThrow()
   208→  })
   209→})
   210→
   211→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   212→// Payload assertions
   213→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   214→
   215→describe('sendWebhook — payload structure', () => {
   216→  it('sends correct JSON body for slack', async () => {
   217→    mockSuccess()
   218→    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 7 })
   219→
   220→    expect(mockHttpsRequest).toHaveBeenCalled()
   221→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   222→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { text: string }
   223→    expect(parsed.text).toContain('7')
   224→    expect(parsed.text).toContain('MemoryTree')
   225→  })
   226→
   227→  it('sends correct JSON body for discord', async () => {
   228→    mockSuccess()
   229→    await sendWebhook({
   230→      url: 'https://discord.com/api/webhooks/123/test',
   231→      sessionCount: 5,
   232→      reportUrl: 'https://example.com/report',
   233→    })
   234→
   235→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   236→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as { content: string }
   237→    expect(parsed.content).toContain('5')
   238→    expect(parsed.content).toContain('MemoryTree')
   239→  })
   240→
   241→  it('includes new session count in feishu payload', async () => {
   242→    mockSuccess()
   243→    await sendWebhook({
   244→      url: 'https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/bot/v2/hook/test',
   245→      sessionCount: 42,
   246→      newSessionIds: ['s1', 's2'],
   247→    })
   248→
   249→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   250→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as Record<string, unknown>
   251→    // Feishu sends interactive card
   252→    expect(parsed.msg_type).toBe('interactive')
   253→  })
   254→
   255→  it('sends generic payload for unknown URL', async () => {
   256→    mockSuccess()
   257→    await sendWebhook({
   258→      url: 'https://my-server.example.com/webhook',
   259→      sessionCount: 3,
   260→      newSessionIds: ['s1'],
   261→    })
   262→
   263→    const writtenBody = mockRequest.write.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as string
   264→    const parsed = JSON.parse(writtenBody) as {
   265→      event: string
   266→      session_count: number
   267→      new_session_count: number
   268→    }
   269→    expect(parsed.event).toBe('memorytree.report.updated')
   270→    expect(parsed.session_count).toBe(3)
   271→    expect(parsed.new_session_count).toBe(1)
   272→  })
   273→
   274→  it('sends correct hostname in request options', async () => {
   275→    mockSuccess()
   276→    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 1 })
   277→
   278→    const reqOpts = mockHttpsRequest.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as { hostname: string }
   279→    expect(reqOpts.hostname).toBe('hooks.slack.com')
   280→  })
   281→
   282→  it('sets Content-Type: application/json and Content-Length headers', async () => {
   283→    mockSuccess()
   284→    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 1 })
   285→
   286→    const reqOpts = mockHttpsRequest.mock.calls[0]?.[0] as {
   287→      headers: Record<string, string | number>
   288→    }
   289→    expect(reqOpts.headers['Content-Type']).toBe('application/json')
   290→    expect(typeof reqOpts.headers['Content-Length']).toBe('number')
   291→    expect(reqOpts.headers['Content-Length'] as number).toBeGreaterThan(0)
   292→  })
   293→
   294→  it('sets request timeout to 5000 ms', async () => {
   295→    mockSuccess()
   296→    await sendWebhook({ url: 'https://hooks.slack.com/services/test', sessionCount: 1 })
   297→
   298→    expect(mockRequest.setTimeout).toHaveBeenCalledWith(5000, expect.any(Function))
   299→  })
   300→})
   301→
Tool Result #353 Read toolu_01QmiQx3zvkJJT6jE1828RK5 12:24:29
Read output=1→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest' 2→import os from 'node:os' 3→import { join } from 'node:path' 4→import { mkdirSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs' 5→ 6→// ------...
Show output payload 
1→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2→import os from 'node:os'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { mkdirSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Helpers
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→function makeTmpDir(): string {
    11→  const dir = join(os.tmpdir(), `mt-tags-test-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`)
    12→  mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })
    13→  return dir
    14→}
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// renderTagBadges tests (synchronous, no AI)
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→import { renderTagBadges } from '../src/tags.js'
    21→
    22→describe('renderTagBadges', () => {
    23→  it('returns empty string for empty tags', () => {
    24→    expect(renderTagBadges([])).toBe('')
    25→  })
    26→
    27→  it('renders tag pills', () => {
    28→    const html = renderTagBadges(['typescript', 'testing'])
    29→    expect(html).toContain('class="tag"')
    30→    expect(html).toContain('typescript')
    31→    expect(html).toContain('testing')
    32→  })
    33→
    34→  it('escapes HTML special chars in tags', () => {
    35→    const html = renderTagBadges(['a&b', '<test>'])
    36→    expect(html).toContain('a&amp;b')
    37→    expect(html).toContain('&lt;test&gt;')
    38→    expect(html).not.toContain('<test>')
    39→  })
    40→
    41→  it('sets data-tag attribute', () => {
    42→    const html = renderTagBadges(['typescript'])
    43→    expect(html).toContain('data-tag="typescript"')
    44→  })
    45→})
    46→
    47→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→// getTags tests
    49→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→
    51→import { getTags } from '../src/tags.js'
    52→
    53→describe('getTags', () => {
    54→  it('returns [] when noAi=true', async () => {
    55→    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()
    56→    const result = await getTags('abc123', 'some summary', { cacheDir, noAi: true, model: 'test' })
    57→    expect(result).toEqual([])
    58→  })
    59→
    60→  it('returns [] when summary is empty', async () => {
    61→    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()
    62→    const result = await getTags('abc123', '', { cacheDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })
    63→    expect(result).toEqual([])
    64→  })
    65→
    66→  it('returns [] when summary is whitespace only', async () => {
    67→    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()
    68→    const result = await getTags('abc123', '   \n  ', { cacheDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })
    69→    expect(result).toEqual([])
    70→  })
    71→
    72→  it('returns [] when no API key set', async () => {
    73→    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()
    74→    const saved = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    75→    delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    76→    try {
    77→      const result = await getTags('abc123', 'a valid summary', { cacheDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })
    78→      expect(result).toEqual([])
    79→    } finally {
    80→      if (saved !== undefined) process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = saved
    81→    }
    82→  })
    83→
    84→  it('returns cached tags without calling API', async () => {
    85→    const cacheDir = makeTmpDir()
    86→    const sha256 = 'cached-sha256'
    87→    // Write cache file
    88→    const tagsDir = join(cacheDir, 'tags')
    89→    mkdirSync(tagsDir, { recursive: true })
    90→    writeFileSync(
    91→      join(tagsDir, `${sha256}.json`),
    92→      JSON.stringify({ sha256, tags: ['cached', 'tag'], generated_at: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' }),
    93→    )
    94→    // Even with a fake API key, cached result should be returned immediately
    95→    const saved = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    96→    process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = 'fake-key'
    97→    try {
    98→      const result = await getTags(sha256, 'some summary', { cacheDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })
    99→      expect(result).toEqual(['cached', 'tag'])
   100→    } finally {
   101→      if (saved !== undefined) process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = saved
   102→      else delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
   103→    }
   104→  })
   105→})
   106→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→import {
     6→  buildSummaryPrompt,
     7→  readCache,
     8→  writeCache,
     9→  getSummary,
    10→} from '../src/summarize.js'
    11→import type { SummaryMessage } from '../src/summarize.js'
    12→
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→// Setup
    15→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→
    17→let tmpDir: string
    18→
    19→beforeEach(() => {
    20→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'summarize-test-'))
    21→  // Ensure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set for most tests
    22→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    23→})
    24→
    25→afterEach(() => {
    26→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    27→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    28→  vi.restoreAllMocks()
    29→})
    30→
    31→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→// buildSummaryPrompt
    33→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→
    35→describe('buildSummaryPrompt', () => {
    36→  it('includes role and text for each message', () => {
    37→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = [
    38→      { role: 'user', text: 'Hello world' },
    39→      { role: 'assistant', text: 'Hi there' },
    40→    ]
    41→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    42→    expect(prompt).toContain('USER: Hello world')
    43→    expect(prompt).toContain('ASSISTANT: Hi there')
    44→  })
    45→
    46→  it('truncates message text to 500 chars', () => {
    47→    const longText = 'x'.repeat(600)
    48→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = [{ role: 'user', text: longText }]
    49→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    50→    const line = prompt.split('\n')[0] ?? ''
    51→    // "USER: " + 500 chars = 506 chars max
    52→    expect(line.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(506)
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  it('limits to first 30 messages', () => {
    56→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = Array.from({ length: 50 }, (_, i) => ({
    57→      role: i % 2 === 0 ? 'user' : 'assistant',
    58→      text: `Message ${i}`,
    59→    }))
    60→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    61→    // Prompt should not contain message 30+
    62→    expect(prompt).not.toContain('Message 30')
    63→    expect(prompt).toContain('Message 29')
    64→  })
    65→
    66→  it('handles empty messages array', () => {
    67→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt([])
    68→    expect(prompt).toBe('')
    69→  })
    70→})
    71→
    72→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→// Cache read/write
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→
    76→describe('readCache / writeCache', () => {
    77→  it('returns null for cache miss', () => {
    78→    const result = readCache('nonexistent-sha256', tmpDir)
    79→    expect(result).toBeNull()
    80→  })
    81→
    82→  it('round-trips: write then read', () => {
    83→    writeCache('abc123', 'Great session summary.', tmpDir)
    84→    const result = readCache('abc123', tmpDir)
    85→    expect(result).toBe('Great session summary.')
    86→  })
    87→
    88→  it('creates cache file at correct path', () => {
    89→    writeCache('sha256abc', 'some summary', tmpDir)
    90→    expect(existsSync(join(tmpDir, 'sha256abc.json'))).toBe(true)
    91→  })
    92→
    93→  it('returns null for corrupted cache file', async () => {
    94→    const { writeFileSync } = await import('node:fs')
    95→    writeFileSync(join(tmpDir, 'bad.json'), 'not json!!!', 'utf-8')
    96→    expect(readCache('bad', tmpDir)).toBeNull()
    97→  })
    98→})
    99→
   100→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   101→// getSummary
   102→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   103→
   104→describe('getSummary', () => {
   105→  it('returns cached summary without API call', async () => {
   106→    writeCache('cachedhash', 'Cached result', tmpDir)
   107→    const result = await getSummary('cachedhash', [], {
   108→      cacheDir: tmpDir,
   109→      noAi: false,
   110→      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   111→    })
   112→    expect(result).toBe('Cached result')
   113→  })
   114→
   115→  it('returns empty string when noAi=true', async () => {
   116→    const result = await getSummary('newsha', [], {
   117→      cacheDir: tmpDir,
   118→      noAi: true,
   119→      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   120→    })

   122→  })
   123→
   124→  it('returns empty string when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set', async () => {

   126→    const result = await getSummary('newsha2', [], {
   127→      cacheDir: tmpDir,
   128→      noAi: false,
   129→      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   130→    })
   131→    expect(result).toBe('')
   132→  })
   133→
   134→  it('prefers cache over API even when key is set', async () => {
   135→    process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = 'sk-test-key'
   136→    writeCache('cachedwithkey', 'Cached!', tmpDir)
   137→    const result = await getSummary('cachedwithkey', [], {

   141→    })

22→
    23→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→// Report generation
    25→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→
    27→async function runBuildReport(config: Config, projectPath: string, projectName: string): Promise<void> {
    28→  const logger = getLogger()
    29→  const output = join(projectPath, 'Memory', '07_reports')
    30→  try {
    31→    const { buildReport } = await import('@openmnemo/report')
    32→    await buildReport({
    33→      root: projectPath,
    34→      output,
    35→      noAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'],
    36→      model: config.ai_summary_model,
    37→      locale: config.locale,
    38→      ghPagesBranch: config.gh_pages_branch,
    39→      cname: config.cname,
    40→      webhookUrl: config.webhook_url,
    41→      reportBaseUrl: config.report_base_url,
    42→    })
    43→    logger.info(`[${projectName}] Report generated at ${output}.`)
    44→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    45→    logger.error(`[${projectName}] Report generation failed: ${String(err)}`)
    46→  }
    47→}
    48→
    49→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→// Sensitive pattern detection
    51→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    52→
    53→const SENSITIVE_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
    54→  /(?:api[_-]?key|apikey)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
    55→  /(?:password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
    56→  /(?:secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
    57→  /(?:sk-|pk_live_|sk_live_|ghp_|gho_|glpat-)\S{10,}/,
    58→  /Bearer\s+\S{20,}/i,
    59→]
    60→
    61→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→// Main entry point
    63→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→
    65→export async function main(): Promise<number> {
    66→  const config = loadConfig()
    67→  setupLogging(config.log_level as LogLevel)
    68→  const logger = getLogger()
    69→
    70→  if (!acquireLock()) {
    71→    logger.info('Another heartbeat instance is running. Exiting.')

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so any internal path helpers (logging, alerts, config, lock)
     8→// all point to a temp directory and don't pollute the real home.
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→
    11→let tmpDir: string
    12→
    13→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
    14→  const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
    15→  return {
    16→    ...actual,
    17→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    18→  }
    19→})
    20→
    21→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    22→// Hoisted mocks (must be at top level so vi.mock factory captures them)
    23→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→
    25→const buildReportMock = vi.hoisted(() => vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined))
    26→
    27→vi.mock('@openmnemo/report', () => ({
    28→  buildReport: buildReportMock,
    29→}))
    30→
    31→import { scanSensitive, processProject } from '../../src/heartbeat.js'
    32→import { _resetLogger, setupLogging } from '../../src/log.js'
    33→import { readAlerts, clearAlerts } from '../../src/alert.js'
    34→import type { ParsedTranscript } from '@openmnemo/types'
    35→
    36→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// Setup / Teardown
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→beforeEach(() => {
    41→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'hb-test-'))
    42→  _resetLogger()
    43→  setupLogging('warn') // suppress info/debug noise during tests
    44→})
    45→
    46→afterEach(() => {
    47→  clearAlerts()
    48→  _resetLogger()
    49→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    50→})

Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Webhook notification: sends a POST to configurable endpoints.
     3→ * Supports Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and generic webhooks.
     4→ * Never throws — failures are logged as warnings.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { getLogger } from '../log.js'
     8→
     9→export interface WebhookOptions {
    10→  url: string
    11→  sessionCount: number
    12→  newSessionIds?: string[]
    13→  reportUrl?: string
    14→}
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// Platform detection
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→type Platform = 'feishu' | 'telegram' | 'discord' | 'slack' | 'generic'
    21→
    22→/**
    23→ * Detect platform from a parsed URL.
    24→ * Uses exact hostname matching to prevent subdomain-spoofing attacks like
    25→ * https://my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com being mistaken for Feishu.
    26→ */
    27→function detectPlatform(parsedUrl: URL): Platform {
    28→  const h = parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase()
    29→  if (h === 'open.feishu.cn' || h === 'open.larksuite.com') return 'feishu'
    30→  if (h === 'api.telegram.org') return 'telegram'
    31→  if (h === 'discord.com' || h === 'discordapp.com') return 'discord'
    32→  if (h === 'hooks.slack.com') return 'slack'
    33→  return 'generic'
    34→}
    35→
    36→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// Payload builders
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→function buildFeishuPayload(options: WebhookOptions): Record<string, unknown> {
Tool Result #366 Read toolu_01MUCN4RJfbvi47aqWJPZzGi 12:26:28
Read output=95→ } catch (err: unknown) { 96→ logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Deploy failed: ${String(err)}`) 97→ // Never throw; report deploy failure must not abort heartbeat. 98→ } 99→} 100→ 101→...
Show output payload 
95→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    96→    logger.warn(`[gh-pages] Deploy failed: ${String(err)}`)
    97→    // Never throw; report deploy failure must not abort heartbeat.
    98→  }
    99→}
   100→
   101→function remoteBranchExists(cwd: string, branch: string): boolean {
   102→  try {
   103→    git(cwd, 'ls-remote', '--exit-code', '--heads', 'origin', branch)
   104→    return true
   105→  } catch {
   106→    return false
   107→  }
   108→}
   109→
   110→function clearDirectory(root: string): void {
   111→  for (const entry of readdirSync(root)) {
   112→    if (entry === '.git') continue
   113→    rmSync(join(root, entry), { recursive: true, force: true })
   114→  }
   115→}
   116→
   117→function copyDirectoryContents(source: string, destination: string): void {
   118→  for (const entry of readdirSync(source)) {
   119→    cpSync(join(source, entry), join(destination, entry), { recursive: true, force: true })
   120→  }
   121→}
   122→
   123→function hasPendingChanges(cwd: string): boolean {
   124→  return git(cwd, 'status', '--short').trim().length > 0

Tool Call #387 Write toolu_01ARWU2TJEGjQHqYuzZtpYCS 12:27:46
Write input={"content":"import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport os from 'node:os'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, wr...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/tests/tags.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport os from 'node:os'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// renderTagBadges tests (synchronous, no AI)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimport { renderTagBadges } from '../src/tags.js'\n\ndescribe('renderTagBadges', () => {\n  it('returns empty string for empty tags', () => {\n    expect(renderTagBadges([])).toBe('')\n  })\n\n  it('renders tag pills', () => {\n    const html = renderTagBadges(['typescript', 'testing'])\n    expect(html).toContain('class=\"tag\"')\n    expect(html).toContain('typescript')\n    expect(html).toContain('testing')\n  })\n\n  it('escapes HTML special chars in tags', () => {\n    const html = renderTagBadges(['a&b', '<test>'])\n    expect(html).toContain('a&amp;b')\n    expect(html).toContain('&lt;test&gt;')\n    expect(html).not.toContain('<test>')\n  })\n\n  it('sets data-tag attribute', () => {\n    const html = renderTagBadges(['typescript'])\n    expect(html).toContain('data-tag=\"typescript\"')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// getTags tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimport { getTags } from '../src/tags.js'\n\ndescribe('getTags', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(os.tmpdir(), 'mt-tags-test-'))\n  })\n\n  afterEach(() => {\n    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n  })\n\n  it('returns [] when noAi=true', async () => {\n    const result = await getTags('abc123', 'some summary', { cacheDir: tmpDir, noAi: true, model: 'test' })\n    expect(result).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('returns [] when summary is empty', async () => {\n    const result = await getTags('abc123', '', { cacheDir: tmpDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })\n    expect(result).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('returns [] when summary is whitespace only', async () => {\n    const result = await getTags('abc123', '   \\n  ', { cacheDir: tmpDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })\n    expect(result).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('returns [] when no API key set', async () => {\n    const saved = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    try {\n      const result = await getTags('abc123', 'a valid summary', { cacheDir: tmpDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })\n      expect(result).toEqual([])\n    } finally {\n      if (saved !== undefined) process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = saved\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns cached tags without calling API', async () => {\n    const sha256 = 'cached-sha256'\n    // Write cache file\n    const tagsDir = join(tmpDir, 'tags')\n    mkdirSync(tagsDir, { recursive: true })\n    writeFileSync(\n      join(tagsDir, `${sha256}.json`),\n      JSON.stringify({ sha256, tags: ['cached', 'tag'], generated_at: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' }),\n    )\n    // Even with a fake API key, cached result should be returned immediately\n    const saved = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = 'fake-key'\n    try {\n      const result = await getTags(sha256, 'some summary', { cacheDir: tmpDir, noAi: false, model: 'test' })\n      expect(result).toEqual(['cached', 'tag'])\n    } finally {\n      if (saved !== undefined) process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = saved\n      else delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']\n    }\n  })\n})\n"
}

}

Show input payload 
{
  "command": "cd D:/demo1/openmnemo && git add packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts packages/sync/tests/heartbeat/heartbeat.test.ts packages/report/tests/tags.test.ts packages/report/tests/summarize.test.ts packages/report/tests/deploy/github-pages.test.ts packages/report/tests/deploy/webhook.test.ts && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix(tests): address review findings from 2-pass review\n\nheartbeat.ts:\n  - write 'report_failed' alert when runBuildReport() throws\n\nheartbeat.test.ts:\n  - fix misleading test name \"token pattern\" → \"secret/token pattern\"\n  - remove redundant clearAlerts() calls (afterEach already handles it)\n  - assert all 6 config fields passed to buildReport, not just root/output\n\ngithub-pages.test.ts:\n  - fix \"skips commit\" assertion: use full 7-arg commit array so\n    hasCommand() can match (was using 3-arg → always returned false)\n  - add logger.warn assertion to \"does not throw\" test\n  - fix vi.mock path: '../../../src/log.js' → '../../src/log.js'\n    (tests/deploy/ is only 2 levels from packages/report/)\n\nwebhook.test.ts:\n  - fix vi.mock path same as above\n  - promote logger to vi.hoisted() module-level so assertions work\n  - assert logger.warn called on network failure\n\ntags.test.ts:\n  - replace Math.random() tmpdir with mkdtempSync()\n  - add beforeEach/afterEach cleanup to getTags describe block\n\nsummarize.test.ts:\n  - replace 'sk-test-key' with 'test-api-key-not-real' to avoid\n    secret-scanner false positives\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Commit all review fixes"
}

assistant #413 12:30:23

所有问题修复完毕，589 测试全绿。

### 修复汇总

| 类别 | 问题 | 修复 |
|------|------|------|
| Bug | github-pages.test.ts:129 — "skips commit" 断言用 3-arg 数组匹配 7-arg 调用，永远通过 | 改为完整 7-arg commit 数组 |
| Bug | tests/deploy/ 两个文件的 log mock 路径 ../../../src/log.js 解析到不存在的路径 | 改为 ../../src/log.js |
| 架构 | heartbeat.ts 报告生成失败只 log error，不写 alert | 加 writeAlert(..., 'report_failed', ...) |
| 测试质量 | heartbeat.test.ts:93 测试名写 "token" 但 input 测的是 "secret" | 改名为 "secret/token pattern" |
| 测试质量 | heartbeat.test.ts 两处冗余 clearAlerts() | 删除（afterEach 已处理） |
| 测试质量 | heartbeat.test.ts buildReport 只断言 root/output | 加入 6 个 config 字段断言 |
| 测试质量 | github-pages.test.ts + webhook.test.ts "不抛出"测试不断言日志 | 加 logger.warn 断言 |
| 测试质量 | webhook.test.ts logger mock 每次调用返回新对象，无法断言 | 改为 vi.hoisted() 模块级对象 |
| 泄漏 | tags.test.ts 用 Math.random() 创建临时目录且不清理 | 改用 mkdtempSync + afterEach cleanup |
| 安全 | summarize.test.ts 'sk-test-key' 触发 secret scanner | 改为 'test-api-key-not-real' |
