1→/**
     2→ * MemoryTree CLI — unified entry point.
     3→ * Registers all subcommands via commander.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { Command } from 'commander'
     7→
     8→const program = new Command()
     9→
    10→program
    11→  .name('memorytree')
    12→  .description('MemoryTree — transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity')
    13→  .version('0.1.0')
    14→
    15→// ── init ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16→
    17→program
    18→  .command('init')
    19→  .description('Initialize a MemoryTree workspace in a repository')
    20→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    21→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project name', 'this project')
    22→  .option('--goal-summary <text>', 'Initial goal summary', 'Describe the long-term project goal here.')
    23→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Template locale: auto, en, or zh-cn', 'auto')
    24→  .option('--date <date>', 'Override date as YYYY-MM-DD')
    25→  .option('--time <time>', 'Override time as HH:MM')
    26→  .option('--skip-agents', 'Deprecated — use upgrade instead')
    27→  .option('--force', 'Overwrite existing generated files')
    28→  .action(async (opts) => {
    29→    const { cmdInit } = await import('./cmd-init.js')
    30→    process.exitCode = cmdInit({
    31→      root: opts.root,
    32→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    33→      goalSummary: opts.goalSummary,
    34→      locale: opts.locale,
    35→      date: opts.date ?? '',
    36→      time: opts.time ?? '',
    37→      skipAgents: opts.skipAgents ?? false,
    38→      force: opts.force ?? false,
    39→    })
    40→  })
    41→
    42→// ── upgrade ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    43→
    44→program
    45→  .command('upgrade')
    46→  .description('Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree without overwriting existing policy')
    47→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    48→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project name', 'this project')
    49→  .option('--goal-summary <text>', 'Fallback goal summary', 'Describe the long-term project goal here.')
    50→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Requested locale: auto, en, or zh-cn', 'auto')
    51→  .option('--date <date>', 'Override date as YYYY-MM-DD')
    52→  .option('--time <time>', 'Override time as HH:MM')
    53→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
    54→  .action(async (opts) => {
    55→    const { cmdUpgrade } = await import('./cmd-upgrade.js')
    56→    process.exitCode = cmdUpgrade({
    57→      root: opts.root,
    58→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    59→      goalSummary: opts.goalSummary,
    60→      locale: opts.locale,
    61→      date: opts.date ?? '',
    62→      time: opts.time ?? '',
    63→      format: opts.format,
    64→    })
    65→  })
    66→
    67→// ── import ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→
    69→program
    70→  .command('import')
    71→  .description('Import one local transcript into MemoryTree archives')
    72→  .requiredOption('--source <path>', 'Raw transcript source file path')
    73→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    74→  .option('--client <client>', 'Transcript client: auto, codex, claude, gemini', 'auto')
    75→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
    76→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
    77→  .option('--raw-upload-permission <perm>', 'Permission: not-set, approved, denied', 'not-set')
    78→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
    79→  .action(async (opts) => {
    80→    const { cmdImport } = await import('./cmd-import.js')
    81→    process.exitCode = await cmdImport({
    82→      root: opts.root,
    83→      source: opts.source,
    84→      client: opts.client,
    85→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    86→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
    87→      rawUploadPermission: opts.rawUploadPermission,
    88→      format: opts.format,
    89→    })
    90→  })
    91→
    92→// ── discover ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93→
    94→program
    95→  .command('discover')
    96→  .description('Discover and import local AI transcripts')
    97→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    98→  .option('--client <client>', 'Client filter: all, codex, claude, gemini', 'all')
    99→  .option('--scope <scope>', 'Scope: current-project or all-projects', 'all-projects')
   100→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
   101→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
   102→  .option('--raw-upload-permission <perm>', 'Permission: not-set, approved, denied', 'not-set')
   103→  .option('--limit <n>', 'Limit discovered sources', '0')
   104→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   105→  .action(async (opts) => {
   106→    const { cmdDiscover } = await import('./cmd-discover.js')
   107→    process.exitCode = await cmdDiscover({
   108→      root: opts.root,
   109→      client: opts.client,
   110→      scope: opts.scope,
   111→      projectName: opts.projectName,
   112→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
   113→      rawUploadPermission: opts.rawUploadPermission,
   114→      limit: parseInt(opts.limit, 10) || 0,
   115→      format: opts.format,
   116→    })
   117→  })
   118→
   119→// ── locale ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   120→
   121→program
   122→  .command('locale')
   123→  .description('Detect the effective locale for a repository')
   124→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
   125→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Requested locale value', 'auto')
   126→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   127→  .action(async (opts) => {
   128→    const { cmdLocale } = await import('./cmd-locale.js')
   129→    process.exitCode = cmdLocale({
   130→      root: opts.root,
   131→      locale: opts.locale,
   132→      format: opts.format,
   133→    })
   134→  })
   135→
   136→// ── recall ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   137→
   138→program
   139→  .command('recall')
   140→  .description('On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall')
   141→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
   142→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
   143→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
   144→  .option('--activation-time <time>', 'ISO timestamp of session activation')
   145→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   146→  .action(async (opts) => {
   147→    const { cmdRecall } = await import('./cmd-recall.js')
   148→    process.exitCode = await cmdRecall({
   149→      root: opts.root,
   150→      projectName: opts.projectName,
   151→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
   152→      activationTime: opts.activationTime ?? '',
   153→      format: opts.format,
   154→    })
   155→  })
   156→
   157→// ── daemon ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   158→
   159→const daemon = program
   160→  .command('daemon')
   161→  .description('Manage the MemoryTree heartbeat lifecycle')
   162→
   163→daemon
   164→  .command('install')
   165→  .description('Register heartbeat with the OS scheduler')
   166→  .option('--interval <interval>', 'Override heartbeat interval (e.g., "5m")')
   167→  .option('--auto-push <bool>', 'Override auto_push setting (true/false)')
   168→  .action(async (opts) => {
   169→    const { cmdInstall } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   170→    process.exitCode = cmdInstall({
   171→      interval: opts.interval,
   172→      autoPush: opts.autoPush,
   173→    })
   174→  })
   175→
   176→daemon
   177→  .command('uninstall')
   178→  .description('Remove the heartbeat scheduled task')
   179→  .action(async () => {
   180→    const { cmdUninstall } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   181→    process.exitCode = cmdUninstall()
   182→  })
   183→
   184→daemon
   185→  .command('run-once')
   186→  .description('Execute a single heartbeat cycle now')
   187→  .action(async () => {
   188→    const { cmdRunOnce } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   189→    process.exitCode = await cmdRunOnce()
   190→  })
   191→
   192→daemon
   193→  .command('watch')
   194→  .description('Continuous heartbeat loop (development only)')
   195→  .option('--interval <interval>', 'Override interval')
   196→  .action(async (opts) => {
   197→    const { cmdWatch } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   198→    process.exitCode = await cmdWatch({ interval: opts.interval })
   199→  })
   200→
   201→daemon
   202→  .command('status')
   203→  .description('Show heartbeat registration and lock state')
   204→  .action(async () => {
   205→    const { cmdStatus } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   206→    process.exitCode = cmdStatus()
   207→  })
   208→
   209→// ── report ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   210→
   211→const report = program
   212→  .command('report')
   213→  .description('Generate and serve MemoryTree HTML reports')
   214→
   215→report
   216→  .command('build')
   217→  .description('Build a static HTML report from Memory/ directory')
   218→  .option('--root <path>', 'Repository root (contains Memory/)', '.')
   219→  .option('--output <path>', 'Output directory', './Memory/07_reports')
   220→  .option('--no-ai', 'Skip AI summarization')
   221→  .option('--model <model>', 'AI model for summaries', 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001')
   222→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Report locale: en or zh-CN', 'en')
   223→  .option('--report-base-url <url>', 'Base URL for RSS/OG links')
   224→  .action(async (opts) => {
   225→    const { cmdReportBuild } = await import('./cmd-report.js')
   226→    process.exitCode = await cmdReportBuild({
   227→      root: opts.root,
   228→      output: opts.output,
   229→      noAi: opts.ai === false,
   230→      model: opts.model,
   231→      locale: opts.locale,
   232→      reportBaseUrl: opts.reportBaseUrl,
   233→    })
   234→  })
   235→
   236→report
   237→  .command('serve')
   238→  .description('Serve a built report over HTTP')
   239→  .option('--dir <path>', 'Report directory to serve', './Memory/07_reports')
   240→  .option('--port <n>', 'HTTP port', '3000')
   241→  .action(async (opts) => {
   242→    const { cmdReportServe } = await import('./cmd-report.js')
   243→    process.exitCode = cmdReportServe({
   244→      dir: opts.dir,
   245→      port: parseInt(opts.port, 10) || 3000,
   246→    })
   247→  })
   248→
   249→program.parse()
   250→

以下是项目当前完整状态： --- ## 包结构（5 个包，全部完工） | 包 | 功能 | 测试数 | |----|------|--------| | `@openmnemo/types` | TypeScript 类型定义（Client、ManifestEntry、ParsedTranscript 等） | — | | `@openmnemo/core` | 核心逻辑：transcript 解析/导入/去重、git 工具、sl...

Expand full message

以下是项目当前完整状态：

---

## 包结构（5 个包，全部完工）

| 包 | 功能 | 测试数 |
|----|------|--------|
| @openmnemo/types | TypeScript 类型定义（Client、ManifestEntry、ParsedTranscript 等） | — |
| @openmnemo/core | 核心逻辑：transcript 解析/导入/去重、git 工具、slug、dedup | 223 |
| @openmnemo/report | 静态 HTML 看板生成：图表、i18n、知识图谱、GitHub Pages 部署、webhook | 196 |
| @openmnemo/sync | 心跳 daemon、config.toml 管理、敏感信息扫描、alert 系统 | 71 |
| @openmnemo/cli | 统一命令行入口 | 99 |
| 合计 | | 589 ✅ |

---

## CLI 已有命令

memorytree init               # 初始化 Memory/ 工作区
memorytree upgrade            # 升级已有仓库
memorytree import             # 导入单条 transcript
memorytree discover           # 批量发现并导入本地 AI 对话
memorytree locale             # 检测 locale
memorytree recall             # 按需同步 + 最近会话召回
memorytree daemon install     # 注册系统定时任务（心跳）
memorytree daemon run-once    # 手动执行一次心跳
memorytree daemon status      # 查看心跳状态
memorytree report build       # 从 Memory/ 生成静态 HTML 报告
memorytree report serve       # 本地 HTTP 预览报告

---

## 已支持的 transcript 客户端

claude / codex / gemini / doubao（本轮新增）

---

## 尚未做的（下一步候选）

| 优先级 | 事项 |
|--------|------|
| P0 | npm 发布 — 让用户 npm i -g @openmnemo/cli 直接使用 |
| P0 | GitHub Actions CI — push 自动跑 pnpm test |
| P1 | memorytree import --client doubao 的 Web UI 导出流程文档 |
| P1 | report build 端到端集成测试（真实 Memory/ 目录） |
| P2 | memorytree daemon watch 跨平台完善（目前 Windows Task Scheduler + cron 均支持） |
| P2 | README 用户文档（安装、快速上手、config.toml 示例） |

核心功能已经全部落地，代码有 589 个测试覆盖，质量经过 2 轮 review 并修复。下一步最有价值的是 CI + npm publish，这样外部用户才能真正用起来。

  {
    "text": "完美！现在让我创建一份完整的总结报告。所有信息已收集完毕。\n\n---\n\n## 完整项目探索报告：D:/demo1/openmnemo\n\n### 1. 根目录文件列表及配置文件\n\n**根目录结构：**\n```\nD:/demo1/openmnemo/\n├── .git/\n├── .github/           (已有，含 workflows)\n├── .gitignore\n├── .turbo/\n├── AGENTS.md\n├── LICENSE\n├── Memory/\n├── README.md\n├── deploy/\n├── eslint.config.mjs\n├── node_modules/\n├── package.json       (根配置)\n├── packages/          (5个子包)\n├── pnpm-lock.yaml\n├── pnpm-workspace.yaml (工作区配置)\n├── turbo.json         (Turbo 配置)\n└── chat-memo_61_20260316154801/  (未追踪)\n```\n\n### 2. .github/ 目录及 workflow 文件\n\n已有 GitHub Actions 目录，包含一个 CI 工作流：\n- **文件：** `D:/demo1/openmnemo/.github/workflows/ci.yml`\n- **触发条件：** push to main 或 pull_request to main\n- **矩阵配置：**\n  - OS: ubuntu-latest, windows-latest\n  - Node: 20, 22\n- **流程步骤：**\n  1. checkout@v4\n  2. pnpm/action-setup@v4\n  3. actions/setup-node@v4 (缓存 pnpm)\n  4. pnpm install --frozen-lockfile\n  5. pnpm typecheck\n  6. pnpm lint\n  7. pnpm build\n  8. pnpm test\n\n### 3. turbo.json 中的 build/test pipeline 定义\n\n```json\n{\n  \"tasks\": {\n    \"build\": {\n      \"dependsOn\": [\"^build\"],\n      \"outputs\": [\"dist/**\"]\n    },\n    \"test\": {\n      \"dependsOn\": [\"build\"]\n    },\n    \"lint\": {},\n    \"typecheck\": {\n      \"dependsOn\": [\"^build\"]\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Pipeline 逻辑：**\n- `build`：依赖上游包的 build，输出 dist/**\n- `test`：依赖当前包的 build 完成\n- `lint`：无依赖\n- `typecheck`：依赖上游包的 build\n\n### 4. 根 package.json 的 scripts\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"build\": \"turbo build\",\n    \"test\": \"turbo test\",\n    \"lint\": \"turbo lint\",\n    \"typecheck\": \"turbo typecheck\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 5. 每个包的 build script 和 main 字段\n\n| 包名 | main 字段 | build script | test | lint | typecheck |\n|------|----------|--------------|------|------|-----------|\n| **@openmnemo/types** | `./dist/index.js` | `tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts` | ❌ | ✓ | ✓ |\n| **@openmnemo/core** | `./dist/index.js` | `tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\n| **@openmnemo/report** | `dist/index.js` | `tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts` | ✓ | ❌ | ✓ |\n| **@openmnemo/sync** | `./dist/index.js` | `tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\n| **@openmnemo/cli** | 无（bin: openmnemo） | `tsup` (自定义配置) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |\n\n**CLI 包特殊配置** (`packages/cli/tsup.config.ts`)：\n```typescript\nentry: ['src/main.ts']\nformat: ['esm']\nbanner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }\n```\n\n### 6. Node.js 版本要求\n\n**来源：** 根 `package.json` 的 `engines` 字段\n```json\n\"engines\": {\n  \"node\": \">=20\"\n}\n```\n\n**CI 测试版本：** Node 20 和 22 (矩阵测试)\n\n**未发现：** 无 `.nvmrc` 或 `.node-version` 文件\n\n### 7. pnpm 版本\n\n**来源：** 根 `package.json` 的 `packageManager` 字段\n```json\n\"packageManager\": \"pnpm@9.15.0\"\n```\n\n**工作区配置：** `pnpm-workspace.yaml`\n```yaml\npackages:\n  - \"packages/*\"\n```\n\n**未发现：** 无 `.npmrc` 文件（使用默认 pnpm 配置）\n\n### 8. 额外信息\n\n**项目元信息：**\n- 类型：ESM TypeScript monorepo\n- 构建工具：tsup + Turbo\n- 依赖管理：workspace: * (内部引用)\n- TypeScript：5.7.3 (strict mode)\n- 测试框架：Vitest 3.1.1\n- 代码检查：ESLint 10.0.3 + TypeScript ESLint 8.57.0\n\n**.gitignore 条目：**\n- node_modules/, dist/, *.tsbuildinfo\n- .turbo/, coverage/, .env*\n- Memory/07_reports/, Memory/.report-cache/\n\n**包依赖关系：**\n```\ntypes (基础)\n  ↑\n  core, report\n  ↑\n  sync (依赖 core + report)\n  ↑\n  cli (依赖 core + report + sync)\n```",
    "type": "text"
  },

1→# OpenMnemo
     2→
     3→[![CI](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
     4→[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@openmnemo/cli)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@openmnemo/cli)
     5→
     6→Cross-platform AI conversation memory — aggregate, search, and version-control your coding sessions across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI.
     7→
     8→> Named after Mnemosyne, the Greek goddess of memory.
     9→
    10→## Architecture
    11→
    12→Monorepo powered by pnpm workspaces + Turborepo.
    13→
    14→```
    15→packages/
    16→  types/    @openmnemo/types    — shared TypeScript interfaces
    17→  core/     @openmnemo/core     — transcript parsing, import, dedup, indexing
    18→  sync/     @openmnemo/sync     — heartbeat daemon, config, lock, background sync
    19→  cli/      @openmnemo/cli      — unified CLI (openmnemo command)
    20→```
    21→
    22→## Package Dependency Graph
    23→
    24→```
    25→@openmnemo/types
    26→    ↑
    27→@openmnemo/core  (depends on types)
    28→    ↑
    29→@openmnemo/sync  (depends on types + core)
    30→    ↑

Tool Result #454 Read toolu_01GuR5JDLeLyswiMNxT47Grq 12:40:50
Read output=1→# OpenMnemo 2→ 3→[![CI](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml) 4→[![npm](...
Show output payload 
1→# OpenMnemo
     2→
     3→[![CI](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
     4→[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@openmnemo/cli)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@openmnemo/cli)
     5→
     6→Cross-platform AI conversation memory — aggregate, search, and version-control your coding sessions across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI.
     7→
     8→> Named after Mnemosyne, the Greek goddess of memory.
     9→
    10→## Architecture
    11→
    12→Monorepo powered by pnpm workspaces + Turborepo.
    13→
    14→```
    15→packages/
    16→  types/    @openmnemo/types    — shared TypeScript interfaces
    17→  core/     @openmnemo/core     — transcript parsing, import, dedup, indexing
    18→  sync/     @openmnemo/sync     — heartbeat daemon, config, lock, background sync
    19→  cli/      @openmnemo/cli      — unified CLI (openmnemo command)
    20→```
    21→
    22→## Package Dependency Graph
    23→
    24→```
    25→@openmnemo/types
    26→    ↑
    27→@openmnemo/core  (depends on types)
    28→    ↑
    29→@openmnemo/sync  (depends on types + core)
    30→    ↑
    31→@openmnemo/cli   (depends on types + core + sync)
    32→```
    33→
    34→## Supported Clients
    35→
    36→| Client | Transcript Format | Status |
    37→|--------|------------------|--------|
    38→| Claude Code | JSONL | Planned |
    39→| Codex | JSONL | Planned |
    40→| Gemini CLI | JSON/JSONL | Planned |
    41→
    42→## Install
    43→
    44→```bash
    45→npx @openmnemo/cli --help
    46→# or
    47→npm install -g @openmnemo/cli
    48→```
    49→
    50→## Features
    51→
    52→- Multi-client transcript discovery and incremental import
    53→- Deterministic cleaning (no model tokens spent)
    54→- SQLite full-text search index (sql.js, zero native deps)
    55→- Background heartbeat daemon with OS-native scheduling
    56→- Git-based version control for conversation history
    57→- Cross-session context recall
    58→- Sensitive info detection (warn-only, no auto-redact)
    59→
    60→## Requirements
    61→
    62→- Node.js >= 20
    63→- pnpm >= 9
    64→
    65→## Development
    66→
    67→```bash
    68→pnpm install
    69→pnpm build
    70→pnpm test
    71→```
    72→
    73→## Related
    74→
    75→- [memorytree-workflow](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow) — the Skill that powers MemoryTree project memory (upstream source)
    76→
    77→## License
    78→
    79→MIT
    80→

Write input={"content":"# OpenMnemo\n\n[![CI](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml)\n[...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/README.md",
  "content": "# OpenMnemo\n\n[![CI](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml)\n[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@openmnemo/cli)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@openmnemo/cli)\n\nCross-platform AI conversation memory — aggregate, search, and version-control your coding sessions across Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and Doubao.\n\n> Named after Mnemosyne, the Greek goddess of memory.\n\n## Architecture\n\nMonorepo powered by pnpm workspaces + Turborepo.\n\n```\npackages/\n  types/    @openmnemo/types    — shared TypeScript interfaces\n  core/     @openmnemo/core     — transcript parsing, import, dedup, indexing\n  report/   @openmnemo/report   — static HTML dashboard generation\n  sync/     @openmnemo/sync     — heartbeat daemon, config, lock, background sync\n  cli/      @openmnemo/cli      — unified CLI (openmnemo command)\n```\n\n## Package Dependency Graph\n\n```\n@openmnemo/types\n    ↑              ↑\n@openmnemo/core  @openmnemo/report  (both depend on types)\n    ↑              ↑\n@openmnemo/sync  (depends on types + core + report)\n    ↑\n@openmnemo/cli   (depends on types + core + report + sync)\n```\n\n## Supported Clients\n\n| Client | Transcript Format | Status |\n|--------|------------------|--------|\n| Claude Code | JSONL | ✅ Supported |\n| Codex | JSONL | ✅ Supported |\n| Gemini CLI | JSON/JSONL | ✅ Supported |\n| Doubao | JSON | ✅ Supported |\n\n## Install\n\n```bash\nnpx @openmnemo/cli --help\n# or\nnpm install -g @openmnemo/cli\n```\n\n## Features\n\n- Multi-client transcript discovery and incremental import\n- Deterministic cleaning (no model tokens spent)\n- SQLite full-text search index (sql.js, zero native deps)\n- Background heartbeat daemon with OS-native scheduling\n- Git-based version control for conversation history\n- Cross-session context recall\n- Sensitive info detection (warn-only, no auto-redact)\n- Static HTML dashboard with charts, knowledge graph, and search\n- GitHub Pages deploy + webhook notifications (Feishu / Slack / Discord / Telegram)\n\n## Requirements\n\n- Node.js >= 20\n- pnpm >= 9\n\n## Development\n\n```bash\npnpm install\npnpm build\npnpm test\n```\n\n## Related\n\n- [memorytree-workflow](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow) — the Skill that powers MemoryTree project memory (upstream source)\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n"
}

Bash input={"command":"cd D:/demo1/openmnemo && git add .github/workflows/ci.yml packages/report/package.json packages/report/src/build.ts README.md && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nci: a...

{

---

本次完成的内容：

| 改动 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| CI 步骤重排 | build → typecheck → lint → test，build 先跑一次，后续步骤全走缓存 |
| TURBO_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1" | 抑制 Turbo 在 CI 环境里的交互式提示 |
| report 包补 lint 脚本 | 所有 5 个包现在都有统一的 lint 覆盖 |
| 删除 stale eslint-disable 注释 | build.ts:132 的 no-console 注释已无效，移除避免 CI warning |
| README 更新 | 加入 report/ 包；依赖图补 report 节点；Clients 全部标 ✅；Features 补报告/部署功能 |

有什么想接着做的吗？

状态清晰：所有包都有 files: ["dist"]，CLI 有 bin，缺的是：
1. 各包加 publishConfig: { "access": "public" }（scoped 包必须）
2. GitHub Actions publish workflow（tag 触发）
3. npm token 配置说明

同步处理：

Tool Call #492 Bash toolu_01RNmrD8Tyu8TCogKPmXRCvA 12:46:59
Bash input={"command":"for pkg in types core report sync cli; do\n file=\"D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/$pkg/package.json\"\n # Insert publishConfig before the closing brace (last line)\n # ...
Show input payload 
{
  "command": "for pkg in types core report sync cli; do\n  file=\"D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/$pkg/package.json\"\n  # Insert publishConfig before the closing brace (last line)\n  # Use node to add the field cleanly\n  node -e \"\nconst fs = require('fs');\nconst p = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('$file', 'utf8'));\nif (!p.publishConfig) {\n  p.publishConfig = { access: 'public' };\n  fs.writeFileSync('$file', JSON.stringify(p, null, 2) + '\\n');\n  console.log('updated $pkg');\n} else {\n  console.log('skipped $pkg (already has publishConfig)');\n}\n\"\ndone",
  "description": "Add publishConfig to all packages via node"
}

{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/.github/workflows/publish.yml",
  "content": "name: Publish\n\non:\n  push:\n    tags:\n      - 'v*'\n\njobs:\n  publish:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      id-token: write   # required for npm provenance attestation\n\n    env:\n      TURBO_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: \"1\"\n\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4\n\n      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4\n        with:\n          node-version: 22\n          cache: pnpm\n          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org\n\n      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile\n\n      - run: pnpm build\n\n      - name: Publish all packages\n        run: pnpm publish -r --access public --no-git-checks --provenance\n        env:\n          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n"
}

  "old_string": "## Development\n\n```bash\npnpm install\npnpm build\npnpm test\n```",
  "new_string": "## Development\n\n```bash\npnpm install\npnpm build\npnpm test\n```\n\n## Release\n\nAll packages are versioned together. To publish a new release:\n\n```bash\n# 1. Bump the version in every packages/*/package.json (same version for all)\n# 2. Commit the version bump\ngit add packages/*/package.json\ngit commit -m \"chore: bump version to 0.x.y\"\n\n# 3. Tag and push — GitHub Actions publishes to npm automatically\ngit tag v0.x.y\ngit push origin main --tags\n```\n\n> Requires `NPM_TOKEN` secret set in the repository Settings → Secrets → Actions."
}

assistant #523 12:52:30

失败原因清晰：NODE_AUTH_TOKEN 为空，即 NPM_TOKEN secret 尚未在仓库里配置。Workflow 本身完全正确。

需要你完成一步手动操作：

---

### 配置 NPM_TOKEN

第一步：在 npmjs.com 创建 Access Token

1. 登录 [npmjs.com](https://www.npmjs.com) → 右上角头像 → Access Tokens
2. 点 Generate New Token → 选 Granular Access Token（推荐）或 Automation
3. 权限选 Read and write（发包需要）
4. 复制生成的 token（只显示一次）

第二步：添加到 GitHub 仓库 Secrets

https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/settings/secrets/actions

1. 点 New repository secret
2. Name: NPM_TOKEN
3. Value: 粘贴上一步的 token
4. 点 Add secret

第三步：重跑 publish workflow

cd D:/demo1/openmnemo
gh run rerun 23195049544 --failed

或者重新推一个 tag 触发：

git tag -d v0.2.0 && git push origin :v0.2.0
git tag v0.2.0 && git push origin v0.2.0

---

Token 配置好之后 workflow 会自动把 5 个包（@openmnemo/types, core, report, sync, cli 全部 0.2.0）发布到 npm，附带 provenance attestation。

{
  "description": "Review CI workflows and npm package config (pass 1)",
  "prompt": "You are a senior DevOps/release engineer. Review the CI and npm publish configuration for the openmnemo monorepo at D:/demo1/openmnemo.\n\nRead ALL of the following files:\n1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`\n2. `.github/workflows/publish.yml`\n3. `package.json` (root)\n4. `turbo.json`\n5. `packages/types/package.json`\n6. `packages/core/package.json`\n7. `packages/report/package.json`\n8. `packages/sync/package.json`\n9. `packages/cli/package.json`\n10. `packages/cli/tsup.config.ts` (if exists)\n11. `pnpm-workspace.yaml`\n\nFocus on:\n- CI correctness: will the matrix always pass cleanly? Any race conditions, missing steps, caching issues?\n- Publish workflow correctness: will `pnpm publish -r` correctly resolve `workspace:*` deps to real versions? Any packages that shouldn't be published?\n- Package metadata completeness: `files`, `main`, `types`, `bin`, `engines`, `exports` fields\n- Version consistency across packages\n- Security: secret handling, provenance, permissions\n- Any edge cases that would cause a broken published package (wrong entry point, missing dist files, etc.)\n\nProduce a structured report: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW per finding, file:line for each.",
  "subagent_type": "general-purpose"
}
Tool Call #526 Agent toolu_013QD3bJQEw7T3SW3kEWLpom 12:58:24

Show input payload 
{
  "description": "Review CI and npm packages (pass 2 - consumer perspective)",
  "prompt": "You are a TypeScript package consumer and npm ecosystem expert. Review the npm package configuration for the openmnemo monorepo at D:/demo1/openmnemo from the perspective of someone who would install and use these packages.\n\nRead ALL of the following files:\n1. `packages/types/package.json`\n2. `packages/core/package.json`\n3. `packages/report/package.json`\n4. `packages/sync/package.json`\n5. `packages/cli/package.json`\n6. `packages/types/src/index.ts`\n7. `packages/core/src/index.ts` (check what is exported)\n8. `packages/report/src/index.ts`\n9. `packages/cli/tsup.config.ts` (if exists, otherwise `packages/cli/package.json` build script)\n10. `.github/workflows/publish.yml`\n11. `turbo.json`\n\nFocus on:\n- Are `exports` (package.json `exports` field) configured correctly for ESM consumers? Does the absence of `exports` cause issues with Node.js resolution?\n- Are type definitions (`.d.ts`) correctly pointed to? Will `tsc` be happy consuming these packages?\n- `@openmnemo/sync` and `@openmnemo/report` — should they really be public on npm, or are they internal? What is the user story for installing them directly?\n- Is `@openmnemo/cli` the only package most users need? Is the README clear about this?\n- Are peer dependencies missing (e.g., `@anthropic-ai/sdk` in report — should it be a peer dep instead of a regular dep)?\n- Does `@openmnemo/types` dist/index.js being 0 bytes cause any issues for consumers?\n- Will `npx @openmnemo/cli` work correctly (bin field, shebang, etc.)?\n- Any issues with the `workspace:*` protocol after publishing (pnpm resolves these to exact versions, but are the resolved versions correct)?\n\nProduce a structured report: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW per finding.",
  "subagent_type": "general-purpose"
}
Tool Result #527 Agent toolu_013QD3bJQEw7T3SW3kEWLpom 13:02:56
Agent output=[{"text":"I now have a complete picture of all issues. Here is the full structured report.\n\n---\n\n## npm Package Configuration Review — `@openmnemo` Monorepo\n\n---\n\n### CR...
Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "I now have a complete picture of all issues. Here is the full structured report.\n\n---\n\n## npm Package Configuration Review — `@openmnemo` Monorepo\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL\n\n**1. `@openmnemo/report` — `@anthropic-ai/sdk` should be a peer dependency, not a regular dependency**\n\nFile: `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/package.json`\n\n`@anthropic-ai/sdk` is listed as a regular `dependency` and loaded via a dynamic `await import(\"@anthropic-ai/sdk\")` only when AI summarization is active. Because it is a regular dep, every consumer who installs `@openmnemo/report` (or `@openmnemo/cli`, which depends on report) will pull in the entire Anthropic SDK (~several MB, complex native bindings on some platforms) whether they ever use AI summaries or not. Furthermore, the CLI already passes `--no-ai` as a default-off flag, meaning most users never trigger the code path. The correct pattern is: list it as `peerDependencies` with `peerDependenciesMeta.optional: true`, keep the dynamic import, and let consumers who want AI summaries install the SDK themselves. As-is, this inflates install size for all users and can cause version conflicts if a consuming project already pins a different version of the SDK.\n\n**2. `@openmnemo/cli` — `program.name('memorytree')` and `program.version('0.1.0')` do not match the package**\n\nFile: `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/main.ts` lines 11 and 13\n\nThe CLI binary is registered as `openmnemo` in `package.json`'s `bin` field and the package is `@openmnemo/cli` at version `0.2.0`. But the source hardcodes `.name('memorytree')` and `.version('0.1.0')`. A user who runs `openmnemo --help` sees \"memorytree\", and `openmnemo --version` prints `0.1.0`. This is a direct user-facing inconsistency and will cause confusion. The version should be read from `package.json` at runtime (e.g., via `import { createRequire } from 'module'` or by passing it as a build-time constant via tsup's `define`), and the program name must be `openmnemo`.\n\n**3. `@openmnemo/types` — `dist/index.js` is 0 bytes, yet `\"main\"` points to it**\n\n`D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/types/dist/index.js` is confirmed 0 bytes. This is expected because tsup strips type-only exports to nothing in the JS output. Node.js will import an empty file without error, so runtime usage is fine. However, several tooling scenarios break: bundlers that expect `main` to return something (webpack's `sideEffects` analysis), tools that check whether a package entry resolves to a non-empty file, and any consumer who tries `require.resolve('@openmnemo/types')` as an existence check. More critically, there is no `exports` field, so `package.json` with `\"type\": \"module\"` + `\"main\": \"./dist/index.js\"` is the only resolution path. The correct fix for a pure-types package is either: (a) add an `exports` field with a `\"types\"` condition only, removing the misleading `\"main\"`, or (b) add a trivial `// types only` comment line in `dist/index.js` so the 0-byte file is intentional and documented. The `\"types\"` field pointing to `dist/index.d.ts` is fine — that file has content (1178 bytes).\n\n---\n\n### HIGH\n\n**4. No `exports` field on any package — Node.js subpath and conditional export resolution is broken**\n\nFiles: all five `package.json` files under `packages/`\n\nNone of the packages define an `\"exports\"` field. Without `\"exports\"`, Node.js (v12+) and modern bundlers use `\"main\"` as a fallback, which works for the single entry point. But this means:\n- Consumers cannot use `\"imports\"` conditions (e.g., `\"types\"`, `\"import\"`, `\"default\"`) that TypeScript 5.x expects when `moduleResolution: \"bundler\"` or `\"node16\"` is set.\n- Deep imports like `import { something } from '@openmnemo/core/transcript/parse'` are not blocked, allowing accidental coupling to internal paths.\n- TypeScript with `\"moduleResolution\": \"node16\"` or `\"nodenext\"` will fail to find types unless `\"exports\"` has a `\"types\"` condition.\n\nMinimum fix: add `\"exports\": { \".\": { \"import\": \"./dist/index.js\", \"types\": \"./dist/index.d.ts\" } }` to all packages. For `@openmnemo/types`, the entry should only have `\"types\"`.\n\n**5. `@openmnemo/sync` and `@openmnemo/report` have no credible standalone user story, yet are published as public packages**\n\nBoth packages' `description` fields describe internal subsystems (\"heartbeat daemon\", \"report generation\"). There is no install-and-use story independent of the CLI. Publishing them publicly creates a fragmented surface: users who stumble upon `npm i @openmnemo/sync` get a daemon management API with no documentation or stable interface promise. The recommendation is to mark these `\"private\": true` or add `\"publishConfig\": { \"access\": \"restricted\" }` unless there is a deliberate extension API intent. If they must be public (for the programmatic API use case), they each need a `CHANGELOG` and clear semver commitment.\n\n**6. No `engines` field in any individual package — consumers on Node 18 may silently get broken behavior**\n\nFile: root `package.json` has `\"engines\": { \"node\": \">=20\" }`, but none of the published packages declare this. When a user installs `@openmnemo/cli` on Node 18, npm will not warn them. The CLI uses `sql.js`, dynamic imports with top-level `await` patterns, and commander v13 — all of which require Node 18+, but some patterns (notably `import.meta.resolve` usage if present) require Node 20+. Each published package should declare `\"engines\": { \"node\": \">=20\" }` so npm warns users at install time.\n\n**7. Publish workflow skips tests**\n\nFile: `D:/demo1/openmnemo/.github/workflows/publish.yml`\n\nThe publish workflow runs only `pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm publish`. It does not run `pnpm test` or `pnpm typecheck` before publishing. The CI workflow does run tests, but CI only triggers on `push` to `main` and `pull_request` — not on tag pushes. A tag push triggers only the publish workflow, which has no test gate. A broken build could be published. Add `pnpm test && pnpm typecheck` before the publish step, or add a `needs: ci` dependency on a separate test job.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM\n\n**8. `workspace:*` resolves to exact version at publish time — correct behavior, but no version range strategy**\n\npnpm 9 correctly rewrites `workspace:*` to `\"0.2.0\"` (exact) when publishing. Since all packages are at `0.2.0`, the published `@openmnemo/cli@0.2.0` will declare `\"@openmnemo/core\": \"0.2.0\"` as an exact dep. This means if `@openmnemo/core` ships a patch fix as `0.2.1`, consumers who install `@openmnemo/cli@0.2.0` will not get the fix unless CLI is also republished. Consider using `workspace:^` instead of `workspace:*` in package manifests, which pnpm rewrites to `\"^0.2.0\"`, allowing patch/minor updates to propagate independently. This matters most for `@openmnemo/types` and `@openmnemo/core` since they are the most likely to receive fixes.\n\n**9. `@openmnemo/cli` — content-hashed chunk names in `dist/` are fragile stale artifacts**\n\n`D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/dist/` contains 25 files including chunks named `cmd-daemon-7WZ6I4D3.js`, `cmd-daemon-CEF5XYKO.js`, `cmd-daemon-NH56PJ52.js` — three different builds of the same source file with different content hashes. This indicates `dist/` contains outputs from multiple separate build runs that were never cleaned. The `files: [\"dist\"]` field will publish all 25 files including the stale duplicates, bloating the package by ~3x. The fix is to add a `clean` step (`rm -rf dist`) before each build in CI and locally. In the publish workflow, `pnpm build` should be preceded by `pnpm --filter '*' exec -- rm -rf dist` or turbo's `outputs` cache should be invalidated. The correct chunk count should be ~10 files, not 25.\n\n**10. `@openmnemo/core` — `CLIENTS` set is out of sync with `Client` type**\n\n`D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/transcript/common.ts` line 15: `CLIENTS = new Set(['codex', 'claude', 'gemini'])` — `'doubao'` is missing. The `Client` type in `@openmnemo/types` includes `'doubao'`, but the runtime constant set used for validation/inference does not. Any code path that checks `CLIENTS.has(client)` will fail to recognize doubao sessions as valid. This is a data integrity bug for users importing doubao transcripts.\n\n**11. `@openmnemo/cli` has no `main` or `exports` field — programmatic use is impossible**\n\nThis is less critical for a pure CLI tool, but the absence of `main`/`exports` means the package cannot be `import`-ed programmatically. If a user wants to embed the CLI logic in their own Node.js script, there is no entry point. This is acceptable if CLI-only usage is the intent — but it should be documented explicitly. As-is, the package gives no signal either way.\n\n**12. `@openmnemo/sync` exports internal implementation details**\n\n`D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/index.ts` exports `_resetLogger`, `lockPath`, `acquireLock`, `releaseLock`, `readLockPid`, `isProcessAlive` — internal daemon infrastructure. Exporting `_resetLogger` (note the underscore prefix convention indicating internal) as part of the public API is a semver commitment. If these internals change, it is a breaking change. Either mark these with `@internal` JSDoc and strip them from the public types, or keep them private via a separate `/internal` subpath export.\n\n---\n\n### LOW\n\n**13. No `README` in any individual package**\n\nNone of the five packages have a `README.md` in their directory. npm displays the README on the package page. Without it, the npmjs.com page for each package will show the root repository README, which may not be package-specific. This affects discoverability and usability on npm.\n\n**14. `@openmnemo/report` build produces no tsup config — default options may produce suboptimal output**\n\nThe report package uses `tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts` inline in the build script with no `tsup.config.ts`. The CLI has a `tsup.config.ts` with a shebang banner. For `report`, there is no control over whether `node_modules` deps are externalized or bundled. Given `@anthropic-ai/sdk` is in `dependencies`, tsup's default behavior (bundle everything) would inline the entire SDK into `dist/index.js`, producing a very large file. Check this explicitly and add a `tsup.config.ts` for report with `external: ['@anthropic-ai/sdk']` if it should remain an npm dependency.\n\n**15. `npx @openmnemo/cli` works correctly — no issues found**\n\nThe shebang `#!/usr/bin/env node` is correctly injected by tsup's `banner.js` option. The `bin` field points to `./dist/main.js` which exists and starts with the shebang. The `files: [\"dist\"]` field will include the file. ESM is handled correctly because `\"type\": \"module\"` is set. The chunk files are co-located in `dist/` and referenced by relative paths, so they will all be published together. `npx @openmnemo/cli` will work on first invocation.\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | Package(s) | Issue |\n|---|----------|-----------|-------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | report | `@anthropic-ai/sdk` must be optional peer dep |\n| 2 | CRITICAL | cli | Hardcoded `name('memorytree')` and `version('0.1.0')` mismatch |\n| 3 | CRITICAL | types | `dist/index.js` is 0 bytes; `\"main\"` is misleading |\n| 4 | HIGH | all | No `\"exports\"` field; breaks `node16`/`nodenext` moduleResolution |\n| 5 | HIGH | sync, report | No standalone user story; should not be public packages |\n| 6 | HIGH | all | Missing `\"engines\"` in published packages |\n| 7 | HIGH | CI/CD | Publish workflow skips `pnpm test` and `pnpm typecheck` |\n| 8 | MEDIUM | all | `workspace:*` → exact version; use `workspace:^` for flexibility |\n| 9 | MEDIUM | cli | Stale duplicate hashed chunks inflate published package 3x |\n| 10 | MEDIUM | core | `CLIENTS` set missing `'doubao'`; breaks runtime validation |\n| 11 | MEDIUM | cli | No `main`/`exports` field signals intent ambiguously |\n| 12 | MEDIUM | sync | `_resetLogger` and lock internals exported as public API |\n| 13 | LOW | all | No per-package `README.md` for npm pages |\n| 14 | LOW | report | No `tsup.config.ts`; `@anthropic-ai/sdk` may be bundled inline |\n| 15 | LOW | cli | `npx @openmnemo/cli` — works correctly, no issues |",
    "type": "text"
  },

    "type": "text",

  }

Agent output=[{"text":"I now have a complete picture of the configuration. Here is the full structured report.\n\n---\n\n## CI & Publish Configuration Review — `openmnemo` Monorepo\n\n---\n\...
Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "I now have a complete picture of the configuration. Here is the full structured report.\n\n---\n\n## CI & Publish Configuration Review — `openmnemo` Monorepo\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL\n\n**C-1: CLI binary ships stale branding — `openmnemo` binary runs `memorytree` program**\n`packages/cli/src/main.ts:11-13`\n\nThe published binary is named `openmnemo` (via `bin` in `package.json`), but the Commander program is hardcoded as `memorytree` with description \"MemoryTree — transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity\". Every user who runs `openmnemo --help` sees \"memorytree\", every error message says \"memorytree\", and the hardcoded version string on line 13 is `0.1.0` while the package version is `0.2.0`. The version shown by `openmnemo --version` will be wrong.\n\nFix: change `.name('memorytree')` to `.name('openmnemo')`, update the description, and replace the hardcoded `.version('0.1.0')` with a dynamic read from the package's own `version` field (e.g., via `createRequire` or import of `package.json`).\n\n---\n\n**C-2: Publish workflow runs no tests — broken code can be released to npm**\n`.github/workflows/publish.yml:29-36`\n\nThe publish job does `pnpm install → pnpm build → pnpm publish`. There is no `pnpm test` or `pnpm typecheck` step. A tag push triggered after a breaking commit (even one that CI caught on a branch but was force-pushed) will publish a broken package.\n\nFix: add `- run: pnpm test` and `- run: pnpm typecheck` steps between build and publish. Alternatively require CI to pass on the tagged commit via a `needs:` dependency on a separate `ci` job, or use a GitHub environment with a required status check.\n\n---\n\n**C-3: No tag-to-version consistency check — tag `v0.3.0` can publish packages at `0.2.0`**\n`.github/workflows/publish.yml` (entire file)\n\nThe tag pattern `v*` triggers the publish workflow, but nothing verifies that the tag matches the version in `package.json`. If someone forgets to bump the version before tagging, npm will reject the publish (version already exists). Conversely, if packages are already bumped to `0.3.0` but the tag is `v0.2.5`, pnpm will silently publish `0.3.0` under the tag trigger — or fail with a conflict.\n\nFix: add a pre-publish validation step:\n```bash\nTAG_VERSION=\"${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}\"\nPKG_VERSION=$(node -p \"require('./packages/cli/package.json').version\")\nif [ \"$TAG_VERSION\" != \"$PKG_VERSION\" ]; then echo \"Tag/version mismatch\"; exit 1; fi\n```\n\n---\n\n### HIGH\n\n**H-1: `workspace:*` resolves to an exact version on publish, not `^version`**\n`packages/core/package.json:31`, `packages/report/package.json:30`, `packages/sync/package.json:30-32`, `packages/cli/package.json:33-36`\n\npnpm replaces `workspace:*` with the exact version at publish time (e.g., `\"0.2.0\"`, not `\"^0.2.0\"`). This means a consumer who installs `@openmnemo/cli@0.2.0` will have a peer lock to `@openmnemo/core@0.2.0` exactly. If a patch `@openmnemo/core@0.2.1` is released independently, npm will not automatically upgrade it; consumers must manually bump `@openmnemo/cli`. For a monorepo that always releases all packages in lockstep this is acceptable, but it should be a conscious decision.\n\nFix: use `workspace:^` instead of `workspace:*` for inter-package dependencies, or document the \"lockstep release only\" policy explicitly.\n\n---\n\n**H-2: `@types/node@^25.5.0` used in all packages while `engines` targets Node >=20**\n`packages/core/package.json:36`, `packages/report/package.json:36`, `packages/sync/package.json:36`, `packages/cli/package.json:36`\n\nNode 25 type definitions expose APIs that do not exist in Node 20 or 22 (e.g., newer `fs`, `crypto`, `stream` overloads). TypeScript will not warn if code uses a Node 25-only API when `@types/node@25` is the source of truth. The CI matrix tests on Node 20 and 22, but the type checker is satisfied by Node 25 types — so type checking passes even if the code would crash at runtime on Node 20.\n\nFix: align `@types/node` to the minimum supported version: `\"@types/node\": \"^20.0.0\"`.\n\n---\n\n**H-3: `turbo test` is cached by default — stale test results can silently mask failures**\n`turbo.json:8-10`\n\nTurbo caches all tasks by default. Since the `test` task has no `cache: false` and no explicit `outputs`, Turbo will consider tests \"passed\" from cache when inputs haven't changed. This is normally fine, but in the publish workflow (which builds fresh) the absence of a test run combined with turbo cache means tests may not re-execute if the dist is bit-for-bit identical to a previous cached run. More importantly in CI, if a non-deterministic test passes on the first run it will be eternally \"green\" from cache even after unrelated dependency changes.\n\nFix: add `\"cache\": false` to the `test` task in `turbo.json`, or add `\"outputs\": []` with inputs narrowed to source files only.\n\n---\n\n**H-4: All GitHub Actions are pinned to mutable version tags, not SHAs**\n`.github/workflows/ci.yml:21-27`, `.github/workflows/publish.yml:19-27`\n\n`actions/checkout@v4`, `pnpm/action-setup@v4`, and `actions/setup-node@v4` are all referenced by mutable tags. A compromised or mis-tagged release of any of these actions would silently affect all CI runs, including the publish job that writes to npm with `id-token: write` permission.\n\nFix: pin each action to a specific commit SHA. For example:\n```yaml\nuses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af68  # v4.2.2\n```\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM\n\n**M-1: No `engines` field in any published package**\n`packages/types/package.json`, `packages/core/package.json`, `packages/report/package.json`, `packages/sync/package.json`, `packages/cli/package.json` (all missing `engines`)\n\nThe root `package.json` declares `\"engines\": { \"node\": \">=20\" }` but none of the published packages inherit or repeat this. npm will display no engine constraint to consumers, who may install on Node 16/18 and get cryptic runtime errors. The `engines` field in the root of a monorepo does not propagate to published packages.\n\nFix: add `\"engines\": { \"node\": \">=20\" }` to every package's `package.json`.\n\n---\n\n**M-2: No `exports` field in any package — dual-mode resolution is broken for bundlers**\nAll five `packages/*/package.json` files\n\nModern bundlers (Webpack 5, Vite, esbuild), Jest/Vitest with `moduleNameMapper`, and Node's native ESM loader all prefer the `exports` field over `main`/`types`. Without it, deep-import subpath access is unrestricted (any file in `dist/` is importable), and TypeScript 4.7+ with `moduleResolution: bundler` or `nodenext` will fail to resolve types correctly in some tool chains.\n\nFix: add a minimal `exports` block to each library package:\n```json\n\"exports\": {\n  \".\": {\n    \"import\": \"./dist/index.js\",\n    \"types\": \"./dist/index.d.ts\"\n  }\n}\n```\nFor `@openmnemo/cli` (binary-only), `exports` can be omitted or set to `\".\": \"./dist/main.js\"` if programmatic import is ever intended.\n\n---\n\n**M-3: `@openmnemo/report` `main`/`types` paths lack the leading `./` unlike all other packages**\n`packages/report/package.json:7-8`\n\nAll other packages use `\"./dist/index.js\"` (POSIX relative). `@openmnemo/report` uses `\"dist/index.js\"` (bare relative). Both forms are valid per the npm spec, but the inconsistency could confuse tooling that parses these paths and applies path.resolve logic differently across platforms.\n\nFix: change to `\"./dist/index.js\"` and `\"./dist/index.d.ts\"` to match the rest of the monorepo.\n\n---\n\n**M-4: `tsconfig.json` strictness is inconsistent across packages**\n`packages/report/tsconfig.json:7-8` vs all others\n\n`@openmnemo/report` has `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true` and `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`. No other package does. Code in `@openmnemo/core`, `@openmnemo/sync`, and `@openmnemo/cli` that consumes `@openmnemo/report`'s types will silently skip these extra checks, meaning type bugs caught in `report` could be introduced by callers in other packages.\n\nFix: add `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true` and `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true` to all package `tsconfig.json` files, or extract a shared `tsconfig.base.json` at the root.\n\n---\n\n**M-5: `@openmnemo/cli` tsup config produces no `.d.ts` files**\n`packages/cli/tsup.config.ts:3-9`\n\nThe CLI tsup config has no `dts: true`. While `@openmnemo/cli` is primarily a binary, its `package.json` has no `private: true` and is published. Any consumer who attempts to `import` from `@openmnemo/cli` programmatically will get no TypeScript types. Combined with the absence of a `main` field, programmatic import is entirely unguarded.\n\nFix: if `@openmnemo/cli` is binary-only, mark it `\"private\": false` (already) but document that no programmatic API is guaranteed. If programmatic use is intended, add `dts: true` to `tsup.config.ts` and add `main`/`types`/`exports` fields.\n\n---\n\n**M-6: Publish workflow does not verify `NPM_TOKEN` secret is set**\n`.github/workflows/publish.yml:36`\n\nIf `secrets.NPM_TOKEN` is not configured on the repository, the publish step will fail with a cryptic auth error after the build has already completed. There is no early validation that the secret exists.\n\nFix: add an early check step:\n```yaml\n- name: Verify NPM_TOKEN\n  run: |\n    if [ -z \"$NODE_AUTH_TOKEN\" ]; then\n      echo \"NPM_TOKEN secret is not set\"; exit 1\n    fi\n  env:\n    NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n```\n\n---\n\n**M-7: No Dependabot configuration for GitHub Actions or npm dependencies**\n`.github/` (missing `dependabot.yml`)\n\nThere is no Dependabot or Renovate config. Action versions (`@v4` tags) and package versions will silently go stale. Given that actions are pinned to mutable tags (H-4), this compounds the risk.\n\nFix: add `.github/dependabot.yml` with both `package-ecosystem: npm` for the workspace and `package-ecosystem: github-actions` for the workflows.\n\n---\n\n### LOW\n\n**L-1: `turbo.json` has no `globalEnv` for `NODE_ENV` or similar — cache may be non-deterministic**\n`turbo.json` (entire file)\n\nIf any package reads `process.env.NODE_ENV` at build time (e.g., for conditional dev/prod output), Turbo will not factor that into the cache key. A CI build with `NODE_ENV=test` and a local build with `NODE_ENV=development` would share a cache hit.\n\nFix: add `\"globalEnv\": [\"NODE_ENV\"]` (and any other env vars that affect build output) to `turbo.json`.\n\n---\n\n**L-2: `@openmnemo/types` has no `test` script — `turbo test` silently no-ops for it**\n`packages/types/package.json:23-27`\n\nTurbo's `test` task runs `npm run test` per package. `@openmnemo/types` has no `test` script, so Turbo skips it with a warning (or silently depending on version). If type-only utility functions are added to `types/`, there is no test harness available and no reminder to add one.\n\nFix: add `\"test\": \"vitest run\"` to `packages/types/package.json` and create a `tests/` directory with at least a smoke test.\n\n---\n\n**L-3: CI matrix includes Windows but no cross-platform path handling is verified**\n`.github/workflows/ci.yml:14`\n\nThe matrix runs on `windows-latest`. The `fast-glob` package normalizes separators, but `path.join` calls in `@openmnemo/core` and `@openmnemo/sync` may produce `\\`-separated paths on Windows that are then compared against POSIX patterns. If tests are written to expect `/` separators this would fail on Windows CI specifically.\n\nThis is a latent risk — whether it actually manifests depends on the test implementations. Worth auditing path comparison logic in `packages/core/src/` and `packages/sync/src/`.\n\n---\n\n**L-4: `pnpm/action-setup@v4` without explicit `version:` reads from `packageManager` field**\n`.github/workflows/ci.yml:23`, `.github/workflows/publish.yml:21`\n\nThis is actually correct behavior for `pnpm/action-setup@v4`, which automatically reads the `packageManager` field from `package.json` (`pnpm@9.15.0`). No issue here, but it is worth noting that if the `packageManager` field is ever removed or renamed, both workflows will silently fall back to whatever pnpm version the action defaults to.\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| ID | Severity | File | Issue |\n|----|----------|------|-------|\n| C-1 | CRITICAL | `packages/cli/src/main.ts:11-13` | Binary shows wrong name (`memorytree`) and version (`0.1.0`) |\n| C-2 | CRITICAL | `publish.yml:29-36` | No tests run before publish |\n| C-3 | CRITICAL | `publish.yml` (whole file) | Tag/version not validated before publish |\n| H-1 | HIGH | All `packages/*/package.json` deps | `workspace:*` publishes as exact version, not `^` |\n| H-2 | HIGH | All `packages/*/package.json` devDeps | `@types/node@^25.5.0` vs `engines: >=20` |\n| H-3 | HIGH | `turbo.json:8-10` | Tests cached by default — stale results hide failures |\n| H-4 | HIGH | Both workflow files | Actions pinned to mutable tags, not SHAs |\n| M-1 | MEDIUM | All `packages/*/package.json` | No `engines` field in published packages |\n| M-2 | MEDIUM | All `packages/*/package.json` | No `exports` field — bundler resolution broken |\n| M-3 | MEDIUM | `packages/report/package.json:7-8` | `main`/`types` paths missing leading `./` |\n| M-4 | MEDIUM | All `packages/*/tsconfig.json` | Strict flags inconsistent across packages |\n| M-5 | MEDIUM | `packages/cli/tsup.config.ts` | No `dts: true` — no types emitted for CLI |\n| M-6 | MEDIUM | `publish.yml:36` | No early validation that `NPM_TOKEN` is set |\n| M-7 | MEDIUM | `.github/` | No Dependabot config |\n| L-1 | LOW | `turbo.json` | No `globalEnv` for env-sensitive cache invalidation |\n| L-2 | LOW | `packages/types/package.json` | No `test` script in `@openmnemo/types` |\n| L-3 | LOW | `ci.yml:14` | Windows matrix may expose path separator issues |\n| L-4 | LOW | Both workflow files | `pnpm/action-setup` version is implicit via `packageManager` |",

assistant #529 13:05:27

两个问题先回答，然后展示 review 报告。 --- **`gh run rerun` 显示 ✓ 是正确的** — 它只表示"已提交重跑请求"，不代表 job 本身成功。 **E403 的原因：** npm 账号开启了 2FA，用了不支持绕过 2FA 的 token 类型。修复方法： > npmjs.com → Access Tokens → **Generate New Token** → 选 **Classic Token*...

Expand full message

两个问题先回答，然后展示 review 报告。

---

gh run rerun 显示 ✓ 是正确的 — 它只表示"已提交重跑请求"，不代表 job 本身成功。

E403 的原因： npm 账号开启了 2FA，用了不支持绕过 2FA 的 token 类型。修复方法：

> npmjs.com → Access Tokens → Generate New Token → 选 Classic Token → 类型选 Automation（Automation token 专为 CI 设计，自动绕过 2FA）→ 复制 → 替换 GitHub Secrets 里的 NPM_TOKEN

---

两轮 review 完成，合并结果如下：

## Review 汇总

### CRITICAL（必须在发布前修复）

| # | 文件 | 问题 |
|---|------|------|
| C-1 | packages/cli/src/main.ts:11,13 | program.name('memorytree') + program.version('0.1.0') — 二进制名是 openmnemo 但 --help/--version 显示旧品牌和错误版本号 |
| C-2 | publish.yml | publish workflow 没有跑测试，坏代码可以直接发到 npm |
| C-3 | publish.yml | tag 与 package.json 版本号没有一致性校验，打错 tag 会发错版本 |
| C-4 | packages/core/src/transcript/common.ts | CLIENTS set 没有 'doubao'，凡是 CLIENTS.has(client) 的验证路径都会把 doubao 当无效客户端 |

### HIGH

| # | 问题 |
|---|------|
| H-1 | @anthropic-ai/sdk 是 dependencies 而非 optional peer dep — 所有安装 CLI 的用户都会拉取整个 SDK，即使 --no-ai |
| H-2 | publish.yml 跑测试前没有 clean dist，CLI dist/ 里有多次构建产生的同名 hash chunk，会把 3 倍大小的包发到 npm |
| H-3 | 所有包缺少 engines: { "node": ">=20" } — npm 不会在 Node 18 上给用户警告 |
| H-4 | 所有包缺少 exports 字段 — TypeScript moduleResolution: node16/nodenext 无法正确解析类型 |
| H-5 | @types/node@^25.5.0 但 engines: >=20，类型检查通过却可能用到 Node 25 独有 API |
| H-6 | GitHub Actions 用 mutable tag（@v4）而非 commit SHA，存在供应链安全风险 |

### MEDIUM

| # | 问题 |
|---|------|
| M-1 | workspace:* 发布后变成精确版本锁，建议改 workspace:^ 允许 patch 自动升级 |
| M-2 | packages/report/package.json 的 main/types 路径缺 ./ 前缀 |
| M-3 | TypeScript strict flags 各包不一致（report 有 noUncheckedIndexedAccess，其他没有） |
| M-4 | @openmnemo/sync 导出 _resetLogger 等内部实现，不应作为公开 API |
| M-5 | @openmnemo/report 没有 tsup.config.ts，@anthropic-ai/sdk 可能被打包进 dist |
| M-6 | publish workflow 没有提前校验 NPM_TOKEN 是否设置 |

---

现在修复所有 CRITICAL 和 HIGH 问题，你是否要我全部一次修复？

user #530 13:06:16

修复

1→/**
     2→ * Shared constants, data-access helpers, text-extraction utilities, and dedup.
     3→ * Port of scripts/_transcript_common.py
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { createHash } from 'node:crypto'
     7→import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     8→
     9→import type { TranscriptMessage, TranscriptToolEvent } from '@openmnemo/types'
    10→
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→// Constants
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→
    15→export const CLIENTS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['codex', 'claude', 'gemini'])
    16→export const TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['input_text', 'output_text', 'text'])
    17→export const SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['thinking', 'reasoning'])
    18→export const TOOL_USE_TYPES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['tool_use'])
    19→export const TOOL_RESULT_TYPES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['tool_result'])
    20→
    21→const ALL_SKIP_TYPES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
    22→  ...SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES, ...TOOL_USE_TYPES, ...TOOL_RESULT_TYPES,
    23→])
    24→
    25→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→// Generic utilities
    27→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→
    29→export function slugify(value: string, fallback = 'session'): string {
    30→  const asciiValue = value.replace(/[^\x00-\x7F]/g, '').toLowerCase()

Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/main.ts",
  "old_string": "/**\n * MemoryTree CLI — unified entry point.\n * Registers all subcommands via commander.\n */\n\nimport { Command } from 'commander'\n\nconst program = new Command()\n\nprogram\n  .name('memorytree')\n  .description('MemoryTree — transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity')\n  .version('0.1.0')",
  "new_string": "/**\n * OpenMnemo CLI — unified entry point.\n * Registers all subcommands via commander.\n */\n\nimport { createRequire } from 'node:module'\nimport { Command } from 'commander'\n\nconst _require = createRequire(import.meta.url)\nconst { version: cliVersion } = _require('../package.json') as { version: string }\n\nconst program = new Command()\n\nprogram\n  .name('openmnemo')\n  .description('OpenMnemo — cross-platform AI conversation memory manager')\n  .version(cliVersion)"
}

Tool Call #550 Bash toolu_01B4qKWdUZLHGU4JUbhw46ei 13:08:49
Bash input={"command":"node -e \"\nconst fs = require('fs')\nconst path = require('path')\nconst root = 'D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages'\n\nfunction write(file, obj) {\n fs.writeFileSync(file...
Show input payload 
{
  "command": "node -e \"\nconst fs = require('fs')\nconst path = require('path')\nconst root = 'D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages'\n\nfunction write(file, obj) {\n  fs.writeFileSync(file, JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2) + '\\n')\n}\n\n// ── types ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n{\n  const f = path.join(root, 'types/package.json')\n  const p = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(f, 'utf8'))\n  p.engines = { node: '>=20' }\n  p.exports = { '.': { types: './dist/index.d.ts', import: './dist/index.js' } }\n  write(f, p)\n  console.log('types ✓')\n}\n\n// ── core ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n{\n  const f = path.join(root, 'core/package.json')\n  const p = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(f, 'utf8'))\n  p.engines = { node: '>=20' }\n  p.exports = { '.': { types: './dist/index.d.ts', import: './dist/index.js' } }\n  p.devDependencies['@types/node'] = '^20.0.0'\n  write(f, p)\n  console.log('core ✓')\n}\n\n// ── report ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n{\n  const f = path.join(root, 'report/package.json')\n  const p = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(f, 'utf8'))\n  p.main = './dist/index.js'   // fix missing ./\n  p.types = './dist/index.d.ts'\n  p.engines = { node: '>=20' }\n  p.exports = { '.': { types: './dist/index.d.ts', import: './dist/index.js' } }\n  // Move SDK to optional peer dep\n  delete p.dependencies['@anthropic-ai/sdk']\n  p.peerDependencies = { '@anthropic-ai/sdk': '^0.39.0' }\n  p.peerDependenciesMeta = { '@anthropic-ai/sdk': { optional: true } }\n  p.devDependencies['@anthropic-ai/sdk'] = '^0.39.0'\n  p.devDependencies['@types/node'] = '^20.0.0'\n  write(f, p)\n  console.log('report ✓')\n}\n\n// ── sync ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n{\n  const f = path.join(root, 'sync/package.json')\n  const p = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(f, 'utf8'))\n  p.engines = { node: '>=20' }\n  p.exports = { '.': { types: './dist/index.d.ts', import: './dist/index.js' } }\n  p.devDependencies['@types/node'] = '^20.0.0'\n  write(f, p)\n  console.log('sync ✓')\n}\n\n// ── cli ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n{\n  const f = path.join(root, 'cli/package.json')\n  const p = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(f, 'utf8'))\n  p.engines = { node: '>=20' }\n  // CLI installs SDK for users who want AI features\n  p.dependencies['@anthropic-ai/sdk'] = '^0.39.0'\n  p.devDependencies['@types/node'] = '^20.0.0'\n  write(f, p)\n  console.log('cli ✓')\n}\n\"\n",
  "description": "Batch update all package.json: engines, exports, @types/node, SDK peer dep"
}

Now rewrite the publish workflow with all safety checks:

Tool Call #553 Write toolu_01RHSVguZA8Y6j6aDwXamSyE 13:09:04
Write input={"content":"name: Publish\n\non:\n push:\n tags:\n - 'v*'\n\njobs:\n publish:\n runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n permissions:\n contents: read\n id-token: write # required for npm prove...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:/demo1/openmnemo/.github/workflows/publish.yml",
  "content": "name: Publish\n\non:\n  push:\n    tags:\n      - 'v*'\n\njobs:\n  publish:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      id-token: write   # required for npm provenance attestation\n\n    env:\n      TURBO_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: \"1\"\n\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4\n\n      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4\n        with:\n          node-version: 22\n          cache: pnpm\n          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org\n\n      - name: Verify tag matches package version\n        run: |\n          TAG_VERSION=\"${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}\"\n          PKG_VERSION=$(jq -r .version packages/cli/package.json)\n          if [ \"$TAG_VERSION\" != \"$PKG_VERSION\" ]; then\n            echo \"❌ Tag $GITHUB_REF_NAME does not match package version $PKG_VERSION\"\n            exit 1\n          fi\n          echo \"✅ Tag matches package version: $PKG_VERSION\"\n\n      - name: Verify NPM_TOKEN is set\n        run: |\n          if [ -z \"$NODE_AUTH_TOKEN\" ]; then\n            echo \"❌ NPM_TOKEN secret is not configured in repository settings\"\n            exit 1\n          fi\n          echo \"✅ NPM_TOKEN is present\"\n        env:\n          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n\n      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile\n\n      - run: pnpm build\n\n      - run: pnpm typecheck\n\n      - run: pnpm test\n\n      - name: Publish all packages\n        run: pnpm publish -r --access public --no-git-checks --provenance\n        env:\n          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}\n"
}
