Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "Now I have a complete picture of all the changes. Here is the full review.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: Search Feature\n\n### Summary of Changes\n\n- `packages/core/src/transcript/db.ts` — Added `SearchResult` interface, `CREATE_FTS_SQL`, `REBUILD_FTS_SQL`, `SEARCH_SQL` constants, and `searchTranscripts()` function. FTS5 was changed to FTS4 with `content=transcripts`.\n- `packages/core/tests/transcript/db.test.ts` — Added `searchTranscripts` describe block with 6 tests.\n- `packages/cli/src/cmd-search.ts` — New file, `cmdSearch()` and `formatSearchText()`.\n- `packages/cli/src/main.ts` — Registered `search` subcommand.\n- `README.md` — Documented the `search` command.\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL\n\n**1. FTS4 `tokenize=unicode61` is silently unsupported in sql.js (db.ts line 111)**\n\nFTS4 only ships with the `simple`, `porter`, and `icu` tokenizers. The `unicode61` tokenizer is an FTS5 addition. The sql.js WASM build bundles SQLite without the `unicode61` extension, so the `CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE` call will silently succeed (SQLite falls back to `simple`) or throw a runtime error depending on the build — neither outcome is documented or tested.\n\nAction: Either remove `tokenize=unicode61` to use the `simple` default, or migrate back to FTS5 if the sql.js build supports it. The switch from FTS5 to FTS4 was presumably made because sql.js's default WASM build omits the FTS5 module; that should be confirmed and documented in a comment. There is no test covering the case where `tokenize=unicode61` is rejected.\n\n**2. `searchTranscripts` runs `CREATE_FTS` and `REBUILD_FTS` on every search call — potentially catastrophic on a large index (db.ts lines 145-146)**\n\n`REBUILD_FTS` causes a full re-index of the entire `transcripts` table on each call to `searchTranscripts`. For a 10,000-row database this blocks the event loop for seconds and bloats WAL. Moving the FTS table creation to `upsertSearchIndex` (triggered only when data changes) and removing the unconditional `REBUILD_FTS` from `searchTranscripts` would be the correct fix. Alternatively, use an FTS with `content=\"\"` (external content) and rebuild only in the write path.\n\n**3. Raw user query passed directly to `MATCH` without sanitization — query injection (db.ts line 151)**\n\nFTS4's `MATCH` operator uses a mini-query language. Characters like `\"`, `*`, `-`, `(`, `)` have special meaning. A query such as `\"` (a lone double-quote) will raise a SQLite parse error that propagates as a thrown exception to the CLI, printing a raw SQLite error message to stderr. More problematic: `* ` (asterisk-space) or `)` can silently corrupt the query semantics. While the binding uses a prepared statement parameter (preventing SQL injection proper), FTS `MATCH` interprets the parameter value as a query expression — it is not a plain string literal. The error is caught in `cmdSearch` and printed to stderr, so it is not a security hole, but the user experience is poor and the behavior is undocumented.\n\nAction: Wrap the query in double-quotes to force a phrase search (`\"${escapedQuery}\"`), or strip/escape FTS metacharacters before passing them to MATCH. At minimum, document the behavior and add a test for special-character input.\n\n---\n\n### HIGH\n\n**4. `ManifestEntry` has `global_manifest_path` and `cleaning_mode` and `repo_mirror_enabled` fields not present in `ALL_COLUMNS` (db.ts vs types/src/index.ts)**\n\n`ManifestEntry` declares fields `global_manifest_path`, `cleaning_mode`, and `repo_mirror_enabled` that are not in `ALL_COLUMNS` (and therefore not in the table schema). The `upsertSearchIndex` function iterates `ALL_COLUMNS` to map values, so those three fields are silently dropped on every write. This is not directly a bug in the search feature, but it means the index is not a faithful representation of the manifest. With `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` enabled, this divergence will not be caught at compile time because the mapping is done at runtime through `record[col]`. Worth a dedicated fix in the types or a schema migration.\n\n**5. `rank` field is semantically misleading in `SearchResult` (db.ts lines 104, 123, 162)**\n\nThe interface declares `rank: number` but the SQL hardcodes `0 as rank` and the comment says \"order by relevance is not directly available\". A consumer of the JSON output (e.g. an agent integration or external tool) will interpret `rank: 0` as meaning all results have equal relevance, which is correct, but the field name implies relevance scoring. This is misleading for a public exported type. Options:\n- Remove the field from `SearchResult` entirely.\n- Rename it to `rank_score` and document `0` as \"not computed\".\n- Replace it with `sort_key: string` equal to `started_at` to expose the actual ordering criterion.\n\nThe comment in the SQL acknowledges the issue (`// FTS4 uses docid (not rank)`) but the exported interface makes no mention of it, violating the API contract.\n\n**6. `searchTranscripts` opens the database read-only but runs DDL (`CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE`, `INSERT`) against it (db.ts lines 145-146)**\n\nThe function reads the file with `readFileSync` and opens an in-memory copy, but running DDL and DML on an in-memory snapshot does not persist. Every call to `searchTranscripts` therefore creates the FTS table, rebuilds it, queries it, and discards the result — meaning the FTS table is never persisted. This is by design given the sql.js architecture (the DB is in memory), but it means every search pays the full cost of building the FTS index. If the database file is opened by `upsertSearchIndex` concurrently (e.g., a daemon sync while a search is running), both load the file independently and there is no locking. This is documented nowhere.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM\n\n**7. Duplicate import of `join` and `resolve` from `node:path` (cmd-search.ts lines 5-6)**\n\n```ts\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { resolve } from 'node:path'\n```\n\nThese should be a single import: `import { join, resolve } from 'node:path'`. This is a style/lint issue, but with a strict ESLint config (which the project appears to have) this would likely be flagged.\n\n**8. `SearchOptions.format` is typed as `string` instead of a union (cmd-search.ts line 16)**\n\n```ts\nformat: string\n```\n\nEvery other command module (e.g., `cmdImport`, `cmdDiscover`, `cmdRecall`) uses the same loose `string` type, but the actual accepted values are `'text' | 'json'`. An invalid `--format foo` silently falls through to the text branch. The same issue exists in `main.ts` where the `parseInt(opts.limit, 10) || 20` fallback means `--limit 0` is treated as `20`. Applying a union type and/or a Commander `.choices()` call would prevent silent degradation.\n\n**9. `--root` option is documented as unused but still present in `SearchOptions` and wired through to `cmdSearch` (main.ts line 146, cmd-search.ts line 13)**\n\nThe commander option description explicitly says `(unused; reserved for future filtering)`. The `root` field appears in `SearchOptions` but `cmdSearch` never reads `options.root`. This creates dead code in the public interface and a mismatch between the README (which does not list `--root` for `search`) and the actual registered options.\n\nAction: Either remove the option entirely or document it in README. Currently the README omits it, so a user running `openmnemo search --help` sees an option the docs don't mention.\n\n**10. Test for `limit` uses `toBeLessThanOrEqual` instead of `toEqual` (db.test.ts line 285)**\n\n```ts\nexpect(results.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(3)\n```\n\nThis passes even if `results.length` is 0 or 1. Given that 5 records all share the term `'common'`, the test should assert `expect(results.length).toBe(3)` to prove the limit is actually enforced at exactly the requested boundary.\n\n**11. `searchTranscripts` test does not assert the `rank` field value (db.test.ts lines 288-304)**\n\nThe test for `result records have required SearchResult fields` checks every field except `rank`. Since `rank` is part of the exported `SearchResult` interface and its constant value of `0` is a design decision, there should be an explicit `expect(r.rank).toBe(0)` assertion to document and lock in that decision.\n\n**12. No test for multi-word queries or FTS MATCH special characters**\n\nThere are no tests for:\n- Multi-word queries (`'authentication bug'`) — FTS4 interprets this as two separate terms by default (AND), not a phrase. A phrase requires `\"authentication bug\"`. The README example `openmnemo search --query \"authentication bug\"` suggests phrase matching, but that is not what happens.\n- A query containing `\"`, `-`, `*`, or `(` — any of these will cause a runtime error or unexpected results.\n\n---\n\n### LOW\n\n**13. Stale JSDoc comment says \"FTS5\" after migrating to FTS4 (db.ts line 132)**\n\n```ts\n/**\n * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS5.\n */\n```\n\nThe comment above `searchTranscripts` still says \"FTS5\" but the implementation uses FTS4. This is a documentation inaccuracy.\n\n**14. README search section is missing `--root` option (README.md lines 172-178)**\n\nThe registered `--root` option (even if unused) does not appear in the README's option table. This is inconsistent with other commands that document all options. As noted in finding #9, it should either be removed or documented.\n\n**15. README example may mislead users about phrase vs. term search**\n\n```\nopenmnemo search --query \"authentication bug\"\n```\n\nThe shell double-quotes are consumed by the shell and the CLI receives `authentication bug` as the argument. FTS4 `MATCH 'authentication bug'` matches documents containing both words independently (AND logic), not the exact phrase. A user who expects `\"authentication bug\"` (the phrase) to work differently from `authentication bug` (two terms) will be surprised. The README should clarify this or the implementation should wrap the query in FTS quote-escaping.\n\n**16. `cmdSearch` function name capitalization inconsistency in the file header comment**\n\n`cmd-search.ts` line 1 says `CLI: openmnemo search — full-text search over the transcript index.` — this is fine. But `searchTranscripts` in the JSDoc (db.ts line 132) still says \"FTS5\" as noted above. Minor, but combined with other stale comments it suggests the migration from FTS5 to FTS4 was done quickly.\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | Location | Issue |\n|---|----------|----------|-------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | db.ts:111 | `tokenize=unicode61` unsupported in FTS4 / sql.js |\n| 2 | CRITICAL | db.ts:145-146 | Full FTS rebuild on every search call |\n| 3 | CRITICAL | db.ts:151 | FTS MATCH receives raw user query; special chars cause errors |\n| 4 | HIGH | db.ts vs types | `ManifestEntry` fields `global_manifest_path`/`cleaning_mode`/`repo_mirror_enabled` silently dropped |\n| 5 | HIGH | db.ts:104,123 | `rank: 0` hardcoded but exported as meaningful field |\n| 6 | HIGH | db.ts:144-169 | FTS table never persisted; cost paid on every search |\n| 7 | MEDIUM | cmd-search.ts:5-6 | Duplicate `node:path` imports |\n| 8 | MEDIUM | cmd-search.ts:16 | `format` typed as `string` not `'text' \\| 'json'` |\n| 9 | MEDIUM | main.ts:146 / cmd-search.ts:13 | `--root` wired but unused, absent from README |\n| 10 | MEDIUM | db.test.ts:285 | Limit test uses `toBeLessThanOrEqual` not `toBe(3)` |\n| 11 | MEDIUM | db.test.ts:304 | No assertion on `rank` field value |\n| 12 | MEDIUM | tests | No test for multi-word query or special-character input |\n| 13 | LOW | db.ts:132 | JSDoc still says \"FTS5\" after migration |\n| 14 | LOW | README.md:172-178 | `--root` not listed under `search` options |\n| 15 | LOW | README.md:170 | Example implies phrase match; FTS4 does AND-term match |\n| 16 | LOW | db.ts:132 | Minor stale comment from FTS5 era |\n\nThe three most urgent fixes before shipping are: removing or replacing the `tokenize=unicode61` clause (#1), moving the FTS rebuild out of the hot search path (#2), and sanitizing/quoting the FTS MATCH query argument (#3).",
    "type": "text"
  },

[
  {
    "text": "I now have enough information to write a thorough review. Here are all the findings:\n\n---\n\n## Code Review Report\n\n**Files reviewed:**\n- `/packages/cli/src/cmd-search.ts` (new)\n- `/packages/cli/src/main.ts` (search registration)\n- `/packages/core/src/transcript/db.ts` (FTS5 → FTS4)\n- `/packages/core/tests/transcript/db.test.ts` (new search tests)\n- `README.md` (search docs)\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL\n\n**None.** The FTS4 migration is functionally correct, all tests pass (243 core, 99 CLI), and the SQL query produces correct results with the actual 18-column schema.\n\n---\n\n### HIGH\n\n**1. Stale JSDoc comment in `db.ts` — `searchTranscripts` still says \"FTS5\"**\n\nLine 132–133:\n```ts\n/**\n * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS5.\n */\n```\nThe section header on line 93 also says `// FTS5 full-text search`. Both are now factually wrong after the FTS4 migration. Not a runtime bug but misleading to every future reader and inconsistent with the line-120 comment that correctly says `// FTS4 uses \\`docid\\`...`.\n\nFile: `/packages/core/src/transcript/db.ts`, lines 93 and 132.\n\n**2. `searchTranscripts` rebuilds the FTS index on every call**\n\n`CREATE_FTS_SQL` and `REBUILD_FTS_SQL` run unconditionally at the start of every `searchTranscripts` invocation. Because `db.js` loads the database file from disk each time (the db is not a persistent process-level handle), the `IF NOT EXISTS` guard on `CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE` is safe, but the `INSERT INTO transcripts_fts(transcripts_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` runs every time regardless. For a database with hundreds or thousands of transcripts this is O(N) work per search call, redundant because the data was already in the content table.\n\nThe clean fix is to move FTS table creation and initial population into `upsertSearchIndex` (where writes already happen), and remove the rebuild from `searchTranscripts`. If you want `searchTranscripts` to remain standalone (for resilience when the FTS table is missing), the rebuild should be guarded — e.g., only rebuild if the FTS row count is 0. As-is, correctness is fine but the performance overhead grows linearly with transcript count.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM\n\n**3. Two stale `join`/`resolve` imports not combined — minor but inconsistent**\n\n`cmd-search.ts` lines 5–6:\n```ts\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { resolve } from 'node:path'\n```\nAll other `cmd-*.ts` files use a single `import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'` plus a single `import { resolve } from 'node:path'` style. These two `node:path` imports should be one line: `import { join, resolve } from 'node:path'`. This is a minor style inconsistency, not a bug.\n\nFile: `/packages/cli/src/cmd-search.ts`, lines 5–6.\n\n**4. `formatSearchText` does not render the `branch` field**\n\n`SearchResult` has a `branch` field. The JSON format exposes it. The text format silently drops it:\n```ts\nlines.push(`- [${r.client}] ${r.project}/${r.session_id}`)\nlines.push(`  title: ${r.title}`)\nlines.push(`  started_at: ${r.started_at}`)\nif (r.cwd) lines.push(`  cwd: ${r.cwd}`)\n// branch never rendered\n```\nCompare to `cmd-import.ts` `formatImportText` which always includes `branch`. If a user searches for sessions by branch name, the text output gives no feedback confirming it. This is not a data-loss bug (branch is in JSON output), but it creates an asymmetry.\n\nFile: `/packages/cli/src/cmd-search.ts`, lines 50–55.\n\n**5. `main.ts` `--limit` parsing: `parseInt(\"0\") || 20 = 20` (0 silently becomes 20)**\n\nIn the search command registration (line 156):\n```ts\nlimit: parseInt(opts.limit, 10) || 20,\n```\nA user passing `--limit 0` gets 20 results, not 0 or \"unlimited\". The discover command (line 118) uses `|| 0` for the same reason (0 = unlimited). For search, 0 as \"no limit\" is arguably reasonable but `|| 20` makes 0 invisible. Consider `parseInt(..., 10)` followed by a `Number.isNaN` or `<= 0` check for a more deliberate behavior.\n\nFile: `/packages/cli/src/main.ts`, line 156.\n\n**6. `--root` option in `search` command is declared but explicitly marked unused**\n\nThe option comment says `'Target repository root (unused; reserved for future filtering)'`. While the intent is future-proofing, Commander will accept `--root` silently and do nothing with it. Users who pass `--root` expecting project-filtered search will get back all-projects results with no warning. Either omit the option now and add it when the feature lands, or emit a warning when it is supplied with a non-default value.\n\nFile: `/packages/cli/src/main.ts`, lines 146–147.\n\n---\n\n### LOW\n\n**7. Test for `searchTranscripts` limit is weaker than necessary**\n\nTest \"respects the limit parameter\" (line 276):\n```ts\nexpect(results.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(3)\n```\nSince all 5 rows match and limit is 3, the result will always be exactly 3. The assertion `toBeLessThanOrEqual` does not catch a regression where limit is silently ignored (e.g., returns 5 rows). It should be `toEqual(3)`.\n\nFile: `/packages/core/tests/transcript/db.test.ts`, line 285.\n\n**8. No test for malformed FTS4 query string**\n\n`searchTranscripts` with a query like `\"OR OR AND\"` causes sql.js to throw `\"malformed MATCH expression\"`. The `try/catch` in `cmdSearch` handles this correctly and returns exit code 1 with a message. However, no test covers this path — neither in `db.test.ts` (which could test that `searchTranscripts` throws for malformed queries) nor in CLI-level tests. Given the user controls the query string, this is an important error path.\n\n**9. No CLI-level unit tests for `cmdSearch`**\n\n`packages/cli/tests/cli/` only contains `daemon.test.ts`. The other cmd modules (`cmd-recall`, `cmd-import`, `cmd-discover`) also lack CLI-level tests, so this is consistent with the existing pattern. Still, `cmdSearch`'s logic is testable (empty query validation, format=json output shape, db-not-found behavior) and worth adding given the new feature.\n\n**10. `SearchResult.rank` is always `0` — the field is misleading**\n\nThe `rank` field exists in the interface and is always hardcoded to `0 as rank` in `SEARCH_SQL`. FTS4 without the `matchinfo()` function does not provide relevance scores, so the comment correctly explains this. But shipping a public interface field that is permanently `0` is confusing to API consumers. Either remove `rank` from `SearchResult` now, or document it explicitly as \"always 0, reserved for future BM25 ranking\". The field is also exposed in the JSON output, so external tooling could depend on it.\n\nFile: `/packages/core/src/transcript/db.ts`, line 96–105 (interface), line 122–123 (SQL).\n\n**11. README `search` section is missing `--root` from the options table**\n\nThe README (lines 172–178) documents `--query`, `--global-root`, `--limit`, and `--format` but omits `--root`, which is registered in `main.ts`. Even though `--root` is currently a no-op, it is visible in `openmnemo search --help` and users will ask about it.\n\nFile: `README.md`, lines 172–178.\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|\n| 1 | HIGH | `db.ts:93,132` | Stale \"FTS5\" comments after FTS4 migration |\n| 2 | HIGH | `db.ts:144–146` | FTS rebuild runs on every search call (O(N) perf) |\n| 3 | MEDIUM | `cmd-search.ts:5–6` | Redundant split `node:path` import |\n| 4 | MEDIUM | `cmd-search.ts:50–55` | `branch` field missing from text format output |\n| 5 | MEDIUM | `main.ts:156` | `--limit 0` silently maps to 20 |\n| 6 | MEDIUM | `main.ts:146–147` | Unused `--root` option gives no warning when supplied |\n| 7 | LOW | `db.test.ts:285` | Limit test uses `toBeLessThanOrEqual` instead of `toEqual(3)` |\n| 8 | LOW | `db.test.ts` | No test for malformed FTS4 query error path |\n| 9 | LOW | `cli/tests/` | No CLI-level tests for `cmdSearch` |\n| 10 | LOW | `db.ts:96–105` | `rank: 0` always — field is permanently meaningless in public API |\n| 11 | LOW | `README.md:172–178` | `--root` missing from `search` command option docs |\n\nThe FTS5 → FTS4 migration itself is **correct**: `fts4` with `content='transcripts'`, `tokenize=unicode61`, and the `docid = t.rowid` join all work correctly with the sql.js WASM build (which ships FTS4 but not FTS5), verified by direct runtime testing. The migration was necessary and the SQL is sound.",

  },

---

## Review 结果

### CRITICAL（必须修）

| # | 位置 | 问题 |
|---|------|------|
| C1 | db.ts:111 | tokenize=unicode61 在 FTS4 中不支持（FTS5 才有），sql.js 可能静默忽略或报错 |
| C2 | db.ts:145-146 | searchTranscripts 每次调用都全量重建 FTS 索引，O(N) 性能问题 |
| C3 | db.ts:151 | 用户 query 字符串原样传入 FTS MATCH，含 "、*、( 等特殊字符时会报错 |

### HIGH（应修）

| # | 位置 | 问题 |
|---|------|------|
| H1 | db.ts:93,132 | JSDoc 注释和 section header 写的还是 "FTS5" |
| H2 | db.ts:104,123 | rank 字段永远是 0，导出到公开接口里有误导性 |

### MEDIUM（建议修）

| # | 位置 | 问题 |
|---|------|------|
| M1 | cmd-search.ts:5-6 | 两行 node:path import 应合并为一行 |
| M2 | cmd-search.ts:50-55 | 文本输出漏掉了 branch 字段 |
| M3 | main.ts:156 | --limit 0 会静默变成 20 |
| M4 | main.ts:146 | --root 注册但无用，README 也没写 |
| M5 | db.test.ts:285 | limit 测试用 toBeLessThanOrEqual(3) 应改为 toBe(3) |
| M6 | db.test.ts | 未断言 rank 字段值，未测试多词查询和特殊字符 |

### LOW（可选修）

README 示例可能让用户误以为是短语匹配，实际是 AND 多词匹配。

---

Tool Result #1093 Read toolu_01QbN4biL2mHbKhoRageVcWZ 00:17:35
Read output=1→/** 2→ * Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog. 3→ * 4→ * Uses sql.js (WebAssembly SQLite) so the module works without a native 5→ * sqlite...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog.
     3→ *
     4→ * Uses sql.js (WebAssembly SQLite) so the module works without a native
     5→ * sqlite3 binding.  The WASM init is async; we cache the resulting factory
     6→ * promise so it is only resolved once per process.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import initSqlJs, { type SqlJsStatic } from 'sql.js'
    10→import { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    11→import type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'
    12→
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→// Lazy sql.js initialisation (cached promise)
    15→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→
    17→let sqlPromise: Promise<SqlJsStatic> | null = null
    18→
    19→function getSqlJs(): Promise<SqlJsStatic> {
    20→  if (sqlPromise === null) {
    21→    sqlPromise = initSqlJs()
    22→  }
    23→  return sqlPromise
    24→}
    25→
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→// Column definitions
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→const PK_COLUMNS = [
    31→  'client',
    32→  'project',
    33→  'session_id',
    34→  'raw_sha256',
    35→] as const
    36→
    37→const DATA_COLUMNS = [
    38→  'title',
    39→  'started_at',
    40→  'imported_at',
    41→  'cwd',
    42→  'branch',
    43→  'raw_source_path',
    44→  'raw_upload_permission',
    45→  'global_raw_path',
    46→  'global_clean_path',
    47→  'repo_raw_path',
    48→  'repo_clean_path',
    49→  'repo_manifest_path',
    50→  'message_count',
    51→  'tool_event_count',
    52→] as const
    53→
    54→const ALL_COLUMNS = [...PK_COLUMNS, ...DATA_COLUMNS] as const
    55→
    56→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    57→// SQL statements (built once)
    58→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    59→
    60→const CREATE_TABLE_SQL = `
    61→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
    62→    client              TEXT    NOT NULL,
    63→    project             TEXT    NOT NULL,
    64→    session_id          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    65→    raw_sha256          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    66→    title               TEXT    NOT NULL,
    67→    started_at          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    68→    imported_at         TEXT    NOT NULL,
    69→    cwd                 TEXT    NOT NULL,
    70→    branch              TEXT    NOT NULL,
    71→    raw_source_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    72→    raw_upload_permission TEXT  NOT NULL,
    73→    global_raw_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    74→    global_clean_path   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    75→    repo_raw_path       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    76→    repo_clean_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    77→    repo_manifest_path  TEXT    NOT NULL,
    78→    message_count       INTEGER NOT NULL,
    79→    tool_event_count    INTEGER NOT NULL,
    80→    PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
    81→  )
    82→`
    83→
    84→const UPSERT_SQL = `
    85→  INSERT INTO transcripts (
    86→    ${ALL_COLUMNS.join(', ')}
    87→  ) VALUES (${ALL_COLUMNS.map(() => '?').join(', ')})
    88→  ON CONFLICT(${PK_COLUMNS.join(', ')}) DO UPDATE SET
    89→    ${DATA_COLUMNS.map((c) => `${c} = excluded.${c}`).join(',\n    ')}
    90→`
    91→
    92→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    93→// FTS5 full-text search
    94→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    95→
    96→export interface SearchResult {
    97→  client: string
    98→  project: string
    99→  session_id: string
   100→  title: string
   101→  cwd: string
   102→  branch: string
   103→  started_at: string
   104→  rank: number
   105→}
   106→
   107→const CREATE_FTS_SQL = `
   108→  CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts_fts USING fts4(
   109→    content='transcripts',
   110→    title, cwd, branch,
   111→    tokenize=unicode61
   112→  )
   113→`
   114→
   115→const REBUILD_FTS_SQL = `
   116→  INSERT INTO transcripts_fts(transcripts_fts) VALUES('rebuild')
   117→`
   118→
   119→// FTS4 uses `docid` (not `rank`) — order by relevance is not directly available;
   120→// we sort by started_at desc as a pragmatic substitute.
   121→const SEARCH_SQL = `
   122→  SELECT t.client, t.project, t.session_id, t.title, t.cwd, t.branch, t.started_at,
   123→         0 as rank
   124→  FROM transcripts_fts
   125→  JOIN transcripts t ON transcripts_fts.docid = t.rowid
   126→  WHERE transcripts_fts MATCH ?
   127→  ORDER BY t.started_at DESC
   128→  LIMIT ?
   129→`
   130→
   131→/**
   132→ * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS5.
   133→ */
   134→export async function searchTranscripts(
   135→  dbPath: string,
   136→  query: string,
   137→  limit = 20,
   138→): Promise<SearchResult[]> {
   139→  const SQL = await getSqlJs()
   140→
   141→  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) return []
   142→  const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))
   143→
   144→  try {
   145→    db.run(CREATE_FTS_SQL)
   146→    db.run(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)
   147→
   148→    const results: SearchResult[] = []
   149→    const stmt = db.prepare(SEARCH_SQL)
   150→    stmt.bind([query, limit])
   151→
   152→    while (stmt.step()) {
   153→      const row = stmt.getAsObject() as Record<string, unknown>
   154→      results.push({
   155→        client: String(row['client'] ?? ''),
   156→        project: String(row['project'] ?? ''),
   157→        session_id: String(row['session_id'] ?? ''),
   158→        title: String(row['title'] ?? ''),
   159→        cwd: String(row['cwd'] ?? ''),
   160→        branch: String(row['branch'] ?? ''),
   161→        started_at: String(row['started_at'] ?? ''),
   162→        rank: Number(row['rank'] ?? 0),
   163→      })
   164→    }
   165→    stmt.free()
   166→    return results
   167→  } finally {
   168→    db.close()
   169→  }
   170→}
   171→
   172→/**
   173→ * Insert or update a single transcript manifest row in the SQLite search
   174→ * index at `dbPath`.  Creates the database and table if they do not exist.
   175→ *
   176→ * The function is async because the first call must initialise the sql.js
   177→ * WASM module.
   178→ */
   179→export async function upsertSearchIndex(
   180→  dbPath: string,
   181→  manifest: ManifestEntry,
   182→): Promise<void> {
   183→  const SQL = await getSqlJs()
   184→
   185→  const db = existsSync(dbPath)
   186→    ? new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))
   187→    : new SQL.Database()
   188→
   189→  try {
   190→    db.run('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL')
   191→    db.run(CREATE_TABLE_SQL)
   192→
   193→    const record: Record<string, unknown> = { ...manifest }
   194→    const params = ALL_COLUMNS.map((col) => {
   195→      const value = record[col]
   196→      if (value === undefined || value === null) {
   197→        return ''
   198→      }
   199→      return typeof value === 'number' ? value : String(value)
   200→    })
   201→
   202→    db.run(UPSERT_SQL, params)
   203→
   204→    const data = db.export()
   205→    writeFileSync(dbPath, Buffer.from(data))
   206→  } finally {
   207→    db.close()
   208→  }
   209→}
   210→

Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\src\\transcript\\db.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog.\n *\n * Uses sql.js (WebAssembly SQLite) so the module works without a native\n * sqlite3 binding.  The WASM init is async; we cache the resulting factory\n * promise so it is only resolved once per process.\n *\n * Full-text search uses FTS4 (not FTS5) because the default sql.js WASM build\n * includes FTS4 but not FTS5.  The `simple` tokenizer is used (FTS4 default);\n * `unicode61` is a FTS5-only extension and is not available here.\n */\n\nimport initSqlJs, { type SqlJsStatic } from 'sql.js'\nimport { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Lazy sql.js initialisation (cached promise)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nlet sqlPromise: Promise<SqlJsStatic> | null = null\n\nfunction getSqlJs(): Promise<SqlJsStatic> {\n  if (sqlPromise === null) {\n    sqlPromise = initSqlJs()\n  }\n  return sqlPromise\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Column definitions\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst PK_COLUMNS = [\n  'client',\n  'project',\n  'session_id',\n  'raw_sha256',\n] as const\n\nconst DATA_COLUMNS = [\n  'title',\n  'started_at',\n  'imported_at',\n  'cwd',\n  'branch',\n  'raw_source_path',\n  'raw_upload_permission',\n  'global_raw_path',\n  'global_clean_path',\n  'repo_raw_path',\n  'repo_clean_path',\n  'repo_manifest_path',\n  'message_count',\n  'tool_event_count',\n] as const\n\nconst ALL_COLUMNS = [...PK_COLUMNS, ...DATA_COLUMNS] as const\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// SQL statements (built once)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst CREATE_TABLE_SQL = `\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (\n    client              TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    project             TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    session_id          TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    raw_sha256          TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    title               TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    started_at          TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    imported_at         TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    cwd                 TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    branch              TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    raw_source_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    raw_upload_permission TEXT  NOT NULL,\n    global_raw_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    global_clean_path   TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    repo_raw_path       TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    repo_clean_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    repo_manifest_path  TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    message_count       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    tool_event_count    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)\n  )\n`\n\nconst UPSERT_SQL = `\n  INSERT INTO transcripts (\n    ${ALL_COLUMNS.join(', ')}\n  ) VALUES (${ALL_COLUMNS.map(() => '?').join(', ')})\n  ON CONFLICT(${PK_COLUMNS.join(', ')}) DO UPDATE SET\n    ${DATA_COLUMNS.map((c) => `${c} = excluded.${c}`).join(',\\n    ')}\n`\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// FTS4 full-text search\n//\n// The FTS table is created and rebuilt inside upsertSearchIndex (the write\n// path) so that searchTranscripts never pays the O(N) rebuild cost.\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// FTS4 content table — no tokenize option (uses the built-in `simple` tokenizer).\nconst CREATE_FTS_SQL = `\n  CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts_fts USING fts4(\n    content='transcripts',\n    title, cwd, branch\n  )\n`\n\nconst REBUILD_FTS_SQL = `\n  INSERT INTO transcripts_fts(transcripts_fts) VALUES('rebuild')\n`\n\n// FTS4 uses `docid`; results are ordered by started_at DESC (most recent first)\n// because FTS4 does not expose a BM25 relevance score.\nconst SEARCH_SQL = `\n  SELECT t.client, t.project, t.session_id, t.title, t.cwd, t.branch, t.started_at\n  FROM transcripts_fts\n  JOIN transcripts t ON transcripts_fts.docid = t.rowid\n  WHERE transcripts_fts MATCH ?\n  ORDER BY t.started_at DESC\n  LIMIT ?\n`\n\nexport interface SearchResult {\n  client: string\n  project: string\n  session_id: string\n  title: string\n  cwd: string\n  branch: string\n  started_at: string\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize a user-supplied query string for safe use in an FTS4 MATCH clause.\n *\n * FTS4 MATCH interprets the bound parameter as a query expression, not a\n * plain string literal.  Characters like `\"`, `*`, `(`, `)`, `-` have\n * special meaning and cause a parse error if left unescaped.\n *\n * Strategy: keep only Unicode letters, digits, apostrophes, and whitespace;\n * collapse runs of whitespace to a single space.  The result is a plain\n * multi-term AND query (e.g. `\"auth bug\"` → `auth bug` matches both words).\n *\n * Returns an empty string when nothing survives sanitization.\n */\nexport function sanitizeFtsQuery(raw: string): string {\n  return raw\n    .replace(/[^\\p{L}\\p{N}\\s']/gu, ' ')\n    .trim()\n    .replace(/\\s+/g, ' ')\n}\n\n/**\n * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS4.\n *\n * The FTS index is maintained by `upsertSearchIndex`; this function only\n * reads.  Returns an empty array when the database does not exist or the\n * sanitized query is empty.\n */\nexport async function searchTranscripts(\n  dbPath: string,\n  query: string,\n  limit = 20,\n): Promise<SearchResult[]> {\n  const sanitized = sanitizeFtsQuery(query)\n  if (!sanitized) return []\n\n  const SQL = await getSqlJs()\n\n  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) return []\n  const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n\n  try {\n    // Safety net: create the FTS table if it was missing (old DB).\n    // No rebuild here — upsertSearchIndex is responsible for keeping it current.\n    db.run(CREATE_FTS_SQL)\n\n    const results: SearchResult[] = []\n    const stmt = db.prepare(SEARCH_SQL)\n    stmt.bind([sanitized, limit])\n\n    while (stmt.step()) {\n      const row = stmt.getAsObject() as Record<string, unknown>\n      results.push({\n        client: String(row['client'] ?? ''),\n        project: String(row['project'] ?? ''),\n        session_id: String(row['session_id'] ?? ''),\n        title: String(row['title'] ?? ''),\n        cwd: String(row['cwd'] ?? ''),\n        branch: String(row['branch'] ?? ''),\n        started_at: String(row['started_at'] ?? ''),\n      })\n    }\n    stmt.free()\n    return results\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Insert or update a single transcript manifest row in the SQLite search\n * index at `dbPath`.  Creates the database, content table, and FTS index if\n * they do not exist, and rebuilds the FTS index after each write so that\n * `searchTranscripts` never needs to rebuild it.\n *\n * The function is async because the first call must initialise the sql.js\n * WASM module.\n */\nexport async function upsertSearchIndex(\n  dbPath: string,\n  manifest: ManifestEntry,\n): Promise<void> {\n  const SQL = await getSqlJs()\n\n  const db = existsSync(dbPath)\n    ? new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n    : new SQL.Database()\n\n  try {\n    db.run('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL')\n    db.run(CREATE_TABLE_SQL)\n\n    const record: Record<string, unknown> = { ...manifest }\n    const params = ALL_COLUMNS.map((col) => {\n      const value = record[col]\n      if (value === undefined || value === null) {\n        return ''\n      }\n      return typeof value === 'number' ? value : String(value)\n    })\n\n    db.run(UPSERT_SQL, params)\n\n    // Keep the FTS index in sync after every write.\n    db.run(CREATE_FTS_SQL)\n    db.run(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)\n\n    const data = db.export()\n    writeFileSync(dbPath, Buffer.from(data))\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}\n"
}

Tool Call #1111 Write toolu_01B8Hs4et3z39nG4iRAELWhh 00:19:29
Write input={"content":"/**\n * CLI: openmnemo search — full-text search over the transcript index.\n */\n\nimport { join, resolve } from 'node:path'\n\nimport { defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\cli\\src\\cmd-search.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * CLI: openmnemo search — full-text search over the transcript index.\n */\n\nimport { join, resolve } from 'node:path'\n\nimport { defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot, searchTranscripts } from '@openmnemo/core'\nimport type { SearchResult } from '@openmnemo/core'\n\nexport interface SearchOptions {\n  query: string\n  globalRoot: string\n  limit: number\n  format: string\n}\n\nexport async function cmdSearch(options: SearchOptions): Promise<number> {\n  const query = options.query.trim()\n  if (!query) {\n    process.stderr.write('--query must not be empty\\n')\n    return 1\n  }\n\n  const globalRoot = options.globalRoot ? resolve(options.globalRoot) : defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot()\n  const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')\n\n  let results: SearchResult[]\n  try {\n    results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, query, options.limit)\n  } catch (err: unknown) {\n    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)\n    process.stderr.write(`search error: ${msg}\\n`)\n    return 1\n  }\n\n  if (options.format === 'json') {\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ query, results, count: results.length }) + '\\n')\n  } else {\n    process.stdout.write(formatSearchText(query, results) + '\\n')\n  }\n  return 0\n}\n\nfunction formatSearchText(query: string, results: SearchResult[]): string {\n  const lines: string[] = [`query: ${query}`, `count: ${results.length}`]\n  if (results.length > 0) {\n    lines.push('results:')\n    for (const r of results) {\n      lines.push(`- [${r.client}] ${r.project}/${r.session_id}`)\n      lines.push(`  title: ${r.title}`)\n      lines.push(`  started_at: ${r.started_at}`)\n      if (r.cwd) lines.push(`  cwd: ${r.cwd}`)\n      if (r.branch) lines.push(`  branch: ${r.branch}`)\n    }\n  }\n  return lines.join('\\n')\n}\n"
}

'',\n      limit: parseInt(opts.limit, 10) || 20,\n      format: opts.format,\n    })\n  })",

'',\n      limit: !Number.isNaN(n) && n > 0 ? n : 20,\n      format: opts.format,\n    })\n  })"
}

233→// searchTranscripts — FTS4 full-text search
   234→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   235→
   236→describe('searchTranscripts', () => {
   237→  let tmpDir: string
   238→  let dbPath: string
   239→
   240→  beforeEach(() => {
   241→    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'db-fts-test-'))
   242→    dbPath = join(tmpDir, 'search.sqlite')
   243→  })
   244→
   245→  afterEach(() => {
   246→    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
   247→  })
   248→
   249→  it('returns empty array when db does not exist', async () => {
   250→    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'anything')
   251→    expect(results).toEqual([])
   252→  })
   253→
   254→  it('finds records matching query term in title', async () => {
   255→    await upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({ title: 'authentication bug', session_id: 'sess-auth' }))
   256→    await upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({ title: 'unrelated session', session_id: 'sess-other', raw_sha256: 'other' }))
   257→
   258→    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'authentication')
   259→    expect(results).toHaveLength(1)
   260→    expect(results[0]!.session_id).toBe('sess-auth')
   261→  })
   262→
   263→  it('finds records matching query term in cwd', async () => {
   264→    await upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({ cwd: '/home/user/myproject', session_id: 'sess-cwd' }))
   265→    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'myproject')
   266→    expect(results).toHaveLength(1)
   267→    expect(results[0]!.session_id).toBe('sess-cwd')
   268→  })
   269→
   270→  it('returns empty array when no records match', async () => {
   271→    await upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({ title: 'hello world' }))
   272→    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'zzznomatch9999')
   273→    expect(results).toEqual([])
   274→  })
   275→
   276→  it('respects the limit parameter', async () => {
   277→    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
   278→      await upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({
   279→        session_id: `sess-${i}`,
   280→        raw_sha256: `hash-${i}`,
   281→        title: 'common keyword session',
   282→      }))
   283→    }
   284→    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'common', 3)
   285→    expect(results.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(3)
   286→  })
   287→
   288→  it('result records have required SearchResult fields', async () => {
   289→    await upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({
   290→      title: 'feature implementation',
   291→      cwd: '/home/dev',
   292→      branch: 'feature/auth',
   293→      started_at: '2024-07-01T00:00:00Z',
   294→    }))
   295→    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'feature')
   296→    expect(results).toHaveLength(1)
   297→    const r = results[0]!
   298→    expect(r.client).toBe('claude')
   299→    expect(r.project).toBe('my-project')
   300→    expect(r.title).toBe('feature implementation')
   301→    expect(r.cwd).toBe('/home/dev')
   302→    expect(r.branch).toBe('feature/auth')
   303→    expect(r.started_at).toBe('2024-07-01T00:00:00Z')
   304→  })
   305→})

'/home/dev',\n      branch: 'feature/auth',\n      started_at: '2024-07-01T00:00:00Z',\n    }))\n    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'feature')\n    expect(results).toHaveLength(1)\n    const r = results[0]!\n    expect(r.client).toBe('claude')\n    expect(r.project).toBe('my-project')\n    expect(r.title).toBe('feature implementation')\n    expect(r.cwd).toBe('/home/dev')\n    expect(r.branch).toBe('feature/auth')\n    expect(r.started_at).toBe('2024-07-01T00:00:00Z')\n    // rank field no longer exists — check there are exactly 7 keys\n    expect(Object.keys(r)).toEqual(['client', 'project', 'session_id', 'title', 'cwd', 'branch', 'started_at'])\n  })\n\n  it('returns empty array when sanitized query is empty (only special chars)', async () => {\n    await upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({ title: 'hello world' }))\n    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, '\"*()-')\n    expect(results).toEqual([])\n  })\n\n  it('does not throw on multi-word query', async () => {\n    await upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({ title: 'authentication bug fix', session_id: 'sess-multi' }))\n    const results = await searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'authentication bug')\n    expect(results).toHaveLength(1)\n    expect(results[0]!.session_id).toBe('sess-multi')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// sanitizeFtsQuery\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('sanitizeFtsQuery', () => {\n  it('passes through plain words unchanged', () => {\n    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('hello world')).toBe('hello world')\n  })\n\n  it('strips FTS4 metacharacters', () => {\n    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('\"auth* (bug)-')).toBe('auth  bug')\n  })\n\n  it('collapses whitespace runs', () => {\n    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('foo   bar')).toBe('foo bar')\n  })\n\n  it('trims leading and trailing whitespace', () => {\n    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('  hello  ')).toBe('hello')\n  })\n\n  it('returns empty string when only metacharacters given', () => {\n    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('\"*()-^')).toBe('')\n  })\n\n  it('preserves Unicode letters', () => {\n    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('café authentication')).toBe('café authentication')\n  })\n})"
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\README.md",
  "old_string": "### `openmnemo search`\n\nFull-text search over all imported transcripts.\n\n```bash\nopenmnemo search --query \"authentication bug\"\n\nOptions:\n  --query <text>          Search query (required)\n  --global-root <path>    Override global transcript root\n  --limit <n>             Maximum results (default: 20)\n  --format <format>       Output: text or json (default: text)\n```",
  "new_string": "### `openmnemo search`\n\nFull-text search over all imported transcripts (searches title, cwd, and branch).\n\n```bash\nopenmnemo search --query \"authentication bug\"\n```\n\nOptions:\n```\n--query <text>          Search query (required). Multiple words = AND match.\n--global-root <path>    Override global transcript root\n--limit <n>             Maximum results (default: 20)\n--format <format>       Output: text or json (default: text)\n```\n\n> **Note:** The query is split into terms by whitespace; all terms must appear in the document (AND logic). Example: `--query \"auth bug\"` matches sessions where both `auth` and `bug` appear in the title, cwd, or branch."
}

assistant #1152 00:22:46

The real DB has no FTS table at all. I'll add a one-time lazy rebuild in searchTranscripts — only when the segment directory is empty (index was never built):

Tool Call #1153 Edit toolu_01BCSPfACrTVpGbq1RRNEvWm 00:23:00
Edit input={"file_path":"D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\src\\transcript\\db.ts","new_string":"/**\n * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS4.\n *\n * The FTS index is...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\src\\transcript\\db.ts",
  "old_string": "/**\n * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS4.\n *\n * The FTS index is maintained by `upsertSearchIndex`; this function only\n * reads.  Returns an empty array when the database does not exist or the\n * sanitized query is empty.\n */\nexport async function searchTranscripts(\n  dbPath: string,\n  query: string,\n  limit = 20,\n): Promise<SearchResult[]> {\n  const sanitized = sanitizeFtsQuery(query)\n  if (!sanitized) return []\n\n  const SQL = await getSqlJs()\n\n  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) return []\n  const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n\n  try {\n    // Safety net: create the FTS table if it was missing (old DB).\n    // No rebuild here — upsertSearchIndex is responsible for keeping it current.\n    db.run(CREATE_FTS_SQL)\n\n    const results: SearchResult[] = []\n    const stmt = db.prepare(SEARCH_SQL)\n    stmt.bind([sanitized, limit])\n\n    while (stmt.step()) {\n      const row = stmt.getAsObject() as Record<string, unknown>\n      results.push({\n        client: String(row['client'] ?? ''),\n        project: String(row['project'] ?? ''),\n        session_id: String(row['session_id'] ?? ''),\n        title: String(row['title'] ?? ''),\n        cwd: String(row['cwd'] ?? ''),\n        branch: String(row['branch'] ?? ''),\n        started_at: String(row['started_at'] ?? ''),\n      })\n    }\n    stmt.free()\n    return results\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}",
  "new_string": "/**\n * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS4.\n *\n * The FTS index is maintained by `upsertSearchIndex` (write path).\n * On first search against a legacy database (no FTS table), this function\n * performs a one-time lazy rebuild so old indexes keep working.\n * Returns an empty array when the database does not exist or the sanitized\n * query is empty.\n */\nexport async function searchTranscripts(\n  dbPath: string,\n  query: string,\n  limit = 20,\n): Promise<SearchResult[]> {\n  const sanitized = sanitizeFtsQuery(query)\n  if (!sanitized) return []\n\n  const SQL = await getSqlJs()\n\n  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) return []\n  const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n\n  try {\n    // Create FTS table if missing (legacy DB or first run).\n    db.run(CREATE_FTS_SQL)\n\n    // Lazy rebuild: if the FTS segment directory is empty the index has never\n    // been populated (e.g. a DB created before the write-path rebuild was\n    // added).  Rebuild once in memory so this search call returns results.\n    const segRows = db.exec('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM transcripts_fts_segdir')\n    const segCount = Number(segRows[0]?.values?.[0]?.[0] ?? 0)\n    if (segCount === 0) {\n      db.run(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)\n    }\n\n    const results: SearchResult[] = []\n    const stmt = db.prepare(SEARCH_SQL)\n    stmt.bind([sanitized, limit])\n\n    while (stmt.step()) {\n      const row = stmt.getAsObject() as Record<string, unknown>\n      results.push({\n        client: String(row['client'] ?? ''),\n        project: String(row['project'] ?? ''),\n        session_id: String(row['session_id'] ?? ''),\n        title: String(row['title'] ?? ''),\n        cwd: String(row['cwd'] ?? ''),\n        branch: String(row['branch'] ?? ''),\n        started_at: String(row['started_at'] ?? ''),\n      })\n    }\n    stmt.free()\n    return results\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}"
}

assistant #1162 00:23:44

---

所有问题修复完成，汇总如下：

CRITICAL（已修）
- C1 tokenize=unicode61 → 已移除（FTS4 不支持，使用默认 simple 分词器）
- C2 FTS rebuild 热路径 → rebuild 移到 upsertSearchIndex 写入时持久化；searchTranscripts 只在 segment 目录为空时才做一次懒重建（兼容旧库）
- C3 用户 query 直传 FTS MATCH → 新增 sanitizeFtsQuery()，剥离 "*()-^ 等元字符，空查询直接返回 []

HIGH（已修）
- H1 "FTS5" 注释 → 全部改为 "FTS4"，补充 sql.js WASM 限制说明
- H2 rank: 0 字段 → 从 SearchResult 接口和 SQL 中彻底移除

MEDIUM（已修）
- M1 两行 node:path import → 合并为一行
- M2 文本输出漏 branch → 补上
- M3 --limit 0 静默变 20 → 用 Number.isNaN 显式判断
- M4 无用 --root 选项 → 从 main.ts、SearchOptions、README 全部移除
- M5 toBeLessThanOrEqual(3) → toBe(3)
- M6 补 sanitizeFtsQuery 6个单元测试 + 特殊字符 + 多词查询测试

1→name: Publish
     2→
     3→on:

     7→
     8→jobs:
     9→  publish:
    10→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    11→    permissions:

    13→      id-token: write   # required for npm provenance attestation
    14→
    15→    env:
    16→      TURBO_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1"
    17→
    18→    steps:
    19→      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
    20→
    21→      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@a15d269cd4658e1107c09f1fabf4cbd7bd1f308a  # v4.4.0

    25→          node-version: 22
    26→          cache: pnpm
    27→          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org
    28→
    29→      - name: Verify tag matches package version
    30→        run: |
    31→          TAG_VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
    32→          PKG_VERSION=$(jq -r .version packages/cli/package.json)
    33→          if [ "$TAG_VERSION" != "$PKG_VERSION" ]; then
    34→            echo "❌ Tag $GITHUB_REF_NAME does not match package version $PKG_VERSION"
    35→            exit 1
    36→          fi
    37→          echo "✅ Tag matches package version: $PKG_VERSION"
    38→
    39→      - name: Verify NPM_TOKEN is set
    40→        run: |
    41→          if [ -z "$NODE_AUTH_TOKEN" ]; then
    42→            echo "❌ NPM_TOKEN secret is not configured in repository settings"
    43→            exit 1
    44→          fi
    45→          echo "✅ NPM_TOKEN is present"
    46→        env:
    47→          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    48→
    49→      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
    50→
    51→      - run: pnpm build
    52→
    53→      - run: pnpm typecheck
    54→
    55→      - run: pnpm test
    56→
    57→      - name: Publish all packages
    58→        run: |
    59→          # Capture output; treat "already published" as success (idempotent re-run)
    60→          if ! pnpm publish -r --access public --no-git-checks --provenance 2>&1 | tee /tmp/publish.log; then
    61→            if grep -q "previously published versions" /tmp/publish.log; then
    62→              echo "ℹ️  All packages already published at this version — skipping"
    63→              exit 0
    64→            fi
    65→            exit 1
    66→          fi
    67→        env:
    68→          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    69→
assistant #1167 00:25:54

--- ## 自动发版机制 触发条件是 **推送 `v*` tag**，整个流程： ``` git tag v0.3.0 git push origin main --tags ``` GitHub Actions 自动执行： 1. 校验 tag 版本号 = `packages/cli/package.json` 中的版本号（不一致直接失败） 2. 安装依赖 → build → typecheck → test（四关都过才继续） ...

Expand full message

---

## 自动发版机制

触发条件是 推送 v* tag，整个流程：

git tag v0.3.0
git push origin main --tags

GitHub Actions 自动执行：
1. 校验 tag 版本号 = packages/cli/package.json 中的版本号（不一致直接失败）
2. 安装依赖 → build → typecheck → test（四关都过才继续）
3. pnpm publish -r 一次性发布全部 5 个包到 npm
4. 如果某个包已发布过（E403 idempotent），视为成功继续

目前需要手工做的事：每次发版前要手动把 5 个 package.json 的 version 字段统一改好，再打 tag 推送。

---

## 待完成事项（按优先级）

### 高优先级

| 事项 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| ✅ search 命令 | 刚完成 |
| Gemini + Doubao E2E 集成测试 | parse → import → 验证 manifest/markdown/index 全链路 |
| CLI 命令测试 | init / import / discover / recall / report 目前没有 CLI 层测试 |

### 中优先级

| 事项 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| sync barrel 清理 | processProject / scanSensitive / gitCommitAndPush / tryPush 仍从 sync/index.ts 导出，属于内部函数不应公开 |
| 发版流程自动化 | 目前手改 5 个 package.json 版本号 → 可用 changesets 管理（自动 bump + changelog） |
| setup-node v4 弃用警告 | GitHub Actions 将在 2026 年 6 月强制 Node 24，setup-node 需跟进 |

### 低优先级

| 事项 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| 各包独立 README | 5 个 npm 包目前只有根目录 README |
| 新客户端解析器 | ChatGPT (JSON)、Kimi (TXT)、DeepSeek、通义千问 |
| Doubao 自动发现 | 目前只能手动 --source，可加可配置的扫描目录 |

### 架构方向（已讨论，未启动）

| 方向 | 现状 |
|------|------|
| Git commit 标准化 | Conventional Commits 约束 + 语义元数据写入 trailer |
| RAG 管道 | clean markdown → 分块 → 向量嵌入 → 混合检索 |
| SQLite FTS 扩展 | 现在只索引 title/cwd/branch，还不索引消息内容 |
| 知识图谱 | Zep/Graphiti 风格双时序图（计划阶段） |

---

你觉得下一步先做哪块？发版流程自动化（changesets）是个独立小任务，做完可以让后续所有发版都省事不少。

user #1168 00:39:03

你刚刚不是列了高中低三个优先级吗？你这样，我们依次执行高中低，每完成一项，我们就review两次，然后修复完以后再进行下一步次。就是这个情况，你依次执行吧。

assistant #1169 00:39:15

好的，我们按高→中→低依次执行，每项完成后双轮 review，修复后再下一项。

先看现有的集成测试，了解模式：

Read output=1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest' 2→import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, existsSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs' 3→import { join }...
Show output payload 
1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, existsSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→import initSqlJs from 'sql.js'
     6→
     7→import { parseCodexTranscript } from '../../src/transcript/parse.js'
     8→import { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '../../src/transcript/import.js'
     9→import { inferProjectSlug } from '../../src/transcript/discover.js'
    10→
    11→describe('integration: parse → import → verify', () => {
    12→  let tmpDir: string
    13→  let repoRoot: string
    14→  let globalRoot: string
    15→
    16→  beforeEach(() => {
    17→    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'integration-'))
    18→    repoRoot = join(tmpDir, 'repo')
    19→    globalRoot = join(tmpDir, 'global')
    20→  })
    21→  afterEach(() => {
    22→    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    23→  })
    24→
    25→  it('end-to-end: create JSONL → parse → import → verify files', async () => {
    26→    // 1. Create a realistic Codex JSONL transcript
    27→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'test-session.jsonl')
    28→    const records = [
    29→      { type: 'session_meta', payload: { id: 'sess-001', title: 'Fix bug', cwd: '/home/user/myproject', git: { branch: 'main' }, timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:00Z' } },
    30→      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:01Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'input_text', text: 'Fix the login bug' }] } },
    31→      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:02Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'I will fix the login bug for you.' }] } },
    32→      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:03Z', payload: { type: 'function_call', name: 'read_file', arguments: { path: 'login.ts' } } },
    33→      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:04Z', payload: { type: 'function_call_output', name: 'read_file', output: 'file contents here' } },
    34→    ]
    35→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, records.map(r => JSON.stringify(r)).join('\n') + '\n')
    36→
    37→    // 2. Parse
    38→    const parsed = parseCodexTranscript(sourceFile)
    39→    expect(parsed.client).toBe('codex')
    40→    expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('sess-001')
    41→    expect(parsed.title).toBe('Fix bug')
    42→    expect(parsed.cwd).toBe('/home/user/myproject')
    43→    expect(parsed.branch).toBe('main')
    44→    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)
    45→    expect(parsed.tool_events).toHaveLength(2)
    46→    expect(transcriptHasContent(parsed)).toBe(true)
    47→
    48→    // 3. Infer project slug
    49→    const slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)
    50→    expect(slug).toBe('myproject')
    51→
    52→    // 4. Import
    53→    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, slug, 'none', true)
    54→
    55→    // 5. Verify manifest fields
    56→    expect(manifest.client).toBe('codex')
    57→    expect(manifest.project).toBe('myproject')
    58→    expect(manifest.session_id).toBe('sess-001')
    59→    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)
    60→    expect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBe(2)
    61→    expect(manifest.cleaning_mode).toBe('deterministic-code')
    62→    expect(manifest.repo_mirror_enabled).toBe(true)
    63→
    64→    // 6. Verify raw file copied to repo + global
    65→    expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_raw_path))).toBe(true)
    66→
    67→    // 7. Verify clean markdown has correct frontmatter
    68→    const cleanContent = readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path), 'utf-8')
    69→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('---')
    70→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('client: codex')
    71→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('project: myproject')
    72→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Messages')
    73→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('### 1. user')
    74→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('Fix the login bug')
    75→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('### 2. assistant')
    76→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('I will fix the login bug for you.')
    77→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Tool Events')
    78→    expect(cleanContent).toContain('read_file')
    79→
    80→    // 8. Verify manifest JSON written
    81→    const manifestContent = JSON.parse(
    82→      readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_manifest_path), 'utf-8'),
    83→    )
    84→    expect(manifestContent.client).toBe('codex')
    85→    expect(manifestContent.project).toBe('myproject')
    86→
    87→    // 9. Verify SQLite index updated
    88→    const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')
    89→    expect(existsSync(dbPath)).toBe(true)
    90→    const SQL = await initSqlJs()
    91→    const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))
    92→    const rows = db.exec('SELECT client, project, session_id FROM transcripts')
    93→    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)
    94→    expect(rows[0]!.values).toEqual([['codex', 'myproject', 'sess-001']])
    95→    db.close()
    96→
    97→    // 10. Verify event log written
    98→    const eventLogPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')
    99→    expect(existsSync(eventLogPath)).toBe(true)
   100→    const eventLines = readFileSync(eventLogPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\n')
   101→    expect(eventLines).toHaveLength(1)
   102→    const event = JSON.parse(eventLines[0]!)
   103→    expect(event.client).toBe('codex')
   104→  })
   105→
   106→  it('idempotent re-import preserves imported_at', async () => {
   107→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'idem.jsonl')
   108→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, '{"type":"session_meta","payload":{"id":"s1","title":"test"}}\n')
   109→
   110→    const parsed = parseCodexTranscript(sourceFile)
   111→    const first = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'proj', 'none')
   112→    const second = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'proj', 'none')
   113→
   114→    expect(second.imported_at).toBe(first.imported_at)
   115→  })
   116→
   117→  it('Claude transcript end-to-end', async () => {
   118→    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'claude-session.jsonl')
   119→    const records = [
   120→      { type: 'user', sessionId: 'cs1', cwd: '/proj', gitBranch: 'dev', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:00Z', message: { role: 'user', content: 'Hello Claude' } },
   121→      { type: 'assistant', sessionId: 'cs1', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:01Z', message: { role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Hello!' }] } },
   122→    ]
   123→    writeFileSync(sourceFile, records.map(r => JSON.stringify(r)).join('\n') + '\n')
   124→
   125→    const { parseClaudeTranscript } = await import('../../src/transcript/parse.js')
   126→    const parsed = parseClaudeTranscript(sourceFile)
   127→    expect(parsed.client).toBe('claude')
   128→    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)
   129→
   130→    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'test-proj', 'none')
   131→    expect(manifest.client).toBe('claude')
   132→    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)
   133→  })
   134→})

Read output=101→export function inferProjectSlug(parsed: ParsedTranscript): string { 102→ if (parsed.cwd) { 103→ try { 104→ const cwdBase = basename(parsed.cwd) 105→ return slugify(cwdBase,...
Show output payload 
101→export function inferProjectSlug(parsed: ParsedTranscript): string {
   102→  if (parsed.cwd) {
   103→    try {
   104→      const cwdBase = basename(parsed.cwd)
   105→      return slugify(cwdBase, 'project')
   106→    } catch {
   107→      // fall through
   108→    }
   109→  }
   110→
   111→  if (parsed.client === 'claude') {
   112→    let parentName = basename(dirname(parsed.source_path)).toLowerCase()
   113→    parentName = parentName.replace(/^[a-z]--/, '')
   114→    return slugify(parentName, 'project')
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  if (parsed.client === 'gemini') {
   118→    const parentDir = basename(dirname(parsed.source_path))
   119→    if (parentDir) {
   120→      return slugify(parentDir, 'project')
   121→    }
   122→  }
   123→
   124→  return 'unknown-project'
   125→}
   126→
   127→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   128→// Repo matching
   129→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   130→
   131→export function transcriptMatchesRepo(
   132→  parsed: ParsedTranscript,
   133→  repoRoot: string,
   134→  repoSlug: string,
   135→): boolean {

Tool Call #1186 Edit toolu_01LmsYQRh4Po7LXRCzGMpaky 00:40:38
Edit input={"file_path":"D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\tests\\transcript\\integration.test.ts","new_string":" const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, ...
Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\tests\\transcript\\integration.test.ts",
  "old_string": "    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'test-proj', 'none')\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('claude')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n  })\n})",
  "new_string": "    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'test-proj', 'none')\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('claude')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n  })\n\n  it('Gemini JSON transcript end-to-end', async () => {\n    // 1. Create a realistic Gemini JSON transcript\n    const geminiDir = join(tmpDir, 'myproject')\n    const { mkdirSync } = await import('node:fs')\n    mkdirSync(geminiDir, { recursive: true })\n    const sourceFile = join(geminiDir, 'conversation.json')\n\n    const geminiData = {\n      sessionId: 'gemini-sess-001',\n      cwd: '/home/user/myproject',\n      branch: 'main',\n      timestamp: '2024-08-10T09:00:00Z',\n      history: [\n        {\n          role: 'user',\n          timestamp: '2024-08-10T09:00:01Z',\n          parts: [{ text: 'How do I write a unit test?' }],\n        },\n        {\n          role: 'model',\n          timestamp: '2024-08-10T09:00:02Z',\n          parts: [{ text: 'Use a testing framework like Vitest or Jest.' }],\n          toolUse: { name: 'read_file', input: { path: 'test.ts' } },\n        },\n      ],\n    }\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, JSON.stringify(geminiData))\n\n    // 2. Parse\n    const parsed = parseGeminiTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.client).toBe('gemini')\n    expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('gemini-sess-001')\n    expect(parsed.cwd).toBe('/home/user/myproject')\n    expect(parsed.branch).toBe('main')\n    // user + model messages\n    expect(parsed.messages.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2)\n    expect(parsed.messages.find(m => m.role === 'user')?.text).toContain('unit test')\n    expect(parsed.messages.find(m => m.role === 'assistant')?.text).toContain('Vitest')\n    // tool event from toolUse\n    expect(parsed.tool_events.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)\n    expect(parsed.tool_events[0]!.summary).toContain('read_file')\n    expect(transcriptHasContent(parsed)).toBe(true)\n\n    // 3. Infer project slug from parent directory name\n    const slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)\n    expect(slug).toBe('myproject')\n\n    // 4. Import\n    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, slug, 'none', true)\n\n    // 5. Verify manifest fields\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('gemini')\n    expect(manifest.project).toBe('myproject')\n    expect(manifest.session_id).toBe('gemini-sess-001')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2)\n    expect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)\n\n    // 6. Verify clean markdown content\n    const cleanContent = readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path), 'utf-8')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('client: gemini')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('project: myproject')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Messages')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('unit test')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('Vitest')\n\n    // 7. Verify SQLite index row\n    const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')\n    expect(existsSync(dbPath)).toBe(true)\n    const SQL = await initSqlJs()\n    const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n    const rows = db.exec(\"SELECT client, project FROM transcripts WHERE client='gemini'\")\n    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)\n    expect(rows[0]!.values[0]).toEqual(['gemini', 'myproject'])\n    db.close()\n  })\n\n  it('Doubao TXT transcript end-to-end', async () => {\n    // 1. Create a realistic Doubao TXT transcript\n    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'doubao_chat_38416801786598914.txt')\n    const doubaoContent = [\n      'Title: TypeScript 最佳实践',\n      'URL: https://www.doubao.com/chat/38416801786598914',\n      'Platform: 豆包',\n      'Created: 2026-03-16 10:22:17',\n      'Messages: 2',\n      '',\n      'User: [2026-03-16 10:22:17]',\n      '',\n      'TypeScript 中如何处理可选链？',\n      '',\n      'AI: [2026-03-16 10:23:45]',\n      '',\n      '可选链操作符 ?. 可以安全访问深层属性，避免 TypeError。',\n      '例如：obj?.foo?.bar',\n      '',\n    ].join('\\n')\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, doubaoContent, 'utf-8')\n\n    // 2. Parse\n    const parsed = parseDoubaoTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.client).toBe('doubao')\n    expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('38416801786598914')\n    expect(parsed.title).toBe('TypeScript 最佳实践')\n    expect(parsed.started_at).toBe('2026-03-16T10:22:17')\n    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(parsed.messages[0]!.role).toBe('user')\n    expect(parsed.messages[0]!.text).toContain('可选链')\n    expect(parsed.messages[1]!.role).toBe('assistant')\n    expect(parsed.messages[1]!.text).toContain('?.')\n    expect(parsed.tool_events).toHaveLength(0)\n    expect(transcriptHasContent(parsed)).toBe(true)\n\n    // 3. Import (Doubao has no cwd, project slug falls back to 'unknown-project')\n    const slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)\n    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, slug, 'none', false)\n\n    // 4. Verify manifest fields\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('doubao')\n    expect(manifest.session_id).toBe('38416801786598914')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n    expect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBe(0)\n\n    // 5. Verify clean markdown content\n    const cleanContent = readFileSync(join(globalRoot, manifest.global_clean_path), 'utf-8')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('client: doubao')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Messages')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('可选链')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('?.')\n\n    // 6. Verify SQLite index row\n    const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')\n    expect(existsSync(dbPath)).toBe(true)\n    const SQL = await initSqlJs()\n    const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n    const rows = db.exec(\"SELECT client, session_id, title FROM transcripts WHERE client='doubao'\")\n    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)\n    expect(rows[0]!.values[0]).toEqual(['doubao', '38416801786598914', 'TypeScript 最佳实践'])\n    db.close()\n  })\n\n  it('Doubao CRLF transcript parses correctly end-to-end', async () => {\n    // Verify Windows CRLF line endings don't break the import pipeline\n    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'doubao_crlf.txt')\n    const lines = [\n      'Title: CRLF Test',\n      'URL: https://www.doubao.com/chat/99999',\n      'Created: 2026-03-17 08:00:00',\n      '',\n      'User: [2026-03-17 08:00:00]',\n      '',\n      'Hello CRLF world',\n      '',\n      'AI: [2026-03-17 08:01:00]',\n      '',\n      'CRLF response here',\n      '',\n    ]\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, lines.join('\\r\\n'), 'utf-8')\n\n    const parsed = parseDoubaoTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(parsed.messages[0]!.text).toBe('Hello CRLF world')\n    expect(parsed.messages[1]!.text).toBe('CRLF response here')\n\n    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'test-proj', 'none', false)\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('doubao')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n  })\n})"
}
