Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "Now I have sufficient context for a complete review. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: `packages/core/tests/transcript/integration.test.ts` — New Tests (lines 135–302)\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL\n\n**C1 — Gemini test: `inferProjectSlug` assertion does not actually exercise the Gemini code path**\n\nLine 179–180:\n```ts\nconst slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)\nexpect(slug).toBe('myproject')\n```\n\n`inferProjectSlug` falls back to the Gemini path **only when `parsed.cwd` is empty**. Because the test fixture has `cwd: '/home/user/myproject'`, the slug is derived from `basename(cwd)`, not from `basename(dirname(sourceFile))`. This means the Gemini-specific branch in `inferProjectSlug` (lines 117–123 of `discover.ts`) is never reached by this test.\n\nEither add a separate sub-case where `cwd` is absent and the parent-directory name drives the slug, or document that the cwd-based path is intentionally tested. As written, the test comment \"Infer project slug from parent directory name\" is **factually wrong** — it tests the `cwd` path.\n\n---\n\n**C2 — Gemini test: `toBeGreaterThanOrEqual` allows silent data loss**\n\nLines 189–190:\n```ts\nexpect(manifest.message_count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2)\nexpect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)\n```\n\nThe fixture has exactly 2 messages and 1 tool event. There is no deduplication or merging reason to expect more. Using `toBeGreaterThanOrEqual` means the test passes even if the parser emits spurious duplicates. The Gemini `visit()` traversal is recursive over all object values (see `parse.ts` line 350), so **duplicate emission is a real risk** — the `toolUse` block nested inside a `history` entry is visited both when the entry itself is processed and potentially again when child values are recursed. The test should pin the exact values and let any failures surface this.\n\nReplace with:\n```ts\nexpect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\nexpect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBe(1)\n```\n\nThe same issue appears at lines 170 and 174:\n```ts\nexpect(parsed.messages.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2)\nexpect(parsed.tool_events.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)\n```\n\n---\n\n### HIGH\n\n**H1 — Doubao test: `global_clean_path` is used as an absolute path without joining `globalRoot`**\n\nLine 258:\n```ts\nconst cleanContent = readFileSync(manifest.global_clean_path, 'utf-8')\n```\n\n`global_clean_path` is stored as an absolute path in the manifest (see `import.ts` line 46). The Gemini test at line 193 correctly does:\n```ts\nreadFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path), 'utf-8')\n```\nThe Doubao test uses a different field (`global_clean_path`) which happens to be absolute, so this works — but it is **inconsistent** with every other test in this file, which all read the repo-mirrored path. It also means the test does not verify that the repo mirror path is populated or readable (because `mirrorToRepo` is `false` for the Doubao test). This divergence should be documented explicitly or a separate assertion should verify what happens to `repo_clean_path` when `mirrorToRepo` is `false` (it should be an empty string — that is never asserted).\n\n**H2 — Doubao CRLF test: no import-level assertions about the clean markdown content**\n\nThe CRLF test (lines 275–302) only verifies:\n- `parsed.messages` count and text (parse only)\n- `manifest.client` and `manifest.message_count`\n\nIt does not verify that CRLF stripping survived all the way through to the written clean markdown file. The main value-add of a CRLF integration test is confirming the produced `.md` file does not contain stray `\\r` characters. Add:\n```ts\nconst cleanContent = readFileSync(manifest.global_clean_path, 'utf-8')\nexpect(cleanContent).not.toContain('\\r')\nexpect(cleanContent).toContain('Hello CRLF world')\nexpect(cleanContent).toContain('CRLF response here')\n```\n\n**H3 — Doubao test: `started_at` value is a naive local-time string but `normalizeTimestamp` behaviour depends on whether a timezone is appended**\n\nLine 238:\n```ts\nexpect(parsed.started_at).toBe('2026-03-16T10:22:17')\n```\n\n`parseDoubaoTranscript` sets `startedAt = raw` directly (no call to `normalizeTimestamp`) for the Created header value. This is intentional per the source comment (\"no timezone info\"). However, `normalizeTimestamp` is called on every message timestamp inside `parseIsoTimestamp`, which appends `+00:00` to bare ISO strings (treating them as UTC). The assertion tests the exact raw value and would catch a regression — that is correct. But the test does not verify that message timestamps are also stored as naive ISO strings (without the `Z` suffix that `normalizeTimestamp` would add). Add:\n```ts\nexpect(parsed.messages[0]!.timestamp).toBe('2026-03-16T10:22:17')\n```\n\n**H4 — Gemini test: `title` field is never verified after import**\n\nThe Gemini fixture has no `title` field in the JSON; the parser falls back to `stem` (`conversation`). The manifest and markdown `title` are never asserted, leaving a gap. The Codex and Claude tests both assert meaningful titles. Add:\n```ts\nexpect(manifest.title).toBe('conversation') // stem fallback\n```\nAnd assert `cleanContent` contains `# conversation`.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM\n\n**M1 — Gemini test: no Gemini JSONL variant tested**\n\nThe Gemini parser handles both `.json` and `.jsonl` extensions (parse.ts lines 295–299). Only the `.json` path is tested in the integration test. Since the dispatch branches on `extname(filePath)`, the `.jsonl` code path should have at least one integration test — either here or noted as a gap to be covered in `parse.test.ts`.\n\n**M2 — Doubao test: `SQLite title` column stores the raw Unicode title, but it is only asserted in the Doubao test, not the Gemini test**\n\nLine 271:\n```ts\nexpect(rows[0]!.values[0]).toEqual(['doubao', '38416801786598914', 'TypeScript 最佳实践'])\n```\n\nThis correctly queries three columns and asserts the full row. The Gemini SQLite query (line 205–207) only checks two columns and uses a string comparison rather than an exact-row assertion:\n```ts\nconst rows = db.exec(\"SELECT client, project FROM transcripts WHERE client='gemini'\")\nexpect(rows[0]!.values[0]).toEqual(['gemini', 'myproject'])\n```\nFor consistency and completeness, the Gemini SQLite assertion should also check `session_id` and `title`.\n\n**M3 — Gemini test: no assertion on `branch` field in `parsed` or `manifest`**\n\nThe Gemini fixture includes `branch: 'main'`. The test asserts `cwd` and `session_id` but never asserts `parsed.branch` or `manifest.branch`. This is a coverage gap for the metadata extraction path.\n\n**M4 — Both new tests: no verification of the event log (`sessions.jsonl`)**\n\nThe Codex test (lines 97–103) verifies that `sessions.jsonl` exists and contains a parseable event with the correct `client`. Neither the Gemini nor the Doubao test verifies the event log. The second import in the idempotent test proves that a second import with the same SHA does not append a duplicate — that logic is not exercised for Gemini or Doubao.\n\n**M5 — Doubao test: `slug` from `inferProjectSlug` is not asserted before being passed to `importTranscript`**\n\nLine 248–249:\n```ts\nconst slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)\nconst manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, slug, 'none', false)\n```\n\nSince Doubao has no `cwd` and no client-specific branch in `inferProjectSlug`, the slug should be `'unknown-project'`. This is never explicitly asserted. The Codex test asserts the slug (`expect(slug).toBe('myproject')`). Adding `expect(slug).toBe('unknown-project')` makes the intent explicit and prevents silent regressions if the fallback changes.\n\n**M6 — Doubao test: manifest fields `repo_raw_path`, `repo_clean_path`, `repo_manifest_path` not verified when `mirrorToRepo` is `false`**\n\nWhen `mirrorToRepo` is `false`, `import.ts` sets these three fields to `''`. The test never asserts that, meaning a regression where mirroring was accidentally triggered would go unnoticed.\n\n---\n\n### LOW\n\n**L1 — Gemini test: dynamic `mkdirSync` import instead of static import at the top of the file**\n\nLine 138:\n```ts\nconst { mkdirSync } = await import('node:fs')\n```\n\n`mkdirSync` is already available from `node:fs` in the same test environment. All other tests use static imports at the top of the file. This dynamic import is unnecessary and should be a static import for consistency.\n\n**L2 — Gemini test: SQL query uses a string WHERE clause that is `client='gemini'` but the existing Codex test uses a parameterless query**\n\nLines 205–206 use a hardcoded SQL filter; lines 92–94 use a plain `SELECT ... FROM transcripts`. Both approaches work, but for consistency and to avoid potential SQL injection patterns in tests that future maintainers might copy, the integration tests should use either prepared statements or consistent patterns.\n\n**L3 — Doubao CRLF test: `session_id` defaults to filename stem (`doubao_crlf`) rather than a URL-derived ID**\n\nThe fixture omits a `URL:` header line, so `session_id` defaults to the stem `doubao_crlf`. The test does not assert the `session_id` value, leaving the fallback behavior untested. Add `expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('doubao_crlf')` or a similarly explicit check.\n\n**L4 — No test for a Doubao file detected via `/doubao/` path (vs. `doubao_` filename prefix)**\n\n`inferClient` detects Doubao via two conditions: `fileName.startsWith('doubao_')` or `normalized.includes('/doubao/')`. Only the filename-prefix path is exercised. The path-directory variant is not tested in either the integration or unit tests.\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table\n\n| ID | Severity | Description |\n|----|----------|-------------|\n| C1 | CRITICAL | Gemini `inferProjectSlug` comment and assertion test wrong code path (cwd, not parent-dir) |\n| C2 | CRITICAL | `toBeGreaterThanOrEqual` masks potential Gemini parser duplication bugs — pin exact counts |\n| H1 | HIGH | Doubao reads `global_clean_path` absolute; inconsistent with other tests, `repo_clean_path=''` never asserted |\n| H2 | HIGH | CRLF test never verifies the written markdown is free of `\\r` characters |\n| H3 | HIGH | Doubao message timestamps (naive ISO vs. UTC-normalised) never asserted |\n| H4 | HIGH | Gemini `title` field in manifest and clean markdown never verified |\n| M1 | MEDIUM | Gemini JSONL variant never covered in integration tests |\n| M2 | MEDIUM | Gemini SQLite row assertion weaker than Doubao (only 2 columns, no `session_id`/`title`) |\n| M3 | MEDIUM | Gemini `branch` field never asserted after parse |\n| M4 | MEDIUM | Neither Gemini nor Doubao test verifies `sessions.jsonl` event log |\n| M5 | MEDIUM | Doubao `inferProjectSlug` result never explicitly asserted before being used |\n| M6 | MEDIUM | Doubao test never asserts `repo_*_path` fields are empty strings when `mirrorToRepo=false` |\n| L1 | LOW | Gemini test uses dynamic `await import('node:fs')` for `mkdirSync` — use static import |\n| L2 | LOW | Gemini SQL uses inline WHERE string; inconsistent query style with Codex test |\n| L3 | LOW | Doubao CRLF test never asserts `session_id` stem fallback value |\n| L4 | LOW | `/doubao/` path-based client inference never exercised |",
    "type": "text"
  },

[
  {
    "text": "I now have everything needed for a thorough review. Here are my findings:\n\n---\n\n## Independent Review: Integration Tests — Gemini, Doubao, Doubao CRLF\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL\n\n**[CRITICAL-1] Doubao `started_at` assertion will fail: naive string vs UTC-normalized string**\n\nTest line 238:\n```ts\nexpect(parsed.started_at).toBe('2026-03-16T10:22:17')\n```\n\nIn `parseDoubaoTranscript` (parse.ts line 427), the `Created:` header value is assigned directly: `startedAt = raw` where `raw = \"2026-03-16T10:22:17\"`. This bypasses `normalizeTimestamp`, so the value is stored verbatim as `'2026-03-16T10:22:17'` (no `Z`).\n\nHowever, the Doubao turn `timestamp` fields follow the same pattern (parse.ts line 451: `const timestamp = (match[2] ?? '').trim().replace(' ', 'T')`), so message timestamps will also be naive ISO without `Z`.\n\nThe assertion `toBe('2026-03-16T10:22:17')` is technically correct for the current parser behavior — the raw value is assigned directly. **But** the Gemini parser takes the same timestamp through `normalizeTimestamp` → `parseIsoTimestamp` → treats it as UTC → outputs `'2026-03-16T10:22:17Z'`. This inconsistency means the Doubao test assertion is testing the current (potentially unintended) behavior of not running through `normalizeTimestamp`. If the parser is later corrected to call `normalizeTimestamp(raw)`, this assertion will break with `'2026-03-16T10:22:17Z'`. The test documents the current behavior but does not flag the inconsistency as something to watch.\n\n**Severity**: CRITICAL — the assertion is coupled to an implementation detail that differs from every other parser, and it will silently start failing if the parser is normalized.\n\n---\n\n**[CRITICAL-2] Gemini slug inference test is correct in isolation but tests the wrong path for real Gemini files**\n\nTest lines 137-138 create the source file at:\n```\n<tmpDir>/myproject/conversation.json\n```\n\n`inferProjectSlug` for Gemini (discover.ts lines 117-122) uses `basename(dirname(parsed.source_path))` — the **parent directory name**. Here that is `'myproject'`. Test line 180 asserts `slug === 'myproject'`. This is correct for this fixture.\n\nHowever: the test's Gemini fixture also provides `cwd: '/home/user/myproject'` (fixture line 145). `inferProjectSlug` checks `parsed.cwd` **first** (discover.ts line 102). Since `cwd` is non-empty, it uses `basename('/home/user/myproject')` = `'myproject'`. The parent-directory slug path for Gemini is never actually exercised by this test — the cwd path is hit instead.\n\nThe comment \"Infer project slug from parent directory name\" (test line 178) is therefore **misleading**: it claims to test the Gemini-specific parent-directory logic, but the cwd branch wins. A separate fixture without a `cwd` field would be needed to actually test that path.\n\n**Severity**: CRITICAL — the test comment documents intent that is never executed, creating false confidence in the Gemini-specific slug branch.\n\n---\n\n### HIGH\n\n**[HIGH-1] `global_clean_path` is read as an absolute path — correct, but `repo_clean_path` is read incorrectly**\n\nDoubao test line 258:\n```ts\nconst cleanContent = readFileSync(manifest.global_clean_path, 'utf-8')\n```\n\nLooking at import.ts lines 80 and 78:\n- `global_clean_path` is assigned `toPosixPath(globalCleanPath)` — an **absolute** path. Correct to use directly with `readFileSync`.\n- `repo_clean_path` is assigned `toPosixPath(relative(root, repoCleanPath))` — a **relative** path (relative to `root`, which is the `repoRoot` parameter of `importTranscript`).\n\nIn the pre-existing Codex and Gemini tests, `repo_clean_path` is read as (e.g. test line 68):\n```ts\nreadFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path), 'utf-8')\n```\n\nThis is correct. The Doubao test avoids `repo_clean_path` entirely (mirror is disabled: `mirrorToRepo=false`), so this distinction does not cause a test failure for Doubao. However, the inconsistency between `global_clean_path` (absolute) and `repo_clean_path` (relative) is not surfaced by any comment or assertion in the new tests, increasing maintenance risk.\n\n**Severity**: HIGH — the path semantics are not documented by the tests, and a future developer could incorrectly use `global_clean_path` relative to `globalRoot` or `repo_clean_path` as absolute, causing silent test failures.\n\n---\n\n**[HIGH-2] Gemini tool event double-counting: the `toolUse` node will be visited twice**\n\nThe Gemini fixture (test lines 155-158) places `toolUse` inside a history entry that already has a `role`:\n```json\n{\n  \"role\": \"model\",\n  \"parts\": [{ \"text\": \"...\" }],\n  \"toolUse\": { \"name\": \"read_file\", \"input\": { \"path\": \"test.ts\" } }\n}\n```\n\nIn `parseGeminiTranscript`, the `visit` function (parse.ts line 310) processes a node and then recursively visits all its values (line 350: `for (const value of Object.values(rec)) { visit(value) }`).\n\nFor the `model` node:\n1. `toolName` is found via `getNested(rec, 'toolUse', 'name')` → pushes one tool event with `\"read_file\"`.\n2. Then recursive `visit` is called on each value, including `toolUse: { name: 'read_file', input: {...} }`.\n3. Inside that nested `visit`, `rec['toolName']` is undefined but `rec['tool']` is also undefined; however `rec['name']` is `'read_file'`. The check is: `getNested(rec, 'toolUse', 'name')` — this resolves to null (no `toolUse` inside `toolUse`). Then `rec['toolName']` and `rec['functionName']` are checked — both undefined. `rec['tool']` is undefined.\n\nActually on re-reading: the inner object `{ name: 'read_file', input: {...} }` has no `role` and no `toolName`/`functionName`/`tool` field. `getNested` on this inner node for `'toolUse'` returns null. So no second tool event is pushed from the inner node.\n\nBut wait — let me check the `input` object `{ path: 'test.ts' }` visited next. It has no role and no tool indicator fields. So no double-counting occurs for this specific fixture.\n\nThe test assertion `toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)` (line 174) would pass regardless of double-counting, making it a weak guard. **The assertion does not verify the exact count is 1**, so if the parser does produce 2 tool events via a future fixture change, this test would still pass. This is a looseness issue rather than a current bug.\n\n**Severity**: HIGH — using `toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)` instead of `toBe(1)` for tool events leaves double-counting bugs undetectable.\n\n---\n\n**[HIGH-3] Doubao session_id extraction: URL query string not handled**\n\nThe parser extracts session_id via `url.split('/').pop()` (parse.ts line 417). The test URL `https://www.doubao.com/chat/38416801786598914` has no query string, so `pop()` returns `'38416801786598914'` correctly.\n\nBut real Doubao URLs may include query parameters such as `?source=...` or fragment identifiers. For example:\n```\nhttps://www.doubao.com/chat/38416801786598914?conv=abc\n```\nIn that case `pop()` returns `'38416801786598914?conv=abc'`, making `session_id` include the query string. The test does not cover this case. If this is a real-world scenario, both the parser and a corresponding test are missing it.\n\n**Severity**: HIGH — if real exports include query strings, session IDs will be incorrect and SQLite lookups will silently fail deduplication.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM\n\n**[MEDIUM-1] Gemini fixture: `metaKeys` set does not include `'branch'`, but the test expects `parsed.branch === 'main'`**\n\nThe `metaKeys` set in the parser is `new Set(['sessionId', 'chatId', 'cwd', 'branch', 'timestamp'])` (parse.ts line 301). The `findFirstMappingWithKeys` function returns the first node that contains **any** of those keys (common.ts line 284). The Gemini fixture root object has `sessionId`, `cwd`, `branch`, `timestamp` — so it matches and `firstMeta` is the root object. `branch = String(firstMeta['branch'] ?? '')` correctly reads `'main'`.\n\nThis works here, but the key `'branch'` being in `metaKeys` is what causes the root node to be matched (it also has `sessionId` anyway). Test line 167 asserting `parsed.branch === 'main'` is valid. No bug here, but noting it for awareness.\n\n**[MEDIUM-2] CRLF test message text assertions: potential trailing carriage return**\n\nTest lines 296-297:\n```ts\nexpect(parsed.messages[0]!.text).toBe('Hello CRLF world')\nexpect(parsed.messages[1]!.text).toBe('CRLF response here')\n```\n\nThe parser splits on `/\\r?\\n/` (parse.ts line 394), which correctly removes `\\r`. The body is then collected line-by-line with `bodyLines.push(bodyLine)` — each `bodyLine` is a result of the split so is already free of `\\r`. The final `bodyLines.join('\\n').trim()` also trims. These assertions are correct.\n\nHowever: the fixture is missing a `Platform:` header line (which the non-CRLF test includes), and is missing `Messages:` count. The parser silently ignores these missing optional fields. This is fine, but the CRLF test fixture is slightly less realistic than the main Doubao test.\n\n**[MEDIUM-3] Gemini test reads `repo_clean_path` via `join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path)` — correct, but `repoRoot` inside `importTranscript` is not the same as the `repoRoot` variable in the test**\n\nIn the test, `repoRoot = join(tmpDir, 'repo')`.\n\nIn `importTranscript` (import.ts line 40): `const repoRoot = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts')`. The `root` parameter is what the test passes as `repoRoot` (i.e. `join(tmpDir, 'repo')`). So the actual transcript storage root is `join(tmpDir, 'repo', 'Memory', '06_transcripts')`.\n\n`manifest.repo_clean_path` is `relative(root, repoCleanPath)` = `relative(join(tmpDir, 'repo'), ...)` which resolves to `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/gemini/...`.\n\nTest line 193: `readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path), 'utf-8')` = `join(join(tmpDir, 'repo'), 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/...')`. This is correct. No bug.\n\n**[MEDIUM-4] No assertion that `repo_clean_path` is relative (not absolute) in the Gemini test**\n\nThe review task explicitly asks whether path assertions verify relativity. The Gemini test does not assert that `manifest.repo_clean_path` is a relative path (e.g., does not contain the tmpdir prefix). This is an implicit trust that the production code returns a relative path. A direct assertion like:\n```ts\nexpect(manifest.repo_clean_path.startsWith('Memory/')).toBe(true)\n```\nwould make the contract explicit.\n\n**Severity for MEDIUM-4**: MEDIUM — missing explicit path-type assertion, relying on `join(repoRoot, ...)` working even if an absolute path is accidentally returned.\n\n---\n\n**[MEDIUM-5] No Chinese text round-trip assertion in the Doubao test's SQLite query**\n\nTest line 271 correctly asserts:\n```ts\nexpect(rows[0]!.values[0]).toEqual(['doubao', '38416801786598914', 'TypeScript 最佳实践'])\n```\n\nThis verifies that the Chinese title is stored and retrieved correctly from SQLite. Good. However, the message bodies (`可选链`, `?.`) are not verified in SQLite (not stored there — only title/session are indexed), and the clean markdown assertion at line 261-262 only checks `toContain('可选链')` and `toContain('?.')`, which is adequate.\n\nNo gap here. The Chinese text handling is adequately covered.\n\n---\n\n### LOW\n\n**[LOW-1] Gemini test missing `cleanContent` assertion for tool event content**\n\nThe Codex end-to-end test asserts `cleanContent.toContain('read_file')` (line 78) for tool events. The Gemini test verifies `tool_events.length >= 1` and `tool_events[0].summary.toContain('read_file')`, but does **not** verify that the tool event summary appears in the clean markdown output. A `toContain('read_file')` on `cleanContent` would complete the round-trip.\n\n**[LOW-2] Gemini JSONL format not tested**\n\nThe parser handles `.jsonl` extension differently (parse.ts lines 295-298): it calls `loadJsonl(filePath)` instead of `JSON.parse(readFileSync(...))`. The test only exercises the `.json` branch. A JSONL Gemini format test is absent.\n\n**[LOW-3] Doubao test does not verify `global_raw_path` is absolute**\n\nThe review task asked about `global_clean_path` being absolute. Test line 258 uses `manifest.global_clean_path` directly with `readFileSync` (no `join`), implicitly asserting it is absolute. This works correctly. For completeness, an explicit assertion like `expect(path.isAbsolute(manifest.global_clean_path)).toBe(true)` would document the contract.\n\n**[LOW-4] The `'Platform: 豆包'` header line is not tested for graceful skipping**\n\nThe non-CRLF Doubao fixture includes `'Platform: 豆包'` (line 218). The parser has no explicit handling for `Platform:` — it simply falls through the `if` chain without matching, so it is silently ignored. This is fine, but no test asserts that a file **without** `Platform:` also parses correctly (the CRLF fixture does implicitly test this).\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| ID | Severity | Description |\n|----|----------|-------------|\n| CRITICAL-1 | CRITICAL | `started_at` assertion uses naive ISO (no Z) — documents inconsistent behavior vs all other parsers; will break if parser is normalized |\n| CRITICAL-2 | CRITICAL | Gemini slug test comment claims to test parent-dir logic but cwd branch is hit first; the Gemini-specific path is never exercised |\n| HIGH-1 | HIGH | `global_clean_path` (absolute) vs `repo_clean_path` (relative) contract not made explicit in new tests |\n| HIGH-2 | HIGH | Gemini tool events asserted with `>= 1` instead of exact count — hides potential double-counting bugs |\n| HIGH-3 | HIGH | Doubao session_id extraction not tested with URL containing query string or fragment |\n| MEDIUM-2 | MEDIUM | CRLF fixture missing `Platform:` and `Messages:` headers — slightly unrealistic |\n| MEDIUM-4 | MEDIUM | No explicit assertion that `repo_clean_path` is relative (not absolute) |\n| LOW-1 | LOW | Gemini tool event not verified in clean markdown output |\n| LOW-2 | LOW | Gemini JSONL format branch not tested at all |\n| LOW-3 | LOW | `global_clean_path` absoluteness not explicitly asserted |\n| LOW-4 | LOW | `Platform:` header skip behavior not directly asserted |",

Write input={"content":"import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, existsSync, rmSync } from 'node:f...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\tests\\transcript\\integration.test.ts",
  "content": "import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, existsSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { isAbsolute, join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\nimport initSqlJs from 'sql.js'\n\nimport {\n  parseCodexTranscript,\n  parseGeminiTranscript,\n  parseDoubaoTranscript,\n} from '../../src/transcript/parse.js'\nimport { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '../../src/transcript/import.js'\nimport { inferProjectSlug } from '../../src/transcript/discover.js'\n\ndescribe('integration: parse → import → verify', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n  let repoRoot: string\n  let globalRoot: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'integration-'))\n    repoRoot = join(tmpDir, 'repo')\n    globalRoot = join(tmpDir, 'global')\n  })\n  afterEach(() => {\n    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n  })\n\n  it('end-to-end: create JSONL → parse → import → verify files', async () => {\n    // 1. Create a realistic Codex JSONL transcript\n    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'test-session.jsonl')\n    const records = [\n      { type: 'session_meta', payload: { id: 'sess-001', title: 'Fix bug', cwd: '/home/user/myproject', git: { branch: 'main' }, timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:00Z' } },\n      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:01Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'input_text', text: 'Fix the login bug' }] } },\n      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:02Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'I will fix the login bug for you.' }] } },\n      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:03Z', payload: { type: 'function_call', name: 'read_file', arguments: { path: 'login.ts' } } },\n      { type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:04Z', payload: { type: 'function_call_output', name: 'read_file', output: 'file contents here' } },\n    ]\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, records.map(r => JSON.stringify(r)).join('\\n') + '\\n')\n\n    // 2. Parse\n    const parsed = parseCodexTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.client).toBe('codex')\n    expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('sess-001')\n    expect(parsed.title).toBe('Fix bug')\n    expect(parsed.cwd).toBe('/home/user/myproject')\n    expect(parsed.branch).toBe('main')\n    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(parsed.tool_events).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(transcriptHasContent(parsed)).toBe(true)\n\n    // 3. Infer project slug\n    const slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)\n    expect(slug).toBe('myproject')\n\n    // 4. Import\n    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, slug, 'none', true)\n\n    // 5. Verify manifest fields\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('codex')\n    expect(manifest.project).toBe('myproject')\n    expect(manifest.session_id).toBe('sess-001')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n    expect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBe(2)\n    expect(manifest.cleaning_mode).toBe('deterministic-code')\n    expect(manifest.repo_mirror_enabled).toBe(true)\n\n    // 6. Verify raw file copied to repo + global\n    expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_raw_path))).toBe(true)\n\n    // 7. Verify clean markdown has correct frontmatter\n    const cleanContent = readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path), 'utf-8')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('---')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('client: codex')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('project: myproject')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Messages')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('### 1. user')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('Fix the login bug')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('### 2. assistant')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('I will fix the login bug for you.')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Tool Events')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('read_file')\n\n    // 8. Verify manifest JSON written\n    const manifestContent = JSON.parse(\n      readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_manifest_path), 'utf-8'),\n    )\n    expect(manifestContent.client).toBe('codex')\n    expect(manifestContent.project).toBe('myproject')\n\n    // 9. Verify SQLite index updated\n    const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')\n    expect(existsSync(dbPath)).toBe(true)\n    const SQL = await initSqlJs()\n    const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n    const rows = db.exec('SELECT client, project, session_id FROM transcripts')\n    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)\n    expect(rows[0]!.values).toEqual([['codex', 'myproject', 'sess-001']])\n    db.close()\n\n    // 10. Verify event log written\n    const eventLogPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')\n    expect(existsSync(eventLogPath)).toBe(true)\n    const eventLines = readFileSync(eventLogPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\\n')\n    expect(eventLines).toHaveLength(1)\n    const event = JSON.parse(eventLines[0]!)\n    expect(event.client).toBe('codex')\n  })\n\n  it('idempotent re-import preserves imported_at', async () => {\n    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'idem.jsonl')\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, '{\"type\":\"session_meta\",\"payload\":{\"id\":\"s1\",\"title\":\"test\"}}\\n')\n\n    const parsed = parseCodexTranscript(sourceFile)\n    const first = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'proj', 'none')\n    const second = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'proj', 'none')\n\n    expect(second.imported_at).toBe(first.imported_at)\n  })\n\n  it('Claude transcript end-to-end', async () => {\n    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'claude-session.jsonl')\n    const records = [\n      { type: 'user', sessionId: 'cs1', cwd: '/proj', gitBranch: 'dev', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:00Z', message: { role: 'user', content: 'Hello Claude' } },\n      { type: 'assistant', sessionId: 'cs1', timestamp: '2024-06-15T10:00:01Z', message: { role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Hello!' }] } },\n    ]\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, records.map(r => JSON.stringify(r)).join('\\n') + '\\n')\n\n    const { parseClaudeTranscript } = await import('../../src/transcript/parse.js')\n    const parsed = parseClaudeTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.client).toBe('claude')\n    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)\n\n    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'test-proj', 'none')\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('claude')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n  })\n\n  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Gemini\n  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n  it('Gemini JSON transcript end-to-end', async () => {\n    // Place source under a named directory — this becomes the parent-dir slug\n    // when cwd is absent (tested separately below).  Here cwd is present and\n    // drives the slug via the cwd-first path in inferProjectSlug.\n    const geminiDir = join(tmpDir, 'myproject')\n    mkdirSync(geminiDir, { recursive: true })\n    const sourceFile = join(geminiDir, 'conversation.json')\n\n    const geminiData = {\n      sessionId: 'gemini-sess-001',\n      cwd: '/home/user/myproject',\n      branch: 'main',\n      timestamp: '2024-08-10T09:00:00Z',\n      history: [\n        {\n          role: 'user',\n          timestamp: '2024-08-10T09:00:01Z',\n          parts: [{ text: 'How do I write a unit test?' }],\n        },\n        {\n          role: 'model',\n          timestamp: '2024-08-10T09:00:02Z',\n          parts: [{ text: 'Use a testing framework like Vitest or Jest.' }],\n          toolUse: { name: 'read_file', input: { path: 'test.ts' } },\n        },\n      ],\n    }\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, JSON.stringify(geminiData))\n\n    // 2. Parse\n    const parsed = parseGeminiTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.client).toBe('gemini')\n    expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('gemini-sess-001')\n    expect(parsed.cwd).toBe('/home/user/myproject')\n    expect(parsed.branch).toBe('main')\n    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(parsed.messages.find(m => m.role === 'user')?.text).toContain('unit test')\n    expect(parsed.messages.find(m => m.role === 'assistant')?.text).toContain('Vitest')\n    expect(parsed.tool_events).toHaveLength(1)\n    expect(parsed.tool_events[0]!.summary).toContain('read_file')\n    expect(transcriptHasContent(parsed)).toBe(true)\n\n    // 3. Slug is derived from cwd (cwd-first path in inferProjectSlug)\n    const slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)\n    expect(slug).toBe('myproject')\n\n    // 4. Import\n    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, slug, 'none', true)\n\n    // 5. Verify manifest fields\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('gemini')\n    expect(manifest.project).toBe('myproject')\n    expect(manifest.session_id).toBe('gemini-sess-001')\n    expect(manifest.title).toBe('conversation')   // stem fallback — no title field in Gemini JSON\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n    expect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBe(1)\n    // repo paths are relative (not absolute)\n    expect(isAbsolute(manifest.repo_clean_path)).toBe(false)\n    expect(manifest.repo_clean_path.startsWith('Memory/')).toBe(true)\n\n    // 6. Verify clean markdown content\n    const cleanContent = readFileSync(join(repoRoot, manifest.repo_clean_path), 'utf-8')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('client: gemini')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('project: myproject')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Messages')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('unit test')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('Vitest')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('read_file')   // tool event appears in clean markdown\n\n    // 7. Verify SQLite index row (client, project, session_id)\n    const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')\n    expect(existsSync(dbPath)).toBe(true)\n    const SQL = await initSqlJs()\n    const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n    const rows = db.exec(\"SELECT client, project, session_id FROM transcripts WHERE client='gemini'\")\n    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)\n    expect(rows[0]!.values[0]).toEqual(['gemini', 'myproject', 'gemini-sess-001'])\n    db.close()\n\n    // 8. Verify event log\n    const eventLogPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')\n    expect(existsSync(eventLogPath)).toBe(true)\n    const event = JSON.parse(readFileSync(eventLogPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\\n')[0]!)\n    expect(event.client).toBe('gemini')\n  })\n\n  it('Gemini: inferProjectSlug uses parent directory name when cwd is absent', async () => {\n    // This test exercises the Gemini-specific slug fallback in inferProjectSlug\n    // (discover.ts lines 117-122) which kicks in only when cwd is empty.\n    const geminiDir = join(tmpDir, 'slug-from-dir')\n    mkdirSync(geminiDir, { recursive: true })\n    const sourceFile = join(geminiDir, 'session.json')\n    // No 'cwd' field — triggers the parent-directory slug path for gemini\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, JSON.stringify({\n      sessionId: 'g-no-cwd',\n      history: [\n        { role: 'user', parts: [{ text: 'Hello' }] },\n        { role: 'model', parts: [{ text: 'Hi there' }] },\n      ],\n    }))\n\n    const parsed = parseGeminiTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.cwd).toBe('')\n\n    const slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)\n    expect(slug).toBe('slug-from-dir')\n  })\n\n  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Doubao\n  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n  it('Doubao TXT transcript end-to-end', async () => {\n    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'doubao_chat_38416801786598914.txt')\n    const doubaoContent = [\n      'Title: TypeScript 最佳实践',\n      'URL: https://www.doubao.com/chat/38416801786598914',\n      'Platform: 豆包',\n      'Created: 2026-03-16 10:22:17',\n      'Messages: 2',\n      '',\n      'User: [2026-03-16 10:22:17]',\n      '',\n      'TypeScript 中如何处理可选链？',\n      '',\n      'AI: [2026-03-16 10:23:45]',\n      '',\n      '可选链操作符 ?. 可以安全访问深层属性，避免 TypeError。',\n      '例如：obj?.foo?.bar',\n      '',\n    ].join('\\n')\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, doubaoContent, 'utf-8')\n\n    // 2. Parse\n    const parsed = parseDoubaoTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.client).toBe('doubao')\n    expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('38416801786598914')\n    expect(parsed.title).toBe('TypeScript 最佳实践')\n    // Doubao exports user-local time with no timezone — stored as naive ISO (no Z)\n    expect(parsed.started_at).toBe('2026-03-16T10:22:17')\n    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(parsed.messages[0]!.role).toBe('user')\n    expect(parsed.messages[0]!.text).toContain('可选链')\n    // Message timestamps are also naive ISO (no Z suffix)\n    expect(parsed.messages[0]!.timestamp).toBe('2026-03-16T10:22:17')\n    expect(parsed.messages[1]!.role).toBe('assistant')\n    expect(parsed.messages[1]!.text).toContain('?.')\n    expect(parsed.messages[1]!.timestamp).toBe('2026-03-16T10:23:45')\n    expect(parsed.tool_events).toHaveLength(0)\n    expect(transcriptHasContent(parsed)).toBe(true)\n\n    // 3. No cwd → slug falls back to 'unknown-project'\n    const slug = inferProjectSlug(parsed)\n    expect(slug).toBe('unknown-project')\n\n    // 4. Import (not mirrored to repo — Doubao is a global-only import)\n    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, slug, 'none', false)\n\n    // 5. Verify manifest fields\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('doubao')\n    expect(manifest.session_id).toBe('38416801786598914')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n    expect(manifest.tool_event_count).toBe(0)\n    // When mirrorToRepo=false, repo paths must be empty strings\n    expect(manifest.repo_raw_path).toBe('')\n    expect(manifest.repo_clean_path).toBe('')\n    expect(manifest.repo_manifest_path).toBe('')\n    // global_clean_path is absolute\n    expect(isAbsolute(manifest.global_clean_path)).toBe(true)\n\n    // 6. Verify clean markdown content (read via absolute global_clean_path)\n    const cleanContent = readFileSync(manifest.global_clean_path, 'utf-8')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('client: doubao')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('## Messages')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('可选链')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('?.')\n\n    // 7. Verify SQLite index row (Chinese title stored correctly)\n    const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')\n    expect(existsSync(dbPath)).toBe(true)\n    const SQL = await initSqlJs()\n    const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))\n    const rows = db.exec(\"SELECT client, session_id, title FROM transcripts WHERE client='doubao'\")\n    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)\n    expect(rows[0]!.values[0]).toEqual(['doubao', '38416801786598914', 'TypeScript 最佳实践'])\n    db.close()\n\n    // 8. Verify event log\n    const eventLogPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')\n    expect(existsSync(eventLogPath)).toBe(true)\n    const event = JSON.parse(readFileSync(eventLogPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\\n')[0]!)\n    expect(event.client).toBe('doubao')\n  })\n\n  it('Doubao CRLF transcript parses and imports correctly', async () => {\n    const sourceFile = join(tmpDir, 'doubao_crlf.txt')\n    const lines = [\n      'Title: CRLF Test',\n      'URL: https://www.doubao.com/chat/99999',\n      'Platform: 豆包',\n      'Created: 2026-03-17 08:00:00',\n      'Messages: 2',\n      '',\n      'User: [2026-03-17 08:00:00]',\n      '',\n      'Hello CRLF world',\n      '',\n      'AI: [2026-03-17 08:01:00]',\n      '',\n      'CRLF response here',\n      '',\n    ]\n    writeFileSync(sourceFile, lines.join('\\r\\n'), 'utf-8')\n\n    // 2. Parse — CRLF must be stripped cleanly\n    const parsed = parseDoubaoTranscript(sourceFile)\n    expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('99999')\n    expect(parsed.messages).toHaveLength(2)\n    expect(parsed.messages[0]!.text).toBe('Hello CRLF world')\n    expect(parsed.messages[1]!.text).toBe('CRLF response here')\n\n    // 3. Import\n    const manifest = await importTranscript(parsed, repoRoot, globalRoot, 'test-proj', 'none', false)\n    expect(manifest.client).toBe('doubao')\n    expect(manifest.message_count).toBe(2)\n\n    // 4. Verify the written clean markdown is free of carriage returns\n    const cleanContent = readFileSync(manifest.global_clean_path, 'utf-8')\n    expect(cleanContent).not.toContain('\\r')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('Hello CRLF world')\n    expect(cleanContent).toContain('CRLF response here')\n  })\n})\n"
}

621 tests 全绿。高优先级第一项（Gemini + Doubao E2E）完成。

1→import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→
     3→import {
     4→  heartbeatScriptPath,
     5→  isLaunchdRegistered,
     6→  cmdUninstall,
     7→} from '../../src/cmd-daemon.js'
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// heartbeatScriptPath
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→describe('heartbeatScriptPath', () => {
    14→  it('returns a path ending with cli.js', () => {
    15→    const scriptPath = heartbeatScriptPath()
    16→    expect(scriptPath).toMatch(/cli\.js$/)
    17→  })
    18→
    19→  it('contains dist directory in the path', () => {
    20→    const scriptPath = heartbeatScriptPath()
    21→    expect(scriptPath).toContain('dist')
    22→  })
    23→})
    24→
    25→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→// cmdStatus — tested via dynamic import with mocks set before import
    27→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→
    29→describe('cmdStatus', () => {
    30→  let stdoutChunks: string[]
    31→  const originalWrite = process.stdout.write
    32→
    33→  beforeEach(() => {
    34→    stdoutChunks = []
    35→    process.stdout.write = ((chunk: string) => {
    36→      stdoutChunks.push(chunk)
    37→      return true
    38→    }) as typeof process.stdout.write
    39→  })
    40→
    41→  afterEach(() => {
    42→    process.stdout.write = originalWrite
    43→    vi.restoreAllMocks()
    44→    vi.resetModules()
    45→  })
    46→
    47→  it('returns 0 and outputs platform and lock info (no lock)', async () => {
    48→    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/sync', () => ({
    49→      readLockPid: () => null,
    50→      configPath: () => '/nonexistent/config.toml',
    51→      loadConfig: () => ({
    52→        heartbeat_interval: '5m',
    53→        auto_push: true,
    54→        projects: [],
    55→        watch_dirs: [],
    56→        log_level: 'info',
    57→      }),
    58→      intervalToSeconds: () => 300,
    59→      saveConfig: () => {},
    60→    }))
    61→
    62→    const mod = await import('../../src/cmd-daemon.js')
    63→    const result = mod.cmdStatus()
    64→    expect(result).toBe(0)
    65→
    66→    const output = stdoutChunks.join('')
    67→    expect(output).toContain('Platform:')
    68→    expect(output).toContain('Registered:')
    69→    expect(output).toContain('Lock:       not held')
    70→  })
    71→
    72→  it('shows lock held when PID exists', async () => {
    73→    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/sync', () => ({
    74→      readLockPid: () => 12345,
    75→      configPath: () => '/nonexistent/config.toml',
    76→      loadConfig: () => ({
    77→        heartbeat_interval: '5m',
    78→        auto_push: true,
    79→        projects: [],
    80→        watch_dirs: [],
    81→        log_level: 'info',
    82→      }),
    83→      intervalToSeconds: () => 300,
    84→      saveConfig: () => {},
    85→    }))
    86→
    87→    const mod = await import('../../src/cmd-daemon.js')
    88→    const result = mod.cmdStatus()
    89→    expect(result).toBe(0)
    90→
    91→    const output = stdoutChunks.join('')
    92→    expect(output).toContain('held by PID 12345')
    93→  })
    94→})
    95→
    96→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    97→// Platform detection helpers
    98→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    99→
   100→describe('isCronRegistered', () => {
   101→  afterEach(() => {
   102→    vi.restoreAllMocks()
   103→    vi.resetModules()
   104→  })
   105→
   106→  it('returns false when crontab output is empty', async () => {
   107→    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
   108→      execCommand: () => '',
   109→    }))
   110→    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/sync', () => ({

   112→      loadConfig: () => ({ heartbeat_interval: '5m', auto_push: true, projects: [], watch_dirs: [], log_level: 'info' }),

   120→})
   121→

   124→    // isLaunchdRegistered checks existsSync of the plist path
   125→    // In test env, the plist will not exist

   127→  })
   128→})
   129→

   137→    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
   138→      execCommand: () => { throw new Error('not found') },
   139→    }))
   140→    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/sync', () => ({
   141→      configPath: () => '/nonexistent/config.toml',
   142→      loadConfig: () => ({ heartbeat_interval: '5m', auto_push: true, projects: [], watch_dirs: [], log_level: 'info' }),
   143→      intervalToSeconds: () => 300,
   144→      saveConfig: () => {},
   145→    }))
   146→
   147→    const mod = await import('../../src/cmd-daemon.js')
   148→    expect(mod.isSchtasksRegistered()).toBe(false)
   149→  })
   150→})
   151→
   152→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   153→// cmdInstall — test saveConfig is called with overrides
   154→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   155→
   156→describe('cmdInstall', () => {
   157→  afterEach(() => {
   158→    vi.restoreAllMocks()
   159→    vi.resetModules()
   160→  })
   161→
   162→  it('calls saveConfig with overridden interval and auto-push', async () => {
   163→    const savedConfigs: unknown[] = []
   164→    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/sync', () => ({
   165→      loadConfig: () => ({
   166→        heartbeat_interval: '5m',
   167→        auto_push: true,
   168→        projects: [],
   169→        watch_dirs: [],
   170→        log_level: 'info',
   171→      }),
   172→      saveConfig: (cfg: unknown) => { savedConfigs.push(cfg) },
   173→      intervalToSeconds: () => 600,
   174→      configPath: () => '/nonexistent/config.toml',
   175→    }))
   176→    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
   177→      execCommand: () => '',
   178→    }))
   179→
   180→    const mod = await import('../../src/cmd-daemon.js')
   181→    mod.cmdInstall({ interval: '10m', autoPush: 'false' })
   182→
   183→    expect(savedConfigs.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)
   184→    const saved = savedConfigs[0] as Record<string, unknown>
   185→    expect(saved['heartbeat_interval']).toBe('10m')
   186→    expect(saved['auto_push']).toBe(false)
   187→  })
   188→})
   189→
   190→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   191→// cmdUninstall — lightweight test
   192→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   193→
   194→describe('cmdUninstall', () => {
   195→  let stderrChunks: string[]
   196→  let stdoutChunks: string[]
   197→  const originalStderrWrite = process.stderr.write
   198→  const originalStdoutWrite = process.stdout.write
   199→
   200→  afterEach(() => {
   201→    process.stderr.write = originalStderrWrite
   202→    process.stdout.write = originalStdoutWrite
   203→    vi.restoreAllMocks()
   204→  })
   205→
   206→  it('returns 0 or 1 depending on platform', () => {
   207→    stderrChunks = []
   208→    stdoutChunks = []
   209→    process.stderr.write = ((chunk: string) => {
   210→      stderrChunks.push(chunk)
   211→      return true
   212→    }) as typeof process.stderr.write
   213→    process.stdout.write = ((chunk: string) => {
   214→      stdoutChunks.push(chunk)
   215→      return true
   216→    }) as typeof process.stdout.write
   217→
   218→    const result = cmdUninstall()
   219→    // On any supported platform it returns 0; unsupported returns 1
   220→    expect([0, 1]).toContain(result)
   221→  })
   222→})
   223→

Read output=1→/** 2→ * CLI: memorytree recall — on-demand transcript sync + session lookup. 3→ * Port of scripts/recall-session.py CLI wrapper. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { existsSync } from 'node:f...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * CLI: memorytree recall — on-demand transcript sync + session lookup.
     3→ * Port of scripts/recall-session.py CLI wrapper.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { resolve } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→import { recall, formatRecallText } from '@openmnemo/core'
    10→
    11→export interface RecallOptions {
    12→  root: string
    13→  projectName: string
    14→  globalRoot: string
    15→  activationTime: string
    16→  format: string
    17→}
    18→
    19→export async function cmdRecall(options: RecallOptions): Promise<number> {
    20→  const root = resolve(options.root)
    21→  if (!existsSync(root)) {
    22→    process.stderr.write(`root does not exist: ${root}\n`)
    23→    return 1
    24→  }
    25→
    26→  const result = await recall(root, options.projectName, options.globalRoot, options.activationTime)
    27→
    28→  if (options.format === 'json') {
    29→    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(result) + '\n')
    30→  } else {
    31→    process.stdout.write(formatRecallText(result) + '\n')
    32→  }
    33→  return 0
    34→}
    35→
Tool Result #1228 Read toolu_01PGjE7exbbjiDCxSjgwL5gW 00:46:27
Read output=1→/** 2→ * CLI adapters for `memorytree report build` and `memorytree report serve`. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import { createServer } from 'node:http' 6→import { createReadStream, existsSync,...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * CLI adapters for `memorytree report build` and `memorytree report serve`.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { createServer } from 'node:http'
     6→import { createReadStream, existsSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { extname, join, resolve, sep } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// Build command
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→export interface CmdReportBuildOptions {
    14→  root: string
    15→  output: string
    16→  noAi: boolean
    17→  model: string
    18→  locale?: string
    19→  reportBaseUrl?: string
    20→}
    21→
    22→export async function cmdReportBuild(opts: CmdReportBuildOptions): Promise<number> {
    23→  const { buildReport } = await import('@openmnemo/report')
    24→  try {
    25→    const buildOptions = {
    26→      root: resolve(opts.root),
    27→      output: resolve(opts.output),
    28→      noAi: opts.noAi,
    29→      model: opts.model,
    30→      ...(opts.locale ? { locale: opts.locale } : {}),
    31→      ...(opts.reportBaseUrl ? { reportBaseUrl: opts.reportBaseUrl } : {}),
    32→    }
    33→    await buildReport(buildOptions)
    34→    console.log(`Report generated at: ${resolve(opts.output)}`)
    35→    console.log(`Open: ${join(resolve(opts.output), 'index.html')}`)
    36→    return 0
    37→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    38→    console.error(`Report build failed: ${String(err)}`)
    39→    return 1
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→
    43→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44→// Serve command
    45→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46→
    47→export interface CmdReportServeOptions {
    48→  dir: string
    49→  port: number
    50→}
    51→
    52→export function cmdReportServe(opts: CmdReportServeOptions): number {
    53→  const root = resolve(opts.dir)
    54→  const port = opts.port
    55→
    56→  const MIME: Record<string, string> = {
    57→    '.html': 'text/html; charset=utf-8',
    58→    '.css': 'text/css',
    59→    '.js': 'application/javascript',
    60→    '.json': 'application/json',
    61→    '.svg': 'image/svg+xml',
    62→    '.ico': 'image/x-icon',
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  const server = createServer((req, res) => {
    66→    let urlPath: string
    67→    try {
    68→      urlPath = decodeURIComponent((req.url ?? '/').split('?')[0] ?? '/')
    69→    } catch {
    70→      res.writeHead(400, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    71→      res.end('Bad request')
    72→      return
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    let filePath = join(root, urlPath)
    76→
    77→    // Containment check: reject paths that escape the serve root
    78→    // Use sep (\ on Windows, / on Unix) so the check works cross-platform.
    79→    if (!filePath.startsWith(root + sep) && filePath !== root) {
    80→      res.writeHead(403, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    81→      res.end('Forbidden')
    82→      return
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    // Serve index.html for directory requests
    86→    if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) {
    87→      filePath = join(filePath, 'index.html')
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
    91→      res.writeHead(404, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    92→      res.end('Not found')
    93→      return
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    const ext = extname(filePath)
    97→    const contentType = MIME[ext] ?? 'application/octet-stream'
    98→    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType })
    99→    const stream = createReadStream(filePath)
   100→    stream.on('error', () => { res.destroy() })
   101→    stream.pipe(res)
   102→  })
   103→
   104→  server.on('error', (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   105→    if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   106→      console.error(`Error: port ${port} is already in use.`)
   107→      process.exit(1)
   108→    }
   109→    console.error(`Server error: ${String(err)}`)
   110→    process.exit(1)
   111→  })
   112→
   113→  server.listen(port, () => {
   114→    console.log(`Serving ${root}`)
   115→    console.log(`Open: http://localhost:${port}/`)
   116→    console.log('Press Ctrl+C to stop.')
   117→  })
   118→
   119→  return 0
   120→}
   121→

Write input={"content":"/**\n * CLI tests for cmd-import.ts\n */\n\nimport { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } f...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\cli\\tests\\cli\\import.test.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * CLI tests for cmd-import.ts\n */\n\nimport { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\n\nimport { cmdImport } from '../../src/cmd-import.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction captureOutput(): { out: () => string; err: () => string; restore: () => void } {\n  const outChunks: string[] = []\n  const errChunks: string[] = []\n  const origOut = process.stdout.write\n  const origErr = process.stderr.write\n  process.stdout.write = ((c: string) => { outChunks.push(c); return true }) as typeof process.stdout.write\n  process.stderr.write = ((c: string) => { errChunks.push(c); return true }) as typeof process.stderr.write\n  return {\n    out: () => outChunks.join(''),\n    err: () => errChunks.join(''),\n    restore: () => { process.stdout.write = origOut; process.stderr.write = origErr },\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('cmdImport', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cmd-import-test-'))\n  })\n  afterEach(() => {\n    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n    vi.restoreAllMocks()\n    vi.resetModules()\n  })\n\n  it('returns 1 when root does not exist', async () => {\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdImport({\n        root: join(tmpDir, 'nonexistent'),\n        source: join(tmpDir, 'file.jsonl'),\n        client: 'auto',\n        projectName: '',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        rawUploadPermission: 'not-set',\n        format: 'text',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(1)\n      expect(cap.err()).toContain('does not exist')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns 1 when source file does not exist', async () => {\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdImport({\n        root: tmpDir,\n        source: join(tmpDir, 'missing.jsonl'),\n        client: 'auto',\n        projectName: '',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        rawUploadPermission: 'not-set',\n        format: 'text',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(1)\n      expect(cap.err()).toContain('does not exist')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns 0 and prints manifest fields for a valid Codex transcript', async () => {\n    // Create minimal Codex JSONL\n    const source = join(tmpDir, 'rollout-session.jsonl')\n    writeFileSync(source, [\n      JSON.stringify({ type: 'session_meta', payload: { id: 'sess-x', title: 'Test', cwd: tmpDir, git: { branch: 'main' } } }),\n      JSON.stringify({ type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'input_text', text: 'Hello' }] } }),\n      JSON.stringify({ type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-01-01T00:00:01Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Hi' }] } }),\n    ].join('\\n') + '\\n')\n\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdImport({\n        root: tmpDir,\n        source,\n        client: 'codex',\n        projectName: 'myproject',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        rawUploadPermission: 'not-set',\n        format: 'text',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(0)\n      const out = cap.out()\n      expect(out).toContain('client: codex')\n      expect(out).toContain('session_id: sess-x')\n      expect(out).toContain('message_count: 2')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns JSON output when format=json', async () => {\n    const source = join(tmpDir, 'rollout-json.jsonl')\n    writeFileSync(source, [\n      JSON.stringify({ type: 'session_meta', payload: { id: 'sess-json', title: 'JSON test' } }),\n      JSON.stringify({ type: 'response_item', timestamp: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z', payload: { type: 'message', role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'input_text', text: 'test' }] } }),\n    ].join('\\n') + '\\n')\n\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdImport({\n        root: tmpDir,\n        source,\n        client: 'codex',\n        projectName: '',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        rawUploadPermission: 'not-set',\n        format: 'json',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(0)\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(cap.out())\n      expect(parsed.client).toBe('codex')\n      expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('sess-json')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n})\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\cli\\tests\\cli\\discover.test.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * CLI tests for cmd-discover.ts\n */\n\nimport { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\n\nimport { cmdDiscover } from '../../src/cmd-discover.js'\n\nfunction captureOutput(): { out: () => string; err: () => string; restore: () => void } {\n  const outChunks: string[] = []\n  const errChunks: string[] = []\n  const origOut = process.stdout.write\n  const origErr = process.stderr.write\n  process.stdout.write = ((c: string) => { outChunks.push(c); return true }) as typeof process.stdout.write\n  process.stderr.write = ((c: string) => { errChunks.push(c); return true }) as typeof process.stderr.write\n  return {\n    out: () => outChunks.join(''),\n    err: () => errChunks.join(''),\n    restore: () => { process.stdout.write = origOut; process.stderr.write = origErr },\n  }\n}\n\ndescribe('cmdDiscover', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cmd-discover-test-'))\n    vi.resetModules()\n  })\n  afterEach(() => {\n    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n    vi.restoreAllMocks()\n    vi.resetModules()\n  })\n\n  it('returns 1 when root does not exist', async () => {\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdDiscover({\n        root: join(tmpDir, 'no-such-dir'),\n        client: 'all',\n        scope: 'all-projects',\n        projectName: '',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        rawUploadPermission: 'not-set',\n        limit: 0,\n        format: 'text',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(1)\n      expect(cap.err()).toContain('does not exist')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns 0 with zero discovered when client dirs are empty', async () => {\n    // Mock discoverSourceFiles to return no sources\n    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/core', async (importOriginal) => {\n      const original = await importOriginal<typeof import('@openmnemo/core')>()\n      return {\n        ...original,\n        discoverSourceFiles: () => [],\n      }\n    })\n\n    const { cmdDiscover: freshDiscover } = await import('../../src/cmd-discover.js')\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await freshDiscover({\n        root: tmpDir,\n        client: 'all',\n        scope: 'all-projects',\n        projectName: '',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        rawUploadPermission: 'not-set',\n        limit: 0,\n        format: 'text',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(0)\n      const out = cap.out()\n      expect(out).toContain('discovered_count: 0')\n      expect(out).toContain('imported_count: 0')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns JSON when format=json', async () => {\n    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/core', async (importOriginal) => {\n      const original = await importOriginal<typeof import('@openmnemo/core')>()\n      return {\n        ...original,\n        discoverSourceFiles: () => [],\n      }\n    })\n\n    const { cmdDiscover: freshDiscover } = await import('../../src/cmd-discover.js')\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await freshDiscover({\n        root: tmpDir,\n        client: 'claude',\n        scope: 'current-project',\n        projectName: 'myproj',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        rawUploadPermission: 'not-set',\n        limit: 0,\n        format: 'json',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(0)\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(cap.out())\n      expect(parsed.client_filter).toBe('claude')\n      expect(parsed.scope).toBe('current-project')\n      expect(parsed.imported_count).toBe(0)\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n})\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\cli\\tests\\cli\\recall.test.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * CLI tests for cmd-recall.ts\n */\n\nimport { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\n\nfunction captureOutput(): { out: () => string; err: () => string; restore: () => void } {\n  const outChunks: string[] = []\n  const errChunks: string[] = []\n  const origOut = process.stdout.write\n  const origErr = process.stderr.write\n  process.stdout.write = ((c: string) => { outChunks.push(c); return true }) as typeof process.stdout.write\n  process.stderr.write = ((c: string) => { errChunks.push(c); return true }) as typeof process.stderr.write\n  return {\n    out: () => outChunks.join(''),\n    err: () => errChunks.join(''),\n    restore: () => { process.stdout.write = origOut; process.stderr.write = origErr },\n  }\n}\n\nconst MOCK_RECALL_RESULT = {\n  session_id: 'sess-recall-001',\n  title: 'My Session',\n  client: 'claude',\n  started_at: '2024-06-01T10:00:00Z',\n  cwd: '/home/user/project',\n  branch: 'main',\n  message_count: 5,\n  tool_event_count: 2,\n  clean_path: '/global/clean/file.md',\n  summary: 'Session summary text',\n}\n\ndescribe('cmdRecall', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cmd-recall-test-'))\n    vi.resetModules()\n  })\n  afterEach(() => {\n    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n    vi.restoreAllMocks()\n    vi.resetModules()\n  })\n\n  it('returns 1 when root does not exist', async () => {\n    const { cmdRecall } = await import('../../src/cmd-recall.js')\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdRecall({\n        root: join(tmpDir, 'no-such-dir'),\n        projectName: '',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        activationTime: '',\n        format: 'text',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(1)\n      expect(cap.err()).toContain('does not exist')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns 0 and outputs recall result in text format', async () => {\n    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/core', async (importOriginal) => {\n      const original = await importOriginal<typeof import('@openmnemo/core')>()\n      return {\n        ...original,\n        recall: async () => MOCK_RECALL_RESULT,\n        formatRecallText: (r: typeof MOCK_RECALL_RESULT) =>\n          `session_id: ${r.session_id}\\ntitle: ${r.title}\\nclient: ${r.client}`,\n      }\n    })\n\n    const { cmdRecall } = await import('../../src/cmd-recall.js')\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdRecall({\n        root: tmpDir,\n        projectName: 'myproject',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        activationTime: '',\n        format: 'text',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(0)\n      const out = cap.out()\n      expect(out).toContain('session_id: sess-recall-001')\n      expect(out).toContain('title: My Session')\n      expect(out).toContain('client: claude')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns 0 and outputs JSON when format=json', async () => {\n    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/core', async (importOriginal) => {\n      const original = await importOriginal<typeof import('@openmnemo/core')>()\n      return {\n        ...original,\n        recall: async () => MOCK_RECALL_RESULT,\n        formatRecallText: () => '',\n      }\n    })\n\n    const { cmdRecall } = await import('../../src/cmd-recall.js')\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdRecall({\n        root: tmpDir,\n        projectName: '',\n        globalRoot: tmpDir,\n        activationTime: '2024-06-01T09:00:00Z',\n        format: 'json',\n      })\n      expect(code).toBe(0)\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(cap.out())\n      expect(parsed.session_id).toBe('sess-recall-001')\n      expect(parsed.client).toBe('claude')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n})\n"
}

Write input={"content":"/**\n * CLI tests for cmd-report.ts\n */\n\nimport { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync,...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\cli\\tests\\cli\\report.test.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * CLI tests for cmd-report.ts\n */\n\nimport { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'\nimport { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { tmpdir } from 'node:os'\nimport * as http from 'node:http'\n\nimport { cmdReportServe, type CmdReportBuildOptions } from '../../src/cmd-report.js'\n\nfunction captureOutput(): { out: () => string; err: () => string; restore: () => void } {\n  const outChunks: string[] = []\n  const errChunks: string[] = []\n  const origOut = process.stdout.write\n  const origErr = process.stderr.write\n  // Also capture console.log / console.error used in cmd-report\n  const origLog = console.log\n  const origConsoleErr = console.error\n  process.stdout.write = ((c: string) => { outChunks.push(c); return true }) as typeof process.stdout.write\n  process.stderr.write = ((c: string) => { errChunks.push(c); return true }) as typeof process.stderr.write\n  console.log = (...args: unknown[]) => { outChunks.push(args.join(' ') + '\\n') }\n  console.error = (...args: unknown[]) => { errChunks.push(args.join(' ') + '\\n') }\n  return {\n    out: () => outChunks.join(''),\n    err: () => errChunks.join(''),\n    restore: () => {\n      process.stdout.write = origOut\n      process.stderr.write = origErr\n      console.log = origLog\n      console.error = origConsoleErr\n    },\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// cmdReportBuild (with mocked @openmnemo/report)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('cmdReportBuild', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cmd-report-test-'))\n    vi.resetModules()\n  })\n  afterEach(() => {\n    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n    vi.restoreAllMocks()\n    vi.resetModules()\n  })\n\n  it('returns 0 when buildReport succeeds', async () => {\n    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/report', () => ({\n      buildReport: async () => undefined,\n    }))\n\n    const { cmdReportBuild } = await import('../../src/cmd-report.js')\n    const opts: CmdReportBuildOptions = {\n      root: tmpDir,\n      output: join(tmpDir, 'out'),\n      noAi: true,\n      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n    }\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdReportBuild(opts)\n      expect(code).toBe(0)\n      expect(cap.out()).toContain('Report generated at:')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('returns 1 when buildReport throws', async () => {\n    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/report', () => ({\n      buildReport: async () => { throw new Error('build failed') },\n    }))\n\n    const { cmdReportBuild } = await import('../../src/cmd-report.js')\n    const opts: CmdReportBuildOptions = {\n      root: tmpDir,\n      output: join(tmpDir, 'out'),\n      noAi: true,\n      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n    }\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      const code = await cmdReportBuild(opts)\n      expect(code).toBe(1)\n      expect(cap.err()).toContain('build failed')\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('passes locale and reportBaseUrl when provided', async () => {\n    const receivedOptions: unknown[] = []\n    vi.doMock('@openmnemo/report', () => ({\n      buildReport: async (o: unknown) => { receivedOptions.push(o) },\n    }))\n\n    const { cmdReportBuild } = await import('../../src/cmd-report.js')\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      await cmdReportBuild({\n        root: tmpDir,\n        output: join(tmpDir, 'out'),\n        noAi: true,\n        model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',\n        locale: 'zh-CN',\n        reportBaseUrl: 'https://example.com',\n      })\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n\n    expect(receivedOptions).toHaveLength(1)\n    const opts = receivedOptions[0] as Record<string, unknown>\n    expect(opts['locale']).toBe('zh-CN')\n    expect(opts['reportBaseUrl']).toBe('https://example.com')\n    expect(opts['noAi']).toBe(true)\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// cmdReportServe\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('cmdReportServe', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n  let server: http.Server | null = null\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cmd-serve-test-'))\n  })\n  afterEach(async () => {\n    if (server) {\n      await new Promise<void>(resolve => server!.close(() => resolve()))\n      server = null\n    }\n    rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })\n  })\n\n  it('returns 0 synchronously (server starts in background)', () => {\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    let code: number\n    try {\n      code = cmdReportServe({ dir: tmpDir, port: 0 })\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n    expect(code!).toBe(0)\n\n    // Clean up the server that was started\n    // Access the server created by the command via the http module's active handles\n    // We just verify the function returns 0 without throwing\n  })\n\n  it('serves an HTML file and returns 200', async () => {\n    // Create a minimal index.html\n    writeFileSync(join(tmpDir, 'index.html'), '<html><body>Hello</body></html>')\n\n    const port = 19871  // Use a specific port for this test\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      cmdReportServe({ dir: tmpDir, port })\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n\n    // Wait briefly for the server to start\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100))\n\n    const body = await new Promise<string>((resolve, reject) => {\n      const req = http.get(`http://localhost:${port}/index.html`, (res) => {\n        let data = ''\n        res.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => { data += chunk.toString() })\n        res.on('end', () => resolve(data))\n      })\n      req.on('error', reject)\n    })\n    expect(body).toContain('Hello')\n\n    // Close the server\n    await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {\n      // Get the server by re-requesting info — but we don't have a handle.\n      // Use a close via the http.Server internal mechanism.\n      // Actually we cannot easily close it without the reference.\n      // Use a workaround: connect and check, then let it be cleaned up by the test runner.\n      resolve()\n    })\n  })\n\n  it('returns 404 for missing file', async () => {\n    const port = 19872\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      cmdReportServe({ dir: tmpDir, port })\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100))\n\n    const statusCode = await new Promise<number>((resolve, reject) => {\n      const req = http.get(`http://localhost:${port}/no-such-file.html`, (res) => {\n        resolve(res.statusCode ?? 0)\n        res.resume()\n      })\n      req.on('error', reject)\n    })\n    expect(statusCode).toBe(404)\n  })\n\n  it('returns 403 for path traversal attempt', async () => {\n    const port = 19873\n    const cap = captureOutput()\n    try {\n      cmdReportServe({ dir: tmpDir, port })\n    } finally {\n      cap.restore()\n    }\n\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100))\n\n    const statusCode = await new Promise<number>((resolve, reject) => {\n      const req = http.get(`http://localhost:${port}/../../../etc/passwd`, (res) => {\n        resolve(res.statusCode ?? 0)\n        res.resume()\n      })\n      req.on('error', reject)\n    })\n    expect(statusCode).toBe(403)\n  })\n})\n"
}
